inscription.head:
No. 129.
(A.R. No. 129 of 1907.)
Koḍumbāḷūr, Kulattur Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District
Mūvarkōvil—on the south wall of the central shrine
inscription.p:
This is a Sanskrit inscription in early Grantha characters. It gives the
genealogy of the family of Koḍumbāḷur Chiefs. The first line is mutilated and hence
the name of the original ancestor is not known. He is said to have captured an
elephant battalion evidently from some enemy. In his family was born Paravīrajit
Vīratuṅga; his son was Atīvīra, the unrivalled (Anupama); his son was Saṅghakṛit to
whom was born Nṛipakēsari; his son was Paradurgamarddana, the glorious
conqueror of Vātāpi. To him was born Samarābhirāma who killed Chaḷukki in a
battle at Adhirājamaṅgala. He married a princess named Anupamā the daughter
of the Chōḷa king. His son by this princess was Bhūti who by his prowess in battle
obtained the title Vikramakēsari. He is stated to have made the waters of the Kāvēri
red with the blood of the Pallava army slain by him. He also conquered Vīra-
Pāṇḍya in battle and destroyed one Vañchi-Vēḷ. It is stated that he was living at
Koḍumbāḷūr with his two wives Kaṟṟaḷi and Varaguṇā, by the former of whom he
had two sons Parāntakavarman and Ādityavarman. This Bhūti Vikramakēsari built
three shrines in the name of himself and his two consorts for god Mahēśvara at
Koḍumbāḷūr, and presented a maṭha to Mallikārjuna of Madura, a teacher of the
Kāḷāmukha sect of Śaivas and eleven villages for feeding 50 ascetics of that sect (every
day).

Since this chief Bhūti Vikramakēsari is known from other stone records to be
identical with Teṉṉavaṉ Iḷaṅgōvēḷ a feudatory of Chōḷa Āditya I, it is possible that the
destruction of the Pallava army claimed by him was in connection with his liege-lord
Āditya's overthrow of Pallava Aparājita in battle and his annexation of the latter's
territory sometime before A.D. 890. In this case Vīra-Pāṇḍya over whom also,
Bhūti claims a victory cannot be equated with his namesake who was killed in fight
by Āditya II Karikāla (vide An. Rept. for 1908, Part II, para 88), because that event
took place more than 70 years later. He should have been a contemporary of
Parāntaka Vīra-Nārāyaṇa and probably belonging to a collateral Pāṇḍya line as
surmised by Mr. K.V.S. Aiyar (Q.J.M.S. Vol. XLIII, Nos. 3 and 4).

l.gr: vīratuṅgō mutō jā[t]ōsmā datipira ityanupama stasmādabhūt saṅ-
ghakṛt tsmāccrīnṛpakēsarī vivadṛdhē yō bāla ēvōrag[ai]
statsūnuḥ paradurggamarddana iti khyātassavātāvijit u tasya samarābhirā-
ḷukkim tasyācyuta(m)sya kamalēva sarasvatīva patmōtbhavasya girijē [va]
hitā yaduvaṅśakētōḥu tasyā masya babhūva bhūtira parāmmīnāmaḷākhyā-
ndadhan śramānvikramakēsarīti samarē labdhānyanāmā nṛpaḥ u kāvērī-
vīrapāṇḍyaṇ vyajayata samarē vañcivēḷantakō bhūt u mattāri sāmajā-
natā kēśēṣu kārṣṇya ntanau tanvinā ntanutā bhavatsta nayugē cānnyōnya-
svanāmnā priyayō rnnāmnā sōdāt bṛhanmaṭham . tasmai kālamukhā-
khyā na yati mukhyāya yādavaḥ prādādēkādaśagrāma vinibandham bṛhanmaṭhamu
pañcāśatāma sitaktra tapōdha nānām bhuttyai brahanmaṭha••• [gu] ra-
vē sa rājā naivēdya gandhakusumākṣata dhūpadīpa tāmbūla•••

inscription.head:
No. 130.
(A.R. No. 130 of 1907.)
Koḍumbāḷūr, Kulattur Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District
Mūvarkōvil—on the north wall of the central shrine
inscription.p:
This is a mutilated inscription of Rājēndra-Chōḷa I with only a part of his
historical introduction in good preservation, the rest being lost.

edition.l: [svastiśrī] śrī [||*] tirumaṉṉi vaḷara iruni-

inscription.head:
No. 131.
(A.R. No. 131 of 1907.)
Koḍumbāḷūr, Kulattur Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District
Muchukundēśvara temple—on the south wall of the maṇḍapa in front
inscription.p:
This is an inscription of Jaṭāvarman Vīra Pāṇḍyadēva dated in his 17th year,
Siṁha, śu. 10, Thursday, Mūlam, corresponding to A.D. 1269, August 10. He seems
to be the same as the ruler whose accession took place in A.D. 1253. The record
begins with a short introduction mentioning his victories over Koṅgaṇam (Koṅgu),
Vaḍugu (Vaḍugar), Gaṅgai-nāḍu (?) and the Kāvēri and his coronation of heroes
and victors at Puliyūr (Chidambaram). It registers the sale of five pieces of land by
the Kaikkōḷas and Kaikkōḷa-mudalis of Koḍumbāḷūr to the temple of Tirumudu-
kuṉṟamuḍaiya-Nāyanār of the same village, in lieu of certain sums of money which
seem to have been borrowed from the temple by them sometime previously and by
their forebears (?). Owing to the damaged state of the record the sense of the
inscription in its details is not clear. Mention is made of Poṉ, Paḻan-Śōḻiyan-kāśu and
Vīra-Pāṇḍyan-kāśu of which the values seem to have stood in the ratio 110: 670:
1050. Again 150 Paṇam is said to answer to 10505 kāśu and 110 kaḻañju to 12505
kāśu in which the paṇam and kāśu are general terms to be understood specifically
according to the prevalent usage.

Koḍumbāḷūr is said to be situated in Uṟattūr-kūṟṟam a division of Koṅāḍu
otherwise called Kaḍalaḍaiyād-ilangai-koṇḍa-Śōḻa-vaḷanāḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 132.
(A.R. No. 132 of 1907.)
Koḍumbāḷūr, Kulattur Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District
Muchukundēśvara temple—on the same wall
Jaṭ. Sundara-Pāṇḍya
inscription.p:
This belongs to Jaṭāvarman Sundara-Pāṇḍyadēva. This is badly damaged. It
seems to register a sale of land to the temple. With this are found two other pieces of
an inscription which may not however form part of this record. One of them mentions
a certain Pichchaṉ Toṇḍaṉ Vikrama-Pāṇḍya-paṉmaṉ the donor of some land to the
temple of Tirumudukuṉṟam-uḍaiya-Nāyaṉār. He figures in No. 136 below of
Māṟavarman Kulaśēkhara I.

inscription.head:
No. 133.
(A.R. No. 133 of 1907.)
Koḍumbāḷūr, Kulattur Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District
Muchukundēśvara temple—on the north wall of the same maṇḍapa
inscription.p:
This is a damaged and incomplete inscription of the [14]th year of Māṟavarman
Sundara-Pāṇḍya (I) ‘who was pleased to present the chōḷa Country’. The details
of the date are given as Mithuna, śu. 2, Sunday, Pushya which would give the equivalent
A.D. 1229, May 27. It registers a gift of land called Māchchāttan-vayal by one Āḻvāṉ
Tirukkoḍuṅ kuṉṟamuḍaiyāṉ alias Kulaśēkhara-Brahmārāyaṉ a Śivabrāhmaṇa of the
temple of Tiruvattīśura-Nāyanār at Kāraiyūr in Śōḻa-Pāṇḍya-vaḷanāḍu to provide for
the renewal of worship and offerings and for burning lamps during the ardhayāma
service in the temple of Tirumudukuṉṟam-Uḍaiya-Nāyanār at Koḍumbāḷūr which
had been discontinued. Reference is made to a temple of Māśāttīch churam uḍaīya-
Nāyaṉār.

edition.l: svasti śrī [||*] śrīkomāṟapaṉmarāṉa tribhuvavaṉaccakkaravatti-

edition.p: The following 13 lines seem to be part of a different record.

inscription.head:
No. 134.
(A.R. No. 134 of 1907.)
Koḍumbāḷūr, Kulattur Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District
Muchukundēśvara temple—on the same wall
inscription.p:
This seems to be an unusual record-in faulty style-purporting to be an
inscription dated in the 7th year of a Koḍumbāḷūr chief Vīraśōḻa-Irukkuvēḷ with
the Chōḷa title Parakēsarivarman prefixed to his name. It states that on the
representation (made to the chief) by Aḻagaṉ Vīraśōḻa-Aṇukkamāl of the uḍankūṭṭam
(?), the kaṇmāḷar and some others (not clear), an image of the diety called
Akkaśāliśvaram-Uḍai[yār] was consecrated in the temple of Tirumudugaṟam at
Koḍumbāḷūr, and provision was made for its daily worship and offerings by means of a
gift of a land known as Śēral-ēndal as dēvadāna.

edition.l:

panmāke

inscription.head:
No. 135.
(A.R. No. 135 of 1907.)
Koḍumbāḷūr, Kulattur Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District
Muchukundēśvara temple—on the north wall of the kitchen
inscription.p:
This is a record of the 5th year of Māṟavarman Kulaśēkhara, which states that
this kitchen called the Vijayapañjaraṉ-tirumaḍaippaḷḷi is the gift of the Nāṭṭavar of
Muduśolkuḍi-nāḍu. Tirumudukuṉṟam is said to be situated in Vaḍa-Kōnāḍu a
division of Uṟattūr-Kūṟṟam in Kōnāḍu.

edition.l:
svastiśrī [||*] komāṟapaṉmarāṉa tirupuvaṉacakkaravattikaḷ śrī kulacekara
tevaṟku
yāṇṭu 5- vatu koṉāṭāṉa kaṭalaṭaiyāta ilaṅkaiko[ṇ*]ṭa co-

inscription.head:
No. 136.
(A.R. No. 136 of 1907.)
Koḍumbāḷūr, Kulattur Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District
Muchukundēśvara temple—on the same wall
Māṟ. Kulaśēkhara I: year 42: 1309-10 A.D.
inscription.p:
This registers the perpetual lease of the tenancy right over a land known as
Iluppaikkuḍi, given by the temple authorities (sthānattār) of Tirumudukuṉṟam-uḍaiya-
Nāyaṉār to one Dēvaṉ Tirunelvēli-Uḍaiyār. The land which is said to have been
endowed to the temple, by a kaikkōḷa of Koḍumbāḷūr by name Vīraṉ Toṇḍaṉ alias
Vikkira[ma*]-Pāṇḍyapanmar, for the conduct of the ardhayāma service, had been
left fallow for a long time following a breach and destruction of the tank-bund and
consequent spread of wild growth. The lessee now paid 100 paṇam to the temple as
parivaṭṭamudal (price of temple honour ?) and undertook to repair the breach, and
after clearing the wilderness, to bring the land under cultivation and to measure out
7 kalam, 1 tūṇi and 1 padakku of paddy on every mā of land, whether cultivated with
paddy or some other products like turmeric, ginger, betel or sugarcane.

edition.l:
svastiśrī [||*] komāṟapaṉmarāṉa tirupuvaṉacca[k*]karavattikaḷ kulacekara
tevaṟku [yāṇṭu] 42- vatu koṉāṭāṉa

l.ta:
kaṭalaṭaiyāta ilaṅkaikoṇṭa coḻavaḷanāṭṭu uṟattūrkūṟṟattu
koṭumpāḷū[r*] uṭaiyā[r*] tirumutukun-

ṟamuṭaiya nāyanā[r*] koyil tāṉattārom taḷakkā ūrāḷikaḷil

paṇa[m] nūṟu ikkuṭikāṭṭukku varicai kaṭṭiṉapaṭi payir nilattukku māt
tāl varicai nellu eḻukalane tūṇippatakkum

ṭuttom tirunelveli uṭaiyāṟku tānattārom ippaṭikku tānat-

tevaṉ ciṅkapperumāḷ eḻuttu .

inscription.head:
No. 137.
(A.R. No. 137 of 1907.)
Koḍumbāḷūr, Kulattur Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District
Muchukundēśvara temple—on a pillar in the same maṇḍapa
inscription.p:
This pillar is stated to be the gift of one Śeṭṭi Kambaṉ alias Jaṉanātha Aṇukkap-
Pallavaraiyaṉ. Another inscription evidently engraved on a different pillar gives the
name of the donor as Maṅgan Tāmattaṉ alias Kolōttuṅgaśōḻa•••••••• Both the donors
were Kaikkoḷa residents of the place.

inscription.head:
No. 138.
(A.R. No. 138 of 1907.)
Koḍumbāḷūr, Kulāttur Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District
Muchukundēśvara temple—on another pillar in the same place
inscription.p:
This is dated in the 5th year of some king whose name is not given. Perhaps it
was Vīra-Pāṇḍya. Mention is made of Rishabhapperumāṉaḍigaḷ (Nandi) in the
Tiruppūdīśvaram temple.

edition.l: śrī yāṇṭu 5-

inscription.head:
No. 139.
(A.R. No. 139 of 1907.)
Koḍumbāḷūr, Kulattur Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District
Muchukundēśvara temple—on the base of the same maṇḍapa
inscription.p:
This is a mere fragment of an inscription mentioning Uḍaiyār Madhurāṉtakan
Śundaraśōḻaṉ. Reference is made to a woman servant of the royal palace by name
Kaḷḷichchi Uttama cha•••••••• and one Pūdi Paṭṭālakaṉ Nakka••••• and also to two
classes of smiths (Kottar) of Koḍumbāḷūr.

inscription.head:
No. 140.
(A.R. No. 140 of 1907.)
Koḍumbāḷūr, Kulattur Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District
Muchukundēśvara temple—on three stones built into the pond in front of the temple
inscription.p:
These are fragments of some Kannaḍa inscriptions in early characters. One
of them refers to the reign of Madurekōṇḍa Kōpa•••••••• and to a case of self immola-
tion by an individual by means of khaṇḍasphuṭita (mutilation of flesh ?). The second
piece mentions a tank built for (?) one•••• Kēsarīśvaradēva, and the third refers to
Vīra-Pāṇḍya-Iḷaṅgōvēḷār ruling at Koḍumbāḷūr, Vikrama•••••••• dēva and to
one Māramaseṭṭi.

inscription.head:
No. 141.
(A.R. No. 141 of 1907.)
Koḍumbāḷūr, Kulattur Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District
Muchukundēśvara temple—on another stone built into the same pond
inscription.p:
This is a much damaged and fragmentary inscription in Grantha which should
have formed part of a genealogy. Atri is mentioned.

inscription.head:
No. 142.
(A.R. No. 142 of 1907.)
Pūvālaikkuḍi, Tirumayam Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District
At the entrance into the rock-cut cave
inscription.p:
This is a small inscription in characters of about the 9th century A.D. which
states that this (cave) is the gift of one Pūdi Kaḷari alias Amarūṉṟi-Muttaraiyaṉ.

l.ta: śrī pūti kaḷari āyiṉa amaruṉṟimuttaraiyaṉ••• taṉ[ma]-

inscription.head:
No. 143.
(A.R. No. 143 of 1907.)
Pūvālaikkuḍi, Tirumayam Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District
Rock-cut cave—At the same entrance, left side
Rājādhirāja I: year 27: 1044-45 A.D.
inscription.p:
This inscription begins with introduction Tiṅgaḷērtaru etc. The king is also
known from several inscriptions as Vijaya-Rājēndradēva and claims to have destroyed
the city of Kalyāṇapura and performed the anointment of heroes under that name. An
image of a dvārapālaka found set up in the Airāvatēśvara temple at Dārāsuram (Tanjore
district) bears an inscription to the effect that it was brought by Vijayarājēndra after
the conquest of Kalyāṇapuram (An. Rept. 1908, Part II, para 56).

The present record registers a gift of 25 cows for burning a perpetual lamp
with ghee in the temple of Mahādēva at Pūvālaikkuḍi in Kūḍalūr-nāḍu a division
of Kāna-nāḍu in Kēraḷāntaka-vaḷanāḍu, by a resident of the place by name
Iḷamagaṉ Attāṇi Nāvaṟkuḍaiyāṉ alias Jayaṅkoṇḍaśōḻap-pēraiyaṉ for the merit
of his younger sister Ariyanaṅgai. The donor seems to have been a member of
a merchant guild known as Diśai-Āyirattu-Nāṉādēśi.

edition.l: svasti śrī [||*] tiṅkaḷḷe[r*] taru

inscription.head:
No. 144.
(A.R. No. 144 of 1907.)
Pūvālaikkuḍi, Tirumayam Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District
Rock-cut cave—on the south wall of the maṇḍapa in front
Māṟ. Kulaśēkhara I: year 18: 1285-86 A.D.
inscription.p:
This is a mutilated record of Māṟavarman Kulaśēkhara “who took every
country”. It registers an endowment of land for providing for offerings to the deity
during the early morning service in the temple, by three residents of Sundaraśōḻapuram
in Ollaiyūr-kūṟṟam a division of Teṉ-Kōṉāḍu in Kaḍalaḍaiyād-ilaṅgaikoṇḍa-Śōḻa
vaḷanāḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 145.
(A.R. No. 145 of 1907.)
Pūvālaikkuḍi, Tirumayam Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District
Rock-cut cave—on the same wall
Par. Rājēndradēva: year 5: 1055-56 A.D.
inscription.p:
This begins with the introduction Tirumagaḷ maruviya etc. It is a mutilated
record registering a gift of cows for a perpetual lamp in the temple of Mahādēva at
Pūvālaikkuḍi by Iḷamagaṉ Attāṇi Nāvaṟku[ḍaiyan]••••• Śoḻa-Tennavadiyaraiyaṉ of
Koṟṟamaṅgalam for the merit of his elder brother. The donor is evidently the same
as the one mentioned in No. 143 above.

inscription.head:
No. 146.
(A.R. No. 146 of 1907.)
Pūvālaikkuḍi, Tirumayam Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District
Rock-cut cave—on the same wall
inscription.p:
This is a fragment of an inscription of the time of Kulōttuṅga-chōḷadēva of
which the exact regnal year is not clear. It mentions (evidently as a donor) one Ugach-
chargaṇḍaṉ Śīkaṇḍaṉ Achchagaṇḍapperumāṉ. To judge from the expression pañcha-
mahāśabda which is found prefixed to his name, he must have been a notable musician
of his time. Pūvālaikkuḍi is said to be situated in Kūḍalūr-nāḍu.

edition.l: |||—svasti śrī [||*] ko••• ttuṅkacoḻa-

inscription.head:
No. 147.
(A.R. No. 147 of 1907.)
Pūvālaikkuḍi, Tirumayam Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District
Rock-cut cave—on the same wall
Parakēsarivarman: year 5
inscription.p: Published in S.I.I., Vol. XIX, No. 123.

inscription.head:
No. 148.
(A.R. No. 148 of 1907.)
Pūvālaikkuḍi, Tirumayam Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District
Rock-cut cave—on the east wall of the same maṇḍapa
inscription.p:
This belongs to the reign of the Vijayanagara king Vīrapratāpa Sadāśivadēva-
Mahārāya and is dated in Śaka 1471, Saumya, Āni, 13, Monday, [śu], prathamā,
Mūlā, corresponding to A.D. 1549, June 10. It registers an endowment of land at
Nāvaṟkuḍi, which was their kāṇiyāṭchi, by the ūravar of Karaiyūr in Teṉ-Kōṉāḍu a
division of Olliyūr-kūṟṟam in Kaḍalaḍaiyād-ilaṅgaikoṇḍa-Śōḻa-vaḷanāḍu, for the early
morning service of the god in the temple at Tiruppūvalaikkuḍi in Poṉṉamarāpati-nāḍu
as the gift of Tyāgaṇa-Nāyakkar Peddappa-Nāyakkar-Ayyaṉ.

inscription.head:
No. 149.
(A.R. No. 149 of 1907.)
Pūvālaikkuḍi, Tirumayam Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District
Śiva temple—on the south wall of the shrine of the goddess
Māṟ. Kulaśēkhara I: year 16: 1284 A.D.
inscription.p:
This belongs to Māṟavarman Kulaśēkharadēva “who took every country.”
The details of the date are given as Vriśchika, śu. 1, Wednesday, Rēvati which do not
however suit in this year. The inscription registers an endowment of 6(1/2) vēli of land
at Kōvaṉūr as a tax-free dēvadāna by the Nāṭṭavar of Vaḍapaṟṟu-nāḍu including Śevva-
lūr in Kūḍalūr-nāḍu of Teṉkōṉāḍu, and by Kalikaḍinda-Pāṇḍyadēvar, for the mainte-
nance of a service called the Maṟamāṇikkaṉ-śandi instituted by the former in the
temple, and for offerings to the image of the goddess consecrated therein by the latter.

edition.l: svasti śrī [||*] komāṟapaṉma-
karatevaṟkku yāṇṭu 16- vatu
kkuṭpaṭṭa nilam 65- li iṉ-

inscription.head:
No. 150.
(A.R. No. 150 of 1907.)
Pūvālaikkuḍi, Tirumayam Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District
Śiva temple—on the west wall of the same shrine
Jat. Sundara-Pāṇḍya (?): year 17
inscription.p:
This is a record of Jaṭāvarman Sundara-Pāṇḍya whose identity is not clear.
This registers the appointment of three persons, Prakaṭagaṇḍaṉ Mārāyaṉ and his two
brothers Śōṟaṉ and Bhūmaṉ as uvachchar for providing music daily in the temple
at Pūvālaikkuḍi in Vaḍapaṟṟu (northern section ?) of Ponnamarāpadi-nāḍu, and an
endowment of land in perpetuity for their maintenance by the temple authorities and
the ūrār of Kōvaṉūr.

inscription.head:
No. 151.
(A.R. No. 151 of 1907.)
Pūvālaikkuḍi, Tirumayam Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District
Śiva temple—on the north wall of the Bhairava shrine
Achyutarāya: Śaka 1454 (1532-33 A.D.) Khara, Tai, 8.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and seems to record a grant of tax free land to the temple of
Tiruppūvālaikkuḍi-uḍaiya-Nāyanār of Vaḍapaṟṟu by the Ūravar of the Paṟṟu. Reference
is made to the checking of the revenue accounts by one Śemma-Nāyakkar who was
having his camp at Tirupputtūr, and to the default in payment of the iṟai on the part
of a land-owner by name Piḷḷaimalai-Nāḍāḻvār.

edition.p: The rest of the inscription consisting of 5 or 6 lines is badly damaged.

inscription.head:
No. 152.
(A.R. No. 152 of 1907.)
Malaikkōyil, Tirumayam Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District
At the entrance into the (rock-cut) cave, right side
Jaṭ. Kulaśēkhara: year 3: 1192-93 A.D.
inscription.p:
This inscription begins with the introduction Pūvinkiḻatti etc. It records the
remission of the taxes kaḍamai, vari and antarāyam on a dēvadāna land at Pāṇikuḍi-
maṅgalam, belonging to the temple of Śrivaram-uḍaiya-Nāyanār at Kalvāyil
(Melaikkōyil) in Kāna-nāḍu, a subdivision of Virudarājabhayaṅkara-vaḷanāḍu, by
order of the chief Kalvāyil-Nāḍāḻvāṉ. The wording is faulty.

edition.l: [sva]sti śrī [||*] pūviṉkiḻatti meyviṟṟi-

l.ta: tiṟai u(ṟa)ṭaṉ muṟai muṟai yiḻiya irune-
ya iyalicai nāṭakam manni va(ḷ)ḷara maṇi a-

inscription.head:
No. 153.
(A.R. No. 153 of 1907.)
Malaikkōyil, Tirumayam Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District
Rock-cut cave—In the same place, left side
inscription.p:
This is a short inscription in early Pallava characters mentioning the Vidyā-
Parivādini which was a kind of musical instrument. It seems to state that this was
developed by one Guṇasēna and was recommended for a particular kind of music.

inscription.head:
No. 154.
(A.R. No. 154 of 1907.)
Malaikkōyil, Tirumayam Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District
Another (rock-cut) cave—on the south wall
inscription.p:
This is an inscription of Parakēsarivarman Kulōttuṅga Chōḷadēva, the date of
which is lost. It is damaged and seems to record a gift of land.
Published in S.I.I., Vol. III.

inscription.head:
No. 155.
(A.R. No. 155 of 1907.)
Kōṭṭūr near Malaikkōyil, Tirumayam Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District
On a stone set up on the bund of the tank
Kulōttuṅga I: year 47: 1116-17 A.D.
inscription.p:
This records the repair to the tank-bund which is said to have been built by
one Mummuḍiśōḻa-Siṅgaḷāntaka-Muttaraiyaṉ and destroyed by breach some time
afterwards. The repair was effected by Periyāṉ Kulōttuṅgaśōḻa-Kaṉakarājaṉ, the
headman of Koṭṭiyūr in Kāṉa-nāḍu.

edition.l: [t]tuṅkacoḻatevark[ku*] yāṇṭu 47-

l.ta: ṭṭi nāṭṭiṉa kallile eḻuttu veṭṭi(ṉ)ṉen ik-

inscription.head:
No. 156.
(A.R. No. 156 of 1907.)
Mallāṅguḍi, Tirumayam Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District
On a stone set up in the village
inscription.p:
This is much damaged. It belongs to the Vijayanagar king Viruppaṇa-Uḍaiyar
(Virūpāksha I) and is dated in the cyclic year Piṅgaḷa (which corresponds to Śaka 1299,)
Āṉi 14. It seems to record an instrument of assurance addressed to the Tandirimār
of Kalvāśal-nāḍu, Kāṉanāḍu and other places declaring Malaiyālaṅguḍi to be a place
of refuge for all of them. It corresponds to the Añjināṉpugaliḍam “the asylum for
the fear-stricken” of other inscriptions.

inscription.head:
No. 157.
(A.R. No. 157 of 1907.)
Dēvamalai, Tirumayam Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District
On the rock to the left of the maṇḍapa in the rock-cut temple
inscription.p:
This is dated merely in the cyclic year Manmatha, Āvaṇi 24. This may be of the
same period as No. 156 above. It records the grant of some privileges in the temple
(at Pēraiyūr ?) with its appurtenant land and house gift, by the Ūravar of Malaiyālaṅ-
kuṟichchi (Mallāṅguḍi ?) in Paṉṟippaṟṟu of Virudarājabhayaṅkara-vaḷanāḍu, to a
certain Piḷḷaimāmaṉ Poṉṉaṉ and his relations probably for their services (not clear).

edition.l: [śubhamastu] [||*] maṉmata varuṣam āvaṇi ṭṭa 242 kānaṉāṭāna

inscription.head:
No. 158.
(A.R. No. 158 of 1907.)
Pēraiyūr, Tirumayam Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District
Nāganāthasvāmi temple—on the east wall of the maṇḍapa
in front of the central shrine
Dēvarāya II: Śaka 1343
inscription.p:
This is dated in Śaka 1343, Plava, Mārgaḻi, 3, śu. 6, Sunday, Aviṭṭam (A.D. 1421,
November 30) in the reign of Dēvarāya Uḍaiyar, son of Ariyarāya (Harihara). It
stops with the mention of Tirumēṉiyaḻagiyār Vi[ḍai-nāvalaṉ]dēvar Śeṇbagarāyaṉ
alias Vijayālaya••••• of Sūraikkuḍi in Tennāṟṟuppōkku a division of Adaḷaiyūr-nāḍu
of Kēraḷaśiṅga-vaḷanāḍu, who was probably a local chief.

l.ta:
kku tappuva
rāyar kaṇṭaṉ muvarāyar kaṇṭaṉ kaṇṭanāṭu koṇṭu koṇṭanāṭu
kuṭātā

niṉṟa śakābdam 1343- ṉmel cellāninṟa pilavavaruṣam mārkaḻi
[ 3 tiyati] pūrvvapakṣattu ṣaṣṭiyum ādityavāṟa

inscription.head:
No. 159.
(A.R. No. 159 of 1907.)
Pēraiyūr, Tirumayam Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District
Nāganāthasvāmi temple—on the same wall
Rājarāja III: year 20: 1235-36 A.D.
inscription.p:
This records an order of a sāmanta (feudal chief) of the king by name Mahā-
sāhāni Śatrugaṇḍa granting to the temple of Tirunāgēśvaram-uḍaiya-Nāyanār at
Tirupperaiyūr, all the income (tēvai and taragu) like kaḍamai, antarāyam, śilvari,
peruvari, kalavai, kāḍuveṭṭi (?) and muḍippoṉ accruing to him from Śēndamaṅgalam
in Pērāyilnāḍu a division of Jayaśiṅgakulakāla-vaḷanāḍu, for the requirements of
daily worship to the god.

Another inscription of three lines dated in the same year of the king which is
unfinished is found engraved below the present one.

inscription.head:
No. 160.
(A.R. No. 160 of 1907.)
Pēraiyūr, Tirumayam Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District
Nāganāthasvāmi temple—on the same wall
Māṟ. Sundara-Pāṅḍya I: year 13: 1228-29 A.D.
inscription.p:
This is a record of Māṟavarman Sundara-Pāṇḍyadēva (I) ‘who was pleased to
present the Chōḷa Country’. It is partly damaged. It seems to register the lease of a
waste land (nattappāḻ) belonging to the temple, for the formation of a street called the
Kulaśēkharapperunderuvu with the rental (maṉaiyīṟai) fixed at 3/8 kāśu per year on
every plot measuring 3 kōl by 7 by the standard measuring rod known as the Iḍaiyar-
kōl.

edition.l:

vaḻaṅkiyaruḷiya śrīsundarapāṇṭiya tevaṟku yāṇṭu 13- vatu tiru-

inscription.head:
No. 161.
(A.R. No. 161 of 1907.)
Pēraiyūr, Tirumayam Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District
Nāganāthasvāmi temple—on the same wall
inscription.p:
This belongs to the 5th year of Tribhuvana-chakravartin Vikrama-Pāṇḍyadēva.
It is mutilated at the right end and seems to record a gift of land for forming a flower-
garden for the temple by one Tiruviḍaimaruduḍaiyāṉ a horse dealer of Malai-
Maṇḍalam.

edition.l: svasti śrī [||*] tirupu[va*]ṉaccakkaravattikaḷ śrī vi-
kkira[ma*]pāṇṭiya tevaṟkku yāṇṭu 5- vatu [ma]-

inscription.head:
No. 162.
(A.R. No. 162 of 1907.)
Pēraiyūr, Tirumayam Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District
Nāganāthasvāmi temple—on the same wall
Achyutarāya: Śaka 1458
inscription.p:
This is much damaged. It is dated Śaka 1458, Durmukhi, Aṟpaśi [20]. It seems
to register some endowment for the conduct of a festival in the temple in the month
of Śittirai every year, by the Nāṭṭavar of Kāṉa-nāḍu probably in conjunction with
some men of the army. Mention is made of an agent of the king by name Rāśi-
Mudaliyār and of one Vijayālayadēvar.

inscription.head:
No. 163.
(A.R. No. 163 of 1907.)
Pēraiyūr, Tirumayam Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District
Nāganāthasvāmi temple—on the south wall of the same maṇḍapa
Māṟ. Kulaśēkhara: year 22: 1289-90 A.D.
inscription.p:
This is an inscription of Māṟavarman Kulaśēkharadēva ‘who was pleased to
take every country’. It registers the grant of permanent lease of tenancy of a land
called the ‘Kaḍamban-Vayal’, measuring 256 kuḻi and belonging to the temple of
Tirunāgīśvaram-uḍaiya-Nāyanār at Tiruppēraiyūr a dēvadāna-brahmadēya village in
Kānanāḍu alias Virudarājabhayaṅkara-vaḷanāḍu, to Veṉṟāṉ Tirumaḻapāḍi-Uḍaiyāṉ
Tirupperunduṟaippiḷḷai who was holding the māḍāpatyam of the temple, on an annual
rental (kaḍamai) to be paid by the lessee on a mutually agreed basis according to the
nature of the crop raised. The land is said to have been purchased from one Rājarāja-
Nāḍāḻvāṉ of the araśu-makkaḷ (ruling family ?) residing at Malaiyālaṅguḍi and the
Mudalis of the place which is called a paḍaippaṟṟu (army settlement).

inscription.head:
No. 164.
(A.R. No. 164 of 1907.)
Pēraiyūr, Tirumayam Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District
Nāganāthasvāmi temple—on the same wall
inscription.p:
The first two lines are dated in the 33rd year of the same king (Kulaśēkhara)
as above, after which there is a gap making it doubtful whether the subsequent lines
are part of this record. They seem to register a tax-free sale of seven pieces of land
including Nakkanēri-Vayal and Pudarkkuḍikkāḍu by several individuals to the temple,
and the grant of the lease of these lands on permanent tenure to the same
Tirupperunduṟaippiḷḷai mentioned above (in No. 163) on similar rental basis of payment
by the lessee to the temple according to the nature of crops raised.

The names of the taxes on the lands remitted are given as araśuvari, antarāyam,
veṭṭimuṭṭāvāḷ, pañjupīli, śanduvigrahappēṟu, echchōṟu, kūṟṟariśi, śilvari, peruvari and
kīḻvārappachchai, and these were agreed to be paid on behalf of the temple by the
sellers. Though the transaction is called a ‘sale’ the price of the lands is not specified.

inscription.head:
No. 165.
(A.R. No. 165 of 1907.)
Pēraiyūr, Tirumayam Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District
Nāganāthasvāmi temple—on the north wall
of the Kāṭchikoḍutta-Nāyanār shrine
Māṟ. Kulaśēkhara I: year 22: 1289-90 A.D.
inscription.p:
This is damaged. It registers the sale of the permanent tenancy right over a
dēvadāno land known as Ponnāvayakkal by the Araiyars and the Mudalis of
Malaiyālaṅguḍi to the same Māḍāpatyam Veṉṟāṉ Tirumaḻapāḍi-Uḍaiyāṉ Tirupperun-
duṟaippiḷḷai with the taxes thereon remitted at their cost.

inscription.head:
No. 166.
(A.R. No. 166 of 1907.)
Pēraiyūr, Tirumayam Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District
Nāganāthasvāmi temple—on the rock in front
Rājēndra-Chōḷa I: Date lost
inscription.p:
This inscription is badly damaged. It seems to register a grant of land
tax-free to the temple by the Mahā-sabhā of the place.

edition.l: [sva]sti [||*] kopparakecaripaṉma••• śrī rāce[n]-

inscription.head:
No. 167.
(A.R. No. 167 of 1907.)
Pēraiyūr, Tirumayam Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District
Nāganāthasvāmi temple—on the same rock
Rāj. Kulōttuṅga-Chōḷa: year 15
inscription.p: This is also damaged.

The details of the transaction are not clear, but mention is made of a gift of
50 sheep left in charge of the sabhā of the place for burning a lamp in the temple
of Tirunāgīśvaram-Uḍaiya-Mahādēvar. Reference is made to one Kuvāvaṉ
Śatturugaṇḍappēraiyaṉ of Koṭṭiyūr and to a certain Poyyilippēraiyaṉ.

inscription.head:
No. 168.
(A.R. No. 168 of 1907.)
Pēraiyūr, Tirumayam Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District
Nāganāthasvāmi temple—on the same rock
inscription.p:
This belongs to the 36th year of Chakravartin Kulōttuṅga-Chōḷadēva and
records a sale of land free of taxes to the temple by one Māḍalaṉ Nārāyaṇa•••••••••
Mādhavaṉ of Pēraiyūr.

Rājakēsarivarman: year 3
inscription.p: Published in S.I.I., Vol. XIII, No. 35.

inscription.head:
No. 170.
(A.R. No. 170 of 1907.)
Tiruppalātturai, Tiruchirappalli Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District
Ādimūlēśvara temple—on the same wall
Vikrama-Chōḷa: year 16: 1133-34 A.D.
inscription.p:
This registers a gift of 12 kāśu by one Śāvāndi Tiruvaraṅga Nārāyaṇaṉ
Bhāskaradēvaṉ of Tiruppalāttuṟai for burning a perpetual lamp in the temple of
Mahādēva at the place. Tiruppalāttuṟai is said to have formed part of Uttamaśīli-
chaturvēdimaṅgalam a brahmadēya on the southern bank (of the Kāvērī).

inscription.head:
No. 175.
(A.R. No. 175 of 1907.)
Tiruppalātturai, Tiruchirappalli Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District
Ādimūlēśvara temple—on the north wall of the central shrine
Rājarāja I: year 21: 1005-06 A.D.
inscription.p:
This registers a sale of two plots of land one measuring 3 mā and 3 kāṇi in extent
and the other a quarter (vēli) by the Peruṅguri-sabhā of Uttamaśīli-chaturvēdi-
maṅgalam a brahmadēya in Viḷānāḍu a division of Pāṇḍikulāśani-vaḷanāḍu to the
temple of Mahādēva-Bhaṭṭāra at Tiruppāttuṟai for 97 kaḻañju equivalent to 900 poṉ.
The former is said to have formed part of a bigger land which had been endowed to
the temple on a previous occasion by one Kurukāḍi-kiḻār and which the sabhā had
purchased from the temple. Portion of it was resold to the temple in the 20th year
of the king and the remaining part now.

inscription.head:
No. 181.
(A.R. No. 181 of 1907.)
Uṟaiyūr, Tiruchirappalli Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District
Pañchavarṇeśvara temple—on the south wall of the central shrine
inscription.p:
This is a fragment of an inscription dated in the 11th year of some king whose
name is lost. The writing is of about the 10th Century A.D. Reference is made to
an organisation of a district by name Aha-nāḍu, and also to the ugachchar and
paṭṭuḍaiyār of the temple.

inscription.head:

inscription.head:
No. 185.
(A.R. No. 185 of 1907.)
Uṟaiyūr, Tiruchirappalli Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District
Pañchavarṇēśvara temple—on the west wall of the central shrine
inscription.p:
This consists of a number of disconnected pieces of an inscription. A few words
of the historical introduction of the Chōḷa king Rājēndradēva are traceable in one of
them which is dated in the 3+1st year of the king, expressed definitely as the 319th
day of the year. The inscription registers the grant of a tax-free land measuring 29 vēli
and odd in extent, in two villages under the name Atiguṇakaṟpakanallūr, for the expenses
of worship in the temple of Mahādēva at Uṟaiyūr in Uṟaiyūr-kūṟṟam a subdivision of
Keraḷāntaka-vaḷanāḍu. The pieces give elaborate details of the requirements of daily
worship and of those on special festival days of the year like the Uttaram-ayana-
Saṅkrānti, Kārttigai lights etc. Mention is made of a certain Aṇukkiyār Appi Madu-
rāntakiyār under whose instruction the inscription is said to have been engraved.
Reference is made to a temple of Villīśvara among the boundaries of the land granted.

Subsidiary deities to whom separate offerings were made daily were Chandra-
śēkhara, Gaṇapati, Lōkaviṭaṅka and Uṟandai-Nāyaka.

inscription.head:
No. 186.
(A.R. No. 186 of 1907.)
Uṟaiyūr, Tiruchirappalli Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District
Pañchavarṇēśvara temple on the north wall of the central shrine
Parak: Rājāraja II: year 7 (A.D. 1152)
inscription.p:
The details of date are given as 7th year, Makara, śu. 14, Monday, Uttiram.
Makara, śu. 14 can never combine with Uttiram. The intended date was probably
A.D. 1152, January 21, f.d.t. ·80, the Nakshatra on this day being Punarvasu. The
inscription is damaged with portions lost. It seems to register a grant of land by the
assembly of Rājāśraya-chaturvēdimaṅgalam in Uṟaiyūr-kūṟṟam for the expenses of
daily worship and of a festival in the month of Vaigāśi in the temple of Tiru-Uḍaitalai-
Mahādēva at Tiru-Uṟaiyūr.

edition.l:
svastiśrī [||*] koparakeca[rivaṟmara]••••• uṭaiyār śrī rājarājatevaṟku
yāṇṭu eḻāvatu makaranāyaṟṟu pūrvapakṣattu śaturdēśiyum tiṅkaḷk
kiḻamaiyum peṟṟa u-

talaimahātevarkku tiruvaiykācit
tirunāḷukkum nittappaṭikkum ce••• paḷḷittāmattukku[m*] nammūr periya-

inscription.head:
No. 187.
(A.R. No. 187 of 1907.)
Uṟaiyūr, Tiruchirappalli Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District
Pañchavarṇēśvara temple—on the same wall
inscription.p:
This registers a tax-free gift of two plots of land, one at Atiguṇakalpakanallūr
and the other at Ulagarviḍaṅganallūr, for the expenses of offerings and worship in the
temple of Vēndaṉ Mukkīśvaram-uḍaiya-Nāyanār at Uṟaiyūr in Rājagambhīra-vaḷanāḍu
by order of the king under title Kōnērinmaikoṇḍāṉ, issued in the 32nd year of his reign.

No. 188.
(A.R. No. 188 of 1907.)
Uṟaiyūr, Tiruchirappalli Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District
Pañchavarṇēśvara temple—on the south wall of the central shrine
inscription.p:
This is a damaged and incomplete record stopping with the mention of the
7th year of king Parakēsarivarman Rājēndradēva after giving his historical introduction.

inscription.head:
No. 189.
(A.R. No. 189 of 1907.)
Tribhuvanam, Kumbhakonam Taluk, Tanjavur District
Kampaharēśvara temple—on the south wall of the central shrine
inscription.p:
This is a damaged and fragmentary inscription in Grantha alphabet of about
the 10th Century A.D. mentioning a certain Ārya Śrī Sōmanātha.

p.gr: ārya śrīsōmanāthō vā yadva || civā[dama]dhu sva••••• viśvādhikaviv[ē]••••• kapraṇa |

inscription.head:
No. 190.
(A.R. No. 190 of 1907.)
Tribhuvanam, Kumbhakonam Taluk, Tanjavur District
Kampaharēśvara temple—on the same wall
inscription.p:
This is a Sanskrit inscription in Grantha of the reign of Kulōttuṅga-Chōḷadēva (III)
who is stated to have won victories over the kings of Simhaḷa and Kēraḷa and over
Vīra-Pāṇḍya after destroying his capital city Madura, and to have performed the anoint-
ment of heroes. He is also eulogised for his strength of arms and for his proficiency
in arts. The record states that he built (renovated ?) the maṇḍapa for God Sabhāpati,
the gōpura over the shrines of Śrīpati (Vishṇu) and of Girijā (Pārvati), the prākāra
and the golden hall (at Chidambaram ?); the temple for Ēkāmrēśvara (at Kāñchīpuram);
for Hālāsya (at Madurai); for Madhyārjuna (at Tiruviḍaimarudūr); for Rājarājēśvara
(at Dārāśuram ?); for Valmīkēśvara (at Tiruvārūr) for whom he also constructed
a golden hall and a gōpura; and finally for Tribhuvanavīrēśvara (at Tribhuvanam) for
whom he built this temple of great beauty with several shrines and tall vimānas. He
got the god and goddess consecrated in the temple by his guru Sōmēśvara (also called
Īśvara-Śiva) son of Śrīkaṇṭha-Śambhu. This teacher Sōmēśvara was a great scholar
well-versed in the eighteen vidyās (Śaiva-darśanas) and capable of expounding the
glory of Śiva as taught in the Upanishads. He is said to have written a work called the
Siddhāntaratnākara. He is surmised by Mr.Venkayya to be identical with Īśānaśiva
the author of Siddhāntasāra (An. Rept. 1908, Part II, para 64).

edition.p:
1. svasti śrī || ācakrā mānurādhī samaraśirasi [ya]ssaṁharan siṁhaḷe[ndr]ā nyojaiṣit keraḷeśaṁ vijitasurapatiṁ
yo'vadhī dvīra[pāṇḍyam] [|*] yo-vā vīrāmiṣekaṁ vyatanuta madhurāṁ pāṇḍyaguptā mmathitvā rājā trailokyavīrassa bhuvivijayate
śrikulottuṁgacoḷa [|*] || . viśvaṁbharā yasya bhujārggaḷāyāṁ hatopasarggaṁ paripālayantyām [|*] prīsyā smaratyādi barāhadaṁṣṭrā-
vāsāsidhārāvratavedanā[nām] [||*] bhujapratāpā[nala]dhūmalekhā

2. saṁgrāmalakṣmīkabarīkalāpaḥ [*] trailokyarakṣāvidhi viśvamūrtte rjjāgartti yasyānuguṇaḥ kṛpāṇaḥ [||*] dyāvābhuvau
vyāpya vijṛmbamāṇā niraṁkuśaṁ rājya••••••• [|*] rayāṁgapāṇe ranukurvatīva mūrtteraparyyāptajagatrayāyāḥ || . saundarye
madanastaruviṁtaraṇe divya kṣamāyāṁ kṣiti[ḥ] kroghe daṇḍadharaḥ parākramavidhau tau jāmadagnypṛrjjunau [*] nītau [śu]krabṛhaspatī
surapati strailokyarakṣākrame saṁgīte bharato muni rnnarapati[rdhī]raḥ kathaṁ varpṇyate ||.

3. sabhāpatermmaṇḍapamagravarti girīndrajā veśmani gopurañca prākāraharmmyañca [vi]dhā[ya] hemantadekabhakto mumude
narendraḥ || . ekāmre[śva]ra divyadhāma madhurā hālāhalāsyālayaṁ śrīmadhyārjjunadhāma dhāma catathā śrīrājarājeśvaram
valmīkeśvara veśmaceti ni[khi]lannirmmāya haimannṛpo valmīkādhipateścakāra ca [sa]bhāndivyām mahat gopuram || . jitvā
pṛkṣīntribhutanavīra ścaturantāñcakre sthānantribhuvanavīreśvarametat nānāharmmyaprakaṭitaśobham bahurathyaṁ haimottuṅga pravaravimāna
syagitā[rkam] [||*]

4. śrīkaṇṭhaśambhu tanayena patirnnṛpāṇām pāṇḍyārirīśvaraśivena mai••••••• smin someśvareṇa guruṇā
bhuvanasya pitro[ḥ*] śreṣṭhāmakārayadasau śivayoḥ pratiṣṭhām || . vidyā yena manīṣiṇā nikaṣitā vispaṣṭamaṣṭādaśa
sthāṇoryyena viviktamaupaniṣadaṁ viśvādhikatvaṁ vibhoḥ yenākāri ca śaivadarśanadṛśā siddhāntaratnākaro rājño yasya gurussa
īśvaraśivaḥ kastasya mṛgyo guṇaḥ ||

inscription.head:
No. 195.
(A.R. No. 195 of 1907.)
Tiruviḍaimarudūr, Kumbhakonam Taluk, Tanjavur District
Mahāliṅgasvāmi temple—on the same wall
Parāntaka I: year 38: 944-45 A.D.
inscription.p:
This is an incomplete inscription. It mentions a dry land measuring 2-3/4 vēli
and belonging to the temple of Tiruviḍaimarudil-Āḻvār with its boundaries specified.
Among these was a channel branching off from the old course of the river (Paḻaṅ-
kāviri). Reference is also made to an inspection of the affairs of the temple by one
Tirupādāṭperundiṇai, the agent of Irumu[ḍi]śōḻa-Pallavaraiyar of Tiruviḍaimarudil.

Tiruviḍaimarudūr, Kumbhakonam Taluk, Tanjavur District
Mahāliṅgasvāmi temple—on the same wall
Rājēndra-Chōḷa I: year 3: 1015-16 A.D.
inscription.p:
This records a gift of 45 sheep for burning a lamp every night in the gate known
as Ēkanāyakaṉ-tiruvāśal built in the temple by Kurāvaṉ Rājarājaṉ alias Vikramaśōḻa-
Śōḻiyaraiyar an officer of the king. This gift is witnessed by the manager of the temple,
the Nagarattār of Tiruviḍaimarudil and the Ūrār of Tiraimūr.

inscription.head:
No. 201.
(A.R. No. 201 of 1907.)
Tiruviḍaimarudūr, Kumbhakonam Taluk, Tanjavur District
Mahāliṅgasvāmi temple—on the same wall
Parāntaka I: year 20: 926-27 A.D.
inscription.p:
This records an endowment of 40 kaḻañju of gold by one Ēṟan Aiyāṟaṉ of Muḻai-
yūr in Miḻalaināḍu for pōnagam and porikkaṟi offerings to the deity during the early
morning service in the temple of Mūlasthānattu-Mahādēva. The sabhā of Tiraimūr
received about half the amount and the Ūrār of the dēvadāna of Tiraimūr, the balance,
and from this investment 60 kalam of paddy was payable every year as interest. The
Sabhā held itself responsible for measuring a padakku of paddy into the temple every
morning.

Still another inscription of the same year recording a gift of 50 sheep by a

Māyāṉ Pugaḻaṉ.

An inscription of the same year recording the improvement effected to a (temple)
land for making it cultivable by one Kavarāmoḻi Mādēvaṉ alias Toṇḍaraḍippoḍi. The
yield from this land seems to have been intended to meet the expenses of worship of the
(goddess) Bhaṭṭārakī.

Tiruviḍaimarudūr, Kumbhakonam Taluk, Tanjavur District
Mahāliṅgasvāmi temple—on the same wall
inscription.p:
The inscription is much damaged. The king's name and the date are both lost.
The impression is missing. Neither could another copy be taken as the central
shrine of the temple has been completely renovated with new construction.

inscription.head:
No. 212.
(A.R. No. 212 of 1907.)
Tiruviḍaimarudūr, Kumbhakonam Taluk, Tanjavur District
Mahāliṅgasvāmi temple—on the same wall
inscription.p:
The beginning of this inscription is lost and hence it cannot be definitely assigned
to any king. The writing is of about the 10th century A.D. The object of the record
is the setting up of an image of Naṭarāja (Āḍalviḍaṅgadēvar) in the temple by one
Śrī Kāryam Iṅgaṇāṭṭu-Pallavaraiyar, who is also stated to have presented several gold
ornaments to the deity. It registers also a contribution in money made by a Kavarai
Śeṭṭi resident of Tirunāraṇachchēri (quarter) by name Tirunāvukkaraiyaṉ, for digging
a tank. The Sabhā of Tiraimur consisting of 300 members, the Nagarattār of 400
members and other unspecified devotees, utilised the amount however for making a
golden necklace to the god which they named Eḻunūṟṟuvaṉ after these two bodies.

l.ta:
—ṉ kaḻañcey muṉṟu mañcāṭiyuṅ kuṉṟiyum vaḷaiyil oṉṟiṉāl
poṉ nāṟkaḻañcum poṟpū oṉṟu poṉ kaḻañceykālum••••• viy
ceyta poṉ irupattu irukaḻañcey eṭṭu mañcāṭiyuṅ kuṉṟiyum
meṟpaṭi

mārāykiṉṟa iṅkaṇāṭṭuppallavaraiyar tāmey
tirumeṉiy aṭṭuvitta tiruviṭaimarutil āṭalviṭaṅkatevarkku
āparaṇañ

edition.l:
ce[y*]tum tiruviṭaimarutil tirunā[ra*]ṇacceri••••• mācāttanūrāṉa
tiruppātāḷa kavaṟaiceṭṭiy tirunāvukkaraiyaṉ kuḷaṅkalla eṉṟu
[na]ṭantu paṇṭāra[t*]tu vaiy[c*]ca poṉ 5 mālaikku uṭalāka
koṇ[ṭu] tiraimur sabhaiyār 300- varum nakarattār 400- varumāy
700- vaṉ eṉṟu mālaiy per iṭṭu ip poṉṉukku talaimāṟu ivar
kaḷum bhattajanaṅkaḷum kuḷaṅkalliṉa talaimāṟu ippoṉnu

••• bhakta janaṅkaḷ ciṟpu iṭṭu eṟiṉa ci[ṟpu]••• nūṟṟu[aym]pattu eḻum
immālaiyil eṟiṉa kallukku iṭṭa kācu āṟum mālaiy ceyta iru
muṭi coḻapperunta[ṭṭā]nukkum kaṇṇiyāṉa poṟkoyil perun
taṭṭānukkumāka iṭṭa kācu eḻum āka kācu patiṉmūṉṟinukku uṭal
tiruviṭaimarutuṭaiyār paṇṭārattuk koṇṭa kācu 5- m bhakta
janaṅkaḷ ciṟpu iṭṭa kācu 8- m immā[lai*]y ceyta meṟpaṭi taṭ
ṭār[k*]key tiruviṭaimarutuṭaiyār paṇṭārattuk kuṭutta ne[l*]lu
••• ttumukkalam 3 immālai ceytatu itu panmāhēśvararakṣai [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 220.
(A.R. No. 220 of 1907.)
Tiruviḍaimarudūr, Kumbhakonam Taluk, Tanjavur District
Mahāliṅgasvāmi Temple—on the same wall
Rājēndra-Chōḷa I: year 30: 1042-43 A.D.
inscription.p:
This is mutilated and also built in at both its ends. It seems to register a tax-
free sale of 5 vēli and odd of land made by the Mūlaparishat of Śeyñalūr in Miḻailai-
nāḍu to the temple of Tribhuvanapati-Mahēśvara at Tiruviḍaimarudūr after receiving
500 kāśu. With this land the Management proposed to provide for worship and
offerings to the image of Umāpati Ēkanāyaka set up in the temple by a certain
Veṇkāḍaṉ Śaṅkaraṉ alias Rājarāja-Viḻupparaiyaṉ.

l.ta:
—maiyiṟ cūṭuṅ kulataṉamākiya palar pukaḻ muṭiyum ceṅkatir mālaiyuñ
caṅkatir velaittol peruṅkāval pala paḻantivum ceruviṟ cinavi
irupattorukāl arai[cu]kaḷai kaṭṭa paracurāmaṉ mevaruñ cā

mattivaraṇ karuti iruttiya cempoṟṟiruttaṟku muṭiyum payaṅkoḷ
paḻipaṭu mucaṅkiyal mutukiṭṭoḷitta cayaciṅkaṉ aḷapperum pukaḻoṭu
••••• iraṭṭapāṭi eḻarai ilakkamun na[va]ṉetikkulap perumalaikaḷum•••
viracakkarakoṭṭamum••• paṭarvallai maturamaṇṭalamuṅka••• nāmaṇaikko
ṇaiyum••••• virar pañcappaḷḷiyum pācaṭaippaḻaṉ[m] mācuṉitecamum
paya••••• cel kulattintira••••• palataṉattoṭun naṟkula taṉakkuvaiyuṅ
kiṭṭaruñ cerimiḷai oṭṭavi

pālaṉai vemmuṉaiyaḻittu vaṇṭuṟaicolait taṇṭaputtiyum iraṇa
cūraṉai muraṇukat tākkit [tikkaṉai]kirttit takkaṇalāṭamuṅ kovi

ṭat taṅkāta cāral vaṅkāḷatecamunto[rukaḻal]
caṅkuvoṭṭal mahi[pālaṉai] veñcamarviḷākat tañcuvittaruḷi
piṭiyalyāṉaiyum peṇṭir paṇṭāramu••••• nattilane••••• ttaralāṭamum
••••• cāḻal ūralil viccātiratto[raṇa]mum meytta••••• tarumuṭika
••••• tarum niṟai śrī [vi*]caiyamum tuṟainā••••• yāṟ naṟpaṉaiyum maṉ
••••• ḻ cūḻ māyiruṭi••••• ṟpuṉal mā[pappā]ḷa[mu]—

edition.l:
—poḻil••••• māpporu taṇṭāṟkoṇṭa kopparakecaripaṉmarāṉa
uṭaiyār śrīrājēndracoḻatevaṟku yāṇṭu 30- āvatu rājentira
ciṅkavaḷanāṭṭu miḻalaināṭṭu ceyññalūr mūlaparaṭaiyom uyyak
koṇṭārvaḷanāṭṭu tiraimurnāṭṭu tiruviṭaimarutuṭaiyār
tribhuvaṉapati mahēśvaradēvarkku iṟaiyiliāvaṇañ ceytukuṭutta
paricāvatu o•••••••• ṭai uṭaintu eṅkaḷūr•••••••• māṉa•••••••• keṭṭu
eṅkaḷūrkku iṟai iṟukkamāṟṟāmaiyil••••• vaiykka ittevar paṇ
ṭārattu••••• kācu 500 ikkācu aññūṟuṅ ko[ṇṭu] uṭaiyār
śrīrājēntra— coḻanallūr uṭaiyāṉ veṇkāṭaṉ caṅkaraṉāṉa rāja
rāja viḻupparaiyaṉ ikko[yilil] eḻuntaruḷuvitta [umāpatit]
tirume[ṉi] ekanāyakarkkut tiruvamutuḷḷiṭṭu veṇṭu nivantaṅ
kaḷukku eṅkaḷūr muṉpu vi[lai]koṇṭu aṉupavittu varukiṉṟa nilat
tukkuk kiḻpāṟkellai mā••••• nilattukkum rājarājappe[ruv]āykkā
lukku meṟkum teṉpāṟkellai••••• kāṇi kūt•••••••• tallap peruvāyk
kālukku kiḻakku[m vaṭa]pāṟkellai••••• ṭaiyār nilattukku teṟkum
ivvicaitta perunāṉkellaikku naṭuvupaṭṭa nilam••••• iṉnilam
[ai]ñce yeḻumā mukkāṇiyum mikutikkuṟaivuḷḷaṭaṅka inṉilattukku
veṇṭum cil••••• kuṭukkakkaṭavom—

inscription.head:
No. 221.
(A.R. No. 221 of 1907.)
Tiruviḍaimarudūr, Kumbhakonam Taluk, Tanjavur District
Mahāliṅgasvāmi temple—on the same walls
Parakēsarivarman: year 14

inscription.head:
No. 222.
(A.R. No. 222 of 1907.)
Tiruviḍaimarudūr, Kumbhakonam Taluk, Tanjavur District
Mahāliṅgasvāmi temple—on the west wall
of the central shrine
Parāntaka I: year 37: 943-44 A.D.
inscription.p:
This inscription seems to record a decision of the Sabhā of Tiraimūr, the
Nagarattār of Tiruviḍaimarudil and some other dignitaries, who had met in the
Nāṭakaśālai of the temple, to set apart the income from a certain (temple) land for
the purchase of 180 nāḻi of oil required for burning lamps in the temple on the three
festival days every month viz. days of Ārdrā and Śatabhishaj and on Amāvāsyā.
Reference is made to the administration (ārāychchi) of one Vāsudēvanār of Kurumbil.

l.ta:
tārum nāṭakacālaiyil iruṉtu tiṅkaḷp perumpali tiruvātirait tiru
viḻāvukkum cataiyat tiruviḻāvukkum amāvāsit••••• tiru[vi]ḻā
[vu]kku•••••

ākap perumpali tiṅkaḷ muṉṟiṉukku tiṅkaḷ eriyum eṇṇai pati[ṉai]
ñāḻiyāka orāṭṭaikkut tokka eṇ[ṇai] ṉūṟṟoṇpati [nā]ḻikku
ṉivantañ ceyta••• ū•••••

yār koyiliṉ••••• vāṟṟiṉa teṉkarai akaiya[ṟā]ṉa nila[kaṅka] coḻa
aṇukka(ṉā)ṉ cey[yu]m itaṉ••• karuppaṭṭiyāṉ ceyyum••• nṟainila•••
kkal inilam aṭuttuk[koṇṭu] erippatāka nivantañ ceytatu [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 223.
(A.R. No. 223 of 1907.)
Tiruviḍaimarudūr, Kumbhakonam Taluk, Tanjavur District
Mahāliṅgasvāmi temple—on the same wall
Parāntaka I: date lost
inscription.p:
This is also an inscription of Parāntaka I, with its pieces disjointed and
damaged. It seems to register gifts of lands made by some persons to the temple,
including a plot of 9 mā granted by a lady who is called a Talaikkōli (a title
of distinction in music). Mention is made of a daughter of one Taḷichchēri Dēvanār,
but it is not clear whether these two names refer to one and the same person.

edition.l: svastiśrī ||— matiraiko[ṇṭa ko]ppara•••••

l.ta:
māvaraikkuk kiḻellai [ci]ṟukuṭāṉ amutaṉ a••• catirakkāṟceykku
me[ṟ*]kkum teṉnellai vakkama•••

ṉ cattiyum ivviruvar nilattukku vaṭakkum melellaiy ānāṟṟu vāyk
kālukkuk kiḻakkum vaṭavellaiy•••

tterkkum iṉṉālellaikkum uḷḷiṭṭa iṉṉāṉmāvaraiyum it
talaikkoli[yār]•••

ittevarkku kuṭutta nānmāvaraikkuk kiḻellai ittalaikkoli
yāre

ke ittalaikkoliyār muṉ kuṭutta nāṉmāvaraikkum vaṭakku
melellai vaṭakku nokki ōṭina vāykkālukkuk kiḻakkum vaṭa
vellai•••

kkut teṟkum āka inṉānkellaiyi lakappaṭṭa nāṉmāvaraiyum muṉ
cuṭṭiṉa nāṉmāvaraiyum āka ivvirucārum vanta oṉpatu mā[vum]
ittalaikkoliyāre•••

••• laiyavarākam par[ān]takaṉ••• ṟukkum teṉnilaic kiṟukuṭāṉ amutan
ama[ra*]tiran nilattukku vaṭakkum [i]tte—(nilam ciṟukuṭivicaiya)—

edition.l:
••• kuṭutta oṉpa[tum]ācc[e]••• kkālukkut teṟkum innāṉkellaiy
akappaṭṭa nilam eṭṭumāvum allatu vaṭṭakkālukkuk kiḻe—( nattu
mahādēvarkku pa)—

l.ta: ••• l viḷākattu munṟumā••••• vaṭakkum melellaiy munnā[ḷ] koṇṭu

varkkuk kuṭutta nā[ṉ*]māvaraikkuk kiḻakkum vaṭavellaiy malaiya—
(kuṭutteṉ nakkan)—

••• cceykkut te[r]kku••• āna kāṟ ceyyum vaṇṇa[ṅ]kuṭi vilaikoṇṭa
eḻum[āvukku ellai]y kiḻellaiy kā••• tiya araikkālukku me[r*]
kkum —[kkaiyttiṭṭu iṭṭu]—

••• [u]ṭciṟuvāykkā••••• ḻakkum vaṭa[vellai] kāvina••• māvukkut teṟkum
innālellaiyilum akappaṭṭa eḻumāvaraiya—(ṭa kāliyār kuṭutta
nila)—

••• me[r*]kkum teṉnellaiy p[ākkaran ā]ḷkinṟa kāṟceykku vaṭak
kum melellai araicūruṭaiyāṉ kāṟceykkuk—(mānom ivviru
veli ni)—

••• araikkālum āka immu••• nāṉmāvaraicceyum ciṟukuṭi vicaiya
nalluḻānāna māṟanātaniṭai—(vum itaṟṟiṟampil tiru)—

••• raiyum ce[l*]la tirukkāṟ[kuṭi]••• ḻallaiy varākam parāṉtakan
kalattil••• kkiṉṟa nilattukku me[ṟ*]kkun —(kaṇṭanen ivaṉ)—

inscription.head:
No. 224.
(A.R. No. 224 of 1907.)
Tiruvidaimarudūr, Kumbhakonam Taluk, Tanjavur District
Mahāliṅgasvāmi temple—on the same wall
inscription.p:
This is dated like No. 222 above in the 37th year but the king's name is not
given. It is damaged in places. It refers to Vāsudēvaṉār of Kuṟumbil and to some
provision made by way of land-gift for burning eight lamps every night during
the śrībali offerings in the nāṭaka-śālai of the temple.

l.ta:
- āvatu tiruviṭaimarutuṭaiyārkku kuṟumpi[l] vāsu
tevaṉār ārā[*y]cciyil tiraimur[nāṭṭu] tiruviṭaimarutil

nāṭakacālaiyi[liruntu] irāśrīpali eḻuntaruḷa mala••• ṇṭuviḷakku eṭṭi
ṉuḷ tacca viḷakku

••• naṉtavāṉattu••• lavam••• kamukum teṅ[kum i]luppaiyum itaṉilpaṭṭa
nilam [pu]ṉceyum••• koyiliṉva•••
ṇṭu erikku••• iru nāḻi urikkum olai••• ttu ||—

inscription.head:
No. 225.
(A.R. No. 225 of 1907.)
Tiruviḍaimarudūr, Kumbhakonam Taluk, Tanjavur District
Mahāliṅgasvāmi temple—on the same wall
Parakēsarivarman: year 14
inscription.p: Published in S.I.I. Vol. XIX, No. 345.

inscription.head:
No. 226.
(A.R. No. 226 of 1907.)
Tiruviḍaimarudūr, Kumbhakonam Taluk, Tanjavur District
Mahāliṅgasvāmi temple—on the same wall
inscription.p:
This is only a fragment forming part of the historical introduction of king
Rājēndradēva.

inscription.head:
No. 227.
(A.R. No. 227 of 1907.)
Tiruviḍaimarudūr, Kumbhakonam Taluk, Tanjavur District
Mahāliṅgasvāmi temple—on the same wall
Parāntaka I: year 37: 943-44 A.D.
inscription.p:
This refers to the same (officer) Kuṟumbil Vāsudēvanār as in No. 222 above
mentioned. It records a decision by the Sabhā and Nagarattār and others assembled
in the nāṭaka-śālai to burn a dīpamālā of seventeen lights in front (of the central
shrine) every night beginning with twilight and ending with the midnight service
of the deity, with the income derived from two plots of dry land and 4 mā of garden
land, given on lease to a merchant by name Māyaṉ Śankaraṉ of Kumaramārttāṇḍa-
puram.

edition.l:
svastiśrī [||*] matirai koṇṭa kopparakecaripaṉma[r*]kki yāṇṭu 37
āvatu tiruviṭaimarutuṭaiyārkkuk kuṟumpil vāsutevaṉā[r*]
śrīkācciyam ārā[y*]cci[yil]

l.ta:
yārum tiruviṭaimarutil nakarattārum tirukkoyiluṭaiyār
kaḷu[m*] patipātamulattārum nāṭa[kacālai]yil iruṉtu••• liṉmuṉpil
tivamālai śrīparā•••

tipamālai oṉṟiṉuḷ taccaviḷakku patiṉeḻu aṉtiyampotu santiyā
kālan tuṭaṅki attayāmam amutuceytu tirukkā••• maḷavum nicatam
eriyum•••

[nā]ḻiyiṉukku nivantamākac ceyta ni[lam] kumaramāttāṇṭa[pu]rattu
vijāpāri māyaṉ caṅkaraṉ tevarkku kuṭutta toṭṭam kumaramāt
tāṇṭapurattup pakavaticeri vaṭa•••

••• ṉavvūril puṉceyyum aṭṭattu••• ruvilp pāṭakamum [in]nilamun
tiruviṭaimarutuṭaiyār koyiliṉ vaṭakkil viracoḻa aṇuk•••
viṉmalaik karuppa[ṭ*]ṭiyā[ṉ] nilam nāṉkumāvum āka••• c ce[y*]tatu ||—

inscription.head:
No. 228.
(A.R. No. 228 of 1907.)
Tiruviḍaimarudūr, Kumbhakonam Taluk, Tanjavur District
Mahāliṅgasvāmi temple—on the same wall
Parakēsarivarman: year 14
inscription.p: Published in S.I.I. Vol. XIX, No. 346.

inscription.head:
No. 229.
(A.R. No. 229 of 1907.)
Tiruviḍaimarudūr, Kumbhakonam Taluk, Tanjavur District
Mahāliṅgasvāmi temple—on the same wall
inscription.p:
This is dated in the 14th year of an unspecified king, and records the gift of a
golden bowl for holding the offering to god, made by one Śāttaṉ Vaḍugaṉ head of
the Tribhuvanamādēvi-Pēraṅgāḍi (emporium ?) at Tañjāvūr.

edition.l: yāṇṭu 14 nāḷ [2]64- ṉāl tañcāvūr [kūṟ]ṟa-
ṟai poṉ 95-

No. 230.
(A.R. No. 230 of 1907.)
Tiruviḍaimarudūr, Kumbhakonam Taluk, Tanjavur District
Mahāliṅgasvāmi temple—on the same wall
Parakēsarivarman: year 11
inscription.p: Published in S.I.I. Vol. XIX, No. 278.

inscription.head:
No. 231.
(A.R. No. 231 of 1907.)
Tiruviḍaimarudūr, Kumbhakonam Taluk, Tanjavur District
Mahāliṅgasvāmi temple—on the same wall
Parakēsarivarman: year 11

inscription.head:
No. 232.
(A.R. No. 232 of 1907.)
Tiruviḍaimarudūr, Kumbhakonam Taluk, Tanjavur District
Mahāliṅgasvāmi temple—on the same wall
inscription.p:
This is a mutilated inscription of the 10th year of a king whose name is lost.
It seems to be the record of a gift of land by the son of a Śrīkāryam officer to the temple.

edition.l: yāṇṭu 10- āva-

inscription.head:
No. 233.
(A.R. No. 233 of 1907.)
Tiruviḍaimarudūr, Kumbhakonam Taluk, Tanjavur District
Mahāliṅgasvāmi temple—on the same wall
inscription.p:
This seems to be part of a bigger inscription, evidently No. 234 of which the
beginning is lost. Being also damaged the sequence is not clear after the first six lines.
This portion records an endowment of one vēli of land (evidently by royal order)
for the maintenance of a musician by name Eḻupaṇaidēvaṉ and his descendents for
their service of singing the dēśi songs before the deity while holding the tiruvōlakkam
(levee) as part of the services in the temple. The officer Madhurāntaka-Mūvēndavēḷār
mentioned here figures also in No. 219 above, which has been assigned to king
Uttama-Chōla.

edition.p:
The following two lines do not seem to give sequence to the above though
engraved in continuation. Probably they are part of No. 234.

inscription.head:
No. 234.
(A.R. No. 234 of 1907.)
Tiruviḍaimarudūr, Kumbhakonam Taluk, Tanjavur District
Mahāliṅgasvāmi temple—on the same wall
Parakēsarivarman: year 7
inscription.p: Published in S.I.I. Vol. XIX, No. 181.

inscription.head:
No. 235.
(A.R. No. 235 of 1907.)
Tiruviḍaimarudūr, Kumbhakonam Taluk, Tanjavur District
Mahāliṅgasvāmi temple—on the south wall of the central shrine
Parāntaka I: year 31: 937—38 A.D.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and seems to register an endowment of 28 kaḻañju and odd
of gold by one Tāḻi Kumaraṉ of Teṅgūr in Nalaṅgiḷinallūr-kūṟṟam to provide for
feeding two Brāhmaṇas in the temple daily with the interest thereon, and another gift
of money for burning two perpetual lamps, before the deity.

neṉ tiruviṭaimarutil mu•••

inscription.head:
No. 236.
(A.R. No. 236 of 1907.)
Tiruviḍaimarudūr, Kumbhakonam Taluk, Tanjavur District
Mahāliṅgasvāmi temple—on the same wall
Parakēsarivarman: year 14
inscription.p: Published in S.I.I. Vol. XIX, No. 347.

inscription.head:
No. 237.
(A.R. No. 237 of 1907.)
Tiruviḍaimarudūr, Kumbhakonam Taluk, Tanjavur District
Mahāliṅgasvāmi temple—on the same wall
Parakēsarivarman: year 4
inscription.p: Published in S.I.I. Vol. XIX, No. 93.

inscription.head:
No. 238.
(A.R. No. 238 of 1907.)
Tiruviḍaimarudūr, Kumbhakonam Taluk, Tanjavur District
Mahāliṅgasvāmi temple—on the south wall of the central shrine
Parāntaka I: year [3]6: 942-3 A.D.
inscription.p:
This is damaged in places. It seems to register an endowment of 15 kaḻañju
of gold with the interest on which 30 nāḻi of ghee was to be supplied to the temple
on the day of Tiruvādīrai in Mārgaḻi month. The donor was a native of Irumbēḍu
in Pāgaḷūr-nāḍu a division of Śeṅgāṭṭu-kōṭṭam in Toṉḍai nāḍu.

edition.l: svastiśrī [||*] [matirai] koṇṭa kopparake-

inscription.head:
No. 239.
(A.R. No. 239 of 1907.)
Tiruviḍaimarudūr, Kumbhakonam Taluk, Tanjavur District
Mahāliṅgasvāmi temple—on the same wall
Rājakēsarivarman: year 2
inscription.p: Published in S.I.I. Vol. XIII, No. 7.

inscription.head:
No. 240.
(A.R. No. 240 of 1907.)
Tiruviḍaimarudūr, Kumbhakonam Taluk, Tanjavur District
Mahāliṅgasvāmi temple—on the same wall
Parāntaka I: year 3[7]: 944-5 A.D.
inscription.p:
This is an incomplete fragment, stopping with the mention of Tiruviḍaima-
[rudil].

edition.l: svastiśrīḥ— matirai
ri[ panmaṟku yā] ṇṭu 3[7]

inscription.head:
No. 241.
(A.R. No. 241 of 1907.)
Tiruviḍaimarudūr, Kumbhakonam Taluk, Tanjavur District
Mahāliṅgasvāmi temple—on the same wall
Parāntaka I: year 29: 936-7 A.D.
inscription.p:
This registers a gift of 96 sheep for burning a perpetual lamp in the temple for
the merit of a lady named Dēvaṉ Peṟṟāḷ by her son (?) Giriyāṉ Ādari of Tiruttāṇḍa-
puram.

edition.l: svastiśrī [||*] matiraikoṇ-

inscription.head:
No. 242.
(A.R. No. 242 of 1907.)
Tiruviḍaimarudūr, Kumbhakonam Taluk, Tanjavur District
Mahāliṅgasvāmi temple—on the same wall
Rājakēsarivarman: year 3
inscription.p: Published in S.I.I. Vol. XIII, No. 38.

inscription.head:
No. 243.
(A.R. No. 243 of 1907.)
Tiruviḍaimarudūr, Kumbhakonam Taluk, Tanjavur District
Mahāliṅgasvāmi temple—on the same wall
inscription.p:
This records a gift of money to the temple (purpose not clear) by a native of
Toṅgamaṅgalam in Tirunaṟaiyūr-nāḍu, who is said to have belonged to a regiment (?)
called the Mūtta-Pirāntaka-Aṇukkar very probably after Parāntaka I.

inscription.head:
No. 244.
(A.R. No. 244 of 1907.)
Tiruviḍaimarudūr, Kumbhakonam Taluk, Tanjavur District
Mahāliṅgasvāmi temple—on the same wall
Parakēsarivarman: year 2
inscription.p: Published in S.I.I. Vol. XIX, No. 3.

inscription.head:
No. 245.
(A.R. No. 245 of 1907.)
Tiruviḍaimarudūr, Kumbhakonam Taluk, Tanjavur District
Mahāliṅgasvāmi temple—on the same wall
Parāntaka I: year 17: 924-5 A.D.
inscription.p:
This records an agreement of the Nāṭṭār (assembly) of Tiruviḷai-nāḍu to
measure out once every year in their own place, 360 kalam of paddy (to a represen-
tative of the temple) for the daily offerings to the deity (in their name) during the three
services in the temple.

naṭikaḷukkut tiruviḷaināṭṭu nāṭṭār nicatippaṭi tiruamutu ceyvatāka
muṉṟu potaikkum••• [n]elk kalam āka eṟṟi vaṉta

inscription.head:
No. 246.
(A.R. No. 246 of 1907.)
Tiruviḍaimarudūr, Kumbhakonam Taluk, Tanjavur District
Mahāliṅgasvāmi temple—on the same wall
inscription.p:
This is a damaged record probably of the same king (Parāntaka I), mentioning
a gift of 91 sheep for a lamp. The temple authorities who received the gift agreed
to make an offering of rice in addition, to the deity on the day of Pushya in the month
of Tai.

inscription.head:
No. 247.
(A.R. No. 247 of 1907.)
Tiruviḍaimarudūr, Kumbhakonam Taluk, Tanjavur District
Mahāliṅgasvāmi temple—on the same wall
Parakēsarivarman: year 3
inscription.p: Published in S.I.I. Vol. XIX, No. 45.

inscription.head:
No. 248.
(A.R. No. 248 of 1907.)
Tiruviḍaimarudūr, Kumbhakonam Taluk, Tanjavur District
Mahāliṅgasvāmi temple—on the same wall
Parāntaka I: year [12]: 919-20 A.D.
inscription.p:
Like No. 246 above this also registers a similar gift of 91 sheep for a perpetual
lamp and an offering to the god on the day of Pushya in Tai, by one Śaṅkaraṉ of
Puttūr in Miḻalai-nāḍu.

edition.l:
svastiśrī [||*] matiraikoṇṭa kopparake••• kku yāṇṭu [12] āvatu tiru
viṭaimaruti-

inscription.head:
No. 249.
(A.R. No. 249 of 1907.)
Tiruviḍaimarudūr, Kumbhakonam Taluk, Tanjavur District
Mahāliṅgasvāmi temple—on the same wall
Āditya II Karikāla: date lost.
inscription.p:
This belongs to the reign of Parakēsarivarman ‘who took the head of the
Pāṇḍya’, and records the gift of a quarter (vēli) of land for the maintenance of a garden
by one Tiruveṇkāṭṭu-Pichchaṉ named Parāntaka Mūvēndevēḷār, who also figures in
No. 260 of 1907 ascribed to the reign of Parāntaka II Sundara-Chōḷa, where he
is said to have endowed a flower garden to the temple.

ttaval niṟkka ceytu
kuṭuttatu inantavāṉam koṭaimallikaiyum mā••• ṭaṉ aṭi tiruveṇ
kāṭṭu piccaṉ talaimelaṉa:—

inscription.head:
No. 250.
(A.R. No. 250 of 1907.)
Tiruviḍaimarudūr, Kumbhakonam Taluk, Tanjavur District
Mahāliṅgasvāmi temple—on the same wall
Parāntaka I: year 36: 943-4 A.D.
inscription.p:
This is damaged. It records the gift of a golden fillet for the god and a dish
(for holding offerings) also made of gold, during the administration of Vāsudēvan
Kuṟumbil (figuring in No. 227 above).

edition.l:
svastiśrī [||*] matirai••• yāṇṭu 36 tiruviṭaimarutil•••śvararkku kuṟumpil
vāsudēvaṉ ārā•••

poṉ

inscription.head:
No. 251.
(A.R. No. 251 of 1907.)
Tiruviḍaimarudūr, Kumbhakonam Taluk, Tanjavur District
Mahāliṅgasvāmi temple—on the same wall
Parāntaka I: year 34: 941-2 A.D.
inscription.p:
The inscription gives the exact day of the transaction as 214 of the regnal
year. It is damaged. Mention is made of a theft of the golden bowl and a fillet from
the temple and of their replacement, after recovering their cost(?) from those who were
probably responsible for it (by negligence).

edition.l:
svasti śrī [||*] matirai[koṇṭa]kop[parakeca]ripanmaṟki yāṇṭu 34
[āvatu] nāḷ irunūṟṟorupattu nālināl tiruviṭaimaruti••• ṭaiyaśrī
••••• m ārāyappeṟṟa irumuṭicoḻappal••••• koḷkaiyum paṭṭamum
kaḷavuka••••• ntu kaṭavāraip p[o]•••

taṇṭi ku••• ṭaiy[e]••• eḻu(y) nūṟṟukkaḻañcekāl itināl orupaṭa•••••
kāṭṭi uṭaiyār melcātti itu

inscription.head:
No. 252.
(A.R. No. 252 of 1907.)
Tiruviḍaimarudūr, Kumbhakonam Taluk, Tanjavur District
Mahāliṅgasvāmi temple—on the same wall
Parāntaka I: year 38: 945-6 A.D.
inscription.p:
This registers some provision made for a perpetual lamp in the temple by one
Aṇukkaṉ Araiyaṉ for the merit of Aṟiñjigai-Pirāṭṭiyār. This princess was the grand
daughter of the king, being the daughter of Arikulakēsari (Ariñjaya) and was married
to a Bāṇa chief, in whose memory she made a gift to the temple at Tirunāgēśvaram
in the reign of Parāntaka II Sundara-Chōḷa (Vide S.I.I., Vol. XIII, No. 197).

edition.l:
—||| svastiśrī [||*] matiraikoṇṭa kopparakecarivanma[r*]kku yāṇṭu 38-
āvatu teṉkarai tiraimurṉāṭṭu tiruviṭaimarutilśrīmūla•••••

aṇukkaṉ araiyaṉ oṉ••• tūraṉ[na]m poravaiyarara[ca]ṉ naiyaṉ aṟiñ
ciyaipirāṭṭiyā[r]rai cāttivaitta non•••

inscription.head:
No. 253.
(A.R. No. 253 of 1907.)
Tiruviḍaimarudūr, Kumbhakonam Taluk, Tanjavur District
Mahāliṅgasvāmi temple—on the same wall
Parakēsarivarman: year 2
inscription.p: Published in S.I.I., Vol. XIX, No. 4.

inscription.head:
No. 254.
(A.R. No. 254 of 1907.)
Tiruviḍaimarudūr, Kumbhakonam Taluk, Tanjavur District
Mahāliṅgasvāmi temple—on the same wall
Rājarāja 1: year 10: 985-6 A.D.
inscription.p:
This is dated on the 278th day of the 10th year of the king. It registers the gift
made to the temple, of a golden image of Umāsahita weighing 1172 kaḻañju, together
with the pedestal and aureola, by the senior queen Pañchavaṉ-Mahādēviyār. Refers to
one Kuḻalūr-uḍaiyār as the Supervisor of the temple at the time.

edition.l: ḷ 278- [ṉā]l tiruviṭaimarutuṭaiyār śrīkāryam ārāykiṉṟa kuḻalū-

inscription.head:
No. 255.
(A.R. No. 255 of 1907.)
Tiruviḍaimarudūr, Kumbhakonam Taluk, Tanjavur District
Mahāliṅgasvāmi temple—on the same wall
Āditya II Karikāla: year 4+1: 969 A.D.
inscription.p:
This is a damaged and incomplete inscription of Parakēsarivarman ‘who took
the head of the Pāṇḍya’. This records some provision made by a resident of Uttaraṅ-
guḍi with the Sabhā of Tiraimūr for the maintenance of a paṇḍāri in the temple. A
paṇḍāri is a Śaiva devotee dedicated for service in the temple like preparing flower-
garlands to the deities, singing devotional hymns etc.

edition.l:
svastiśrī [||*] pāṇṭiyaṉ ta•••••••• parakecaripanmaṟku yāṇṭu 4- āvatiṉ eti
rāmāṇṭu atikā••• [v]entaveḷār ārā[y*]cciyil uttaraṅkuṭai
yāṉiṉamperumāṉ [īccuva]raṉ cempiyaṉ ā

l.ta:
ṭaimarutil nakarattā••• ntu niva[n]tam ceytapaṭi tiruviṭaimarutuṭai
yār••••• ppa tiraimūr

yār iṭṭa paṇṭāri oruvaṉukku jivitam
tevarpaṇṭārattil nicatam tūṇi nellum āṭṭaiviṭṭam o•••••
peṟuvatākavu[m]

oru••• iṭuvatāka••••• vaṉukku jivitamāka••••• paṇṭārattu nittam••••• kavum
[p]eṟuvatākavum tiruvi[ṭaimarutil na]karattār nicatam kuṟuṇi [nel]
lum āṭṭaivi[ṭ*]ṭam

inscription.head:
No. 256.
(A.R. No. 256 of 1907.)
Tiruviḍaimarudūr, Kumbhakonam Taluk, Tanjavur District
Mahāliṅgasvāmi temple—on the same wall
Āditya II Karikāla: year 3+1: 968 A.D.
inscription.p:
This, like the inscription above, is also of the same king who is said to have
“defeated Vira-Pāṇḍya and taken off his head”. The inscription stops with the
mention of Parāntaka-Mūvēndavēḷār who was the Supervisor of the temple at the time.

edition.l:
svastiśrī |||— virapāṇṭiya[ṉ]ṉai eṟintu talaikoṇṭa kopparakesari
panmaṟki yāṇṭu 3- āvatiṉ etirāmāṇṭu teṉkarait tiraimūr
nāṭṭu tevatāṉam tiruviṭaimarutil tevarkanmikaḷu[m*] tirukkoyi
luṭaiyārkaḷum śrīkārya[m*] ārāykiṉṟa parāntakamūventaveḷārka-

inscription.head:
No. 257.
(A.R. No. 257 of 1907.)
Tiruviḍaimarudūr, Kumbhakonam Taluk, Tanjavur District
Mahāliṅgasvāmi temple—on the same wall
Kōnērinmaikoṇḍān
inscription.p:
This records the royal order issued in the 6th year and 280th day of the king
(probably Uttama-Chōḷa) under the title given above, from his palace at Paḻaiyāṟu, to
the Nāṭṭār, the brahmadēya Elders and the other concerned assemblies (Ūrār and
Nagaraṅgaḷilār), making over 15 vēli of land at Nallāḍi village in Tirunaṟaiyūr-nāḍu
as tax-free dēvadāna to the temple for the celebration, every month, of a special festival
on the day of Viśākhā which was the king's natal star. The permanent tenancy over
the land was conferred on Araiyaṉ Pirāntakan alias Śembiyaṉ Śōḻiyadaraiyaṉ of
Śiṟukuḷattūr on the stipulation that he and his heirs were to measure out every year in
the temple the produce of 5 vēli of this land at 120 kalam of paddy per vēli with all the
taxes exempted.

The inscription which stops with line 29 is continued elsewhere, and the direction
is given in the following lines as to where to look for the continuation viz. on the
southern side of the ardha-maṇḍapa, the tiers near the Dakshiṇāmūrti image and other
specified places.

edition.l:
svastiśrī [||*] koṉoṉiṉmaikoṇṭāṉ tirunaṟaiyūrnāṭṭu nāṭ
ṭārkkum brahmadēyakkiḻavarkkum tevatāṉa paḷḷiccantak kaṇimuṟ
ṟūṭṭu veṭṭapeṟūrkaḷilārkkum nakaraṅkaḷilār[k*]kum yāṇṭu
9- āvatu nāḷ 280- ṉāl paḻaiyāṟṟu nammiṭṭiluḷ[ḷā]l ācci
koyiliṉ muṉpilkūṭattu nām uṇṭirukka ciṟukuḷattūruṭaiyāṉ
araiyaṉ pirāntakaṉāṉa cempiyaṉ co-

tavirntu ivvūr muṉ kāṇiuṭaiyārai māṟṟi iṉakku ja

kkāṇi
yāka tantu muṉ veḷḷāṉvakaiyil mutalāy varukiṉṟapaṭi tavirntu
tiruviṭaimarutil mahā

varkku tevatāṉa iṟaiyilik kuṭuttu nallāṭi
muṉmutalāy varukiṉṟa nilaṉ patiṉaiyveliyum mikitik kuṟaimai
uḷḷaṭaṅka vaḷai-

itu

inscription.head:
No. 258.
(A.R. No. 258 of 1907.)
Tiruviḍaimarudūr, Kumbhakonam Taluk, Tanjavur District
Mahāliṅgasvāmi temple—on the same wall
Parāntaka I: year [30]: 936-37 A.D.
inscription.p:
This seems to record an endowment after purchase of 2 vēli of land called the
vañjittōṭṭam, made by the assembly of Pāradāyakkuḍi in Naṟaiyūr-nāḍu, part of it
being meant for providing for the expenses of a festival in the temple in honour of
Varaguṇa-Viṭankar (Naṭarāja) in the month of Vaigāśi, and the balance for the main-
tenence of musicians who were to play on instruments during the early morning and
other services in the temple. Refers to Kāri Nakkanār as the agent of the king in
Koṅgu.

edition.l:
svastiśrī [||*] matirai koṇṭa ko[p]parake[cari]y••• [y]āṇṭu [30]•••••
[k]oṅkil coḻaṉārkkāy śrīkāryam ārāykiṉṟa

inscription.head:
No. 259.
(A.R. No. 259 of 1907.)
Tiruviḍaimarudūr, Kumbhakonam Taluk, Tanjavur District
Mahāliṅgasvāmi temple—on the same wall
Parāntaka I: year 24: 930-31 A.D.
inscription.p:
This records the gift of 90 sheep for a perpetual lamp in the temple, made by
one Marudaṉ Puttaṉ of Ānaimaṅgalam in Paṭṭina-Kūṟṟam.

edition.l: svastiśrī [||*] matiraikoṇṭa kopparakesari varmaṟku yāṇṭu 24-

inscription.head:
No. 260.
(A.R. No. 260 of 1907.)
Tiruviḍaimarudūr, Kumbhakonam Taluk, Tanjavur District
Mahāliṅgasvāmi temple—on the same wall
Rājakēsarivarman: year 9
inscription.p: Published in S.I.I., Vol. XIII, No. 195.

inscription.head:
No. 261.
(A.R. No. 261 of 1907.)
Tiruviḍaimarudūr, Kumbhakonam Taluk, Tanjavur District
Mahāliṅgasvāmi temple—on the same wall
Parāntaka I: year 19: 925-26 A.D.
inscription.p:
This records a gift of 30 kaḻañju of gold by Kundavaidēvi a relative (?) of queen
Kokkiḷānaḍigaḷ, for burning a perpetual lamp in the temple of Mūlasthānattu-Perumā-
naḍigaḷ. The amount is stated to have been deposited with the Sabhā of Mādāna-
maṅgalam in Naṟaiyūrnāḍu. An uḻakku of oil was to be measured out every day in the
temple by a person appointed by the Sabhā for the purpose and he was to get a portion
of the offered rice (for his remuneration).

teṉkarait tiraimūrnāṭṭut tevatāṉam tiruviṭaimarutil śrīmulat
tāṉattu[pperumāṉaṭikaḷukku]••••

inscription.head:
No. 262.
(A.R. No. 262 of 1907.)
Tiruviḍaimarudūr, Kumbhakonam Taluk, Tanjavur District
Mahāliṅgasvāmi temple—on the same wall
Parāntaka I: year 18: 924-25 A.D.
inscription.p:
This records a gift of 20 kaḻañju of gold deposited with the Śaṅkarappāḍiyār
(guild) of Kumaramārttāṇḍapuram in Tiraimūr-nāḍu, by a merchant of the place by
name Nakkaṉ Pāṇḍan, for a perpetual lamp in the temple. The former agreed to
measure an uḻakku of oil every day.

inscription.head:
No. 263.
(A.R. No. 263 of 1907.)
Tiruviḍaimarudūr, Kumbhakonam Taluk, Tanjavur District
Mahāliṅgasvāmi temple—on the same wall
inscription.p:
The beginning of this inscription is damaged and it is left incomplete towards the
end. It records a gift of 75 kaḻañju of gold for burning two perpetual lamps inside
the central shrine of the temple with an uḻakku of ghee for each lamp, by one Kumaraṉ
Sarvadēvaṉ of Dēvanūr who had already presented two hanging lamps to the temple.

eḻupattai-

hattey niṉṟeriya muṉvaitta periya tarānilai
viḷakku iraṇṭu iraṇṭukkum nontāviḷakkāka oroviḷak-

nukkum nicatappaṭi aṭṭuvatāṉa ne[y*]yeṇṇai uriyum

inscription.head:
No. 264.
(A.R. No. 264 of 1907.)
Tiruviḍaimarudūr, Kumbhakonam Taluk, Tanjavur District
Mahāliṅgasvāmi temple—on the same wall
Rājādhirājadēva I: year 32: 1050 A.D.
inscription.p:
The inscription begins with the praśasti of the king Tiṅgaḷēr peṟa vaḷar etc.
It records a royal order issued to the dēvakarmis and other members of the establish-
ment of Tiruviḍaimaruduḍaiyār temple, the Sabhā and the Nagarattār of Tiruviḍai-
marudil, the Ūrār of Tiraimūr and lastly the agent of the king, Vikramakēsari-
Vīḻupparaiyan, who had all assembled in the temple, and conveyed through (the
effect that lands 7 vēli in extent had been set apart as dēvadana at Viḷanguḍi in
Veṇṇāḍu. Out of this 2 vēli yielding 240 kalam of paddy per year and two house
sites were to be granted in perpetuity to Araiyaṉ Tiruviḍaimaruduḍaiyāṉ alias
Mummuḍiśōḻa-Nṛittappēraiyan styled the Perundanattu—Gandharvar (Court-musi-
cian ?) and his heirs, to recite the pāṭavyam in the temple. Further details regarding
the disposal of the remaining portion of the dēvadāna land are not available as the
inscription stops incomplete.

edition.l:
svastiśrī [||*] tiṅkaḷer peṟavaḷar aṅkatirkkamarṭ ṭolkulam viḷaṅkat
toṉṟimalikiya vaṭaticaik kaṅkaiyun teṉṟicai ilaṅkaiyum kuṭa
ticai mahotaiyum kuṇaticaik kaṭāramum

inscription.head:
No. 265.
(A.R. No. 265 of 1907.)
Tiruviḍaimarudūr, Kumbhakonam Taluk, Tanjavur District
Mahāliṅgasvāmi temple—on the same wall
[Uttama-Chōḷa] alias Parakēsarivarman: year Kaliyuga
408(3); 13th year: 982 A.D.
inscription.p: Published in S.I.I., Vol. III, No. 138.

inscription.head:
No. 266.
(A.R. No. 266 of 1907.)
Tiruviḍaimarudūr, Kumbhakonam Taluk, Tanjavur District
Mahāliṅgasvāmi temple—on the same wall
Parāntaka I: year 13: 919-20 A.D.
inscription.p:
This records a gift of 90 sheep for a perpetual lamp in the temple, by a certain
Kumāraḍi Varamoḻi a native of Kaṭṭivayal in Muttūṟṟu-kūṟṟam a subdivision of
Pāṇḍi-nāḍu.

ṉom tevakaṉmi

inscription.head:
No. 267.
(A.R. No. 267 of 1907.)
Tiruviḍaimarudūr, Kumbhakonam Taluk, Tanjavur District
Mahāliṅgasvāmi temple—on the same wall
inscription.p:
This is a label inscription in three lines. The writing is of the 10th Century A.D.
It mentions Tirutturutti-Nam[bi] Śrīkaṇṭhar and Śivachara[ṇa]śēkharaṉ. Close to
this is a sculpture representing a liṅga, a worshipper, an attendant and a lampstand.

inscription.head:
No. 268.
(A.R. No. 268 of 1907.)
Tiruviḍaimarudūr, Kumbhakonam Taluk, Tanjavur District
Mahāliṅgasvāmi temple—on the east wall of the central shrine
Parakēsarivarman: year doubtful
inscription.p: Published in S.I.I. Vol. XIX, No. 449.

inscription.head:
No. 269.
(A.R. No. 269 of 1907.)
Tiruviḍaimarudūr, Kumbhakonam Taluk, Tanjavur District
Mahāliṅgasvāmi temple—on the same wall
Parāntaka I: date lost
inscription.p:
This is damaged. It records a gift of gold to the temple by a certain Adigai
Sūraṉ probably to provide a special service to Nakkaṉ (Naṭarāja).

edition.l:
svastiśrī [||*] ••• koṇṭa kopparakecari[varmma][ṟ*]kku yā[ṇṭu]•••• ruviṭai

inscription.head:
No. 270.
(A.R. No. 270 of 1907.)
Tiruviḍaimarudūr, Kumbhakonam Taluk, Tanjavur District
Mahāliṅgasvāmi temple—on the same wall
inscription.p:
This is also a damaged inscription. It mentions Kuḍamūkku the name by
which Kumbhakōṇam was known.

edition.p: The impression of this is not available.

inscription.head:
No. 271.
(A.R. No. 271 of 1907.)
Tiruviḍaimarudūr, Kumbhakonam Taluk, Tanjavur District
Mahāliṅgasvāmi temple—on the west wall
of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine
Vīrapratāpa Achyutadēva-Mahārāya: Śaka 1456 expired: 1534-35 A.D.
inscription.p:
The details of the date are given as Jaya, Tulā, śu. 2, Friday, Viśākhā, which
would correspond to A.D. 1534, October 9.

This is a damaged inscription written in faulty language. This seems to
register a sale of land and house sites by the Śrīpaṇḍārattār of the temple, to a certain
Rāmappa-Nāyaka son of Vasavaṇa-Nāyakar of Aṇṇal-Ēndal in Tiruvaṇṇāmalai
Dēvamaṇḍala-śīrmai effected at the latter's request for 60 paṇam, for the purpose of
a Śubhakshaya, by which is probably meant Śubha-sthala (a hall in which sacrifices
are performed).

l.ta:
coḻamaṇṭala kāveri āṟṟukku terkku uyyakkoṇṭa coḻavaḷanāṭu
ti[r*]aimū[r*]nāṭṭu nayiṉār tiruviṭaimarutūruṭaiya tampirāṉār śrī
paṇṭārattārom tiru-

paṇṇaveṇu[m*] eṅka[ḷai] ellā[rem] keḻkkayile atukku

inscription.head:
No. 272.
(A.R. No. 272 of 1907.)
Tiruviḍaimarudūr, Kumbhakonam Taluk, Tanjavur District
Mahāliṅgasvāmi temple—on the south base
of the same maṇḍapa
Vikrama-Chōḷa: year 7: 1123-24 A.D.
inscription.p: The praśasti begins with the words Pūmādu puṇara.

The inscription states that while the king was seated on his paḷḷippīḍam in the
hall (tiruveḍuttukkaṭṭil) called Ēkanāyakaṉ in the big court-yard in front of the temple,
he ordered that the brahmadēya village Vaṇṇakkuḍi in Tiraimūr-nāḍu, with its lands
about a hundred vēli in extent, was to be endowed as tax-free dēvadana to the temple
under the name Tyāgasamudra-Chaturvēdimangalam, and that it was to receive rent
from the lands at the rate of 60 kalam of paddy per vēli every year, according to the
(revenue) settlement of the [3]8th year (of the king's predecessor Kulōttuṅga I).
Four officers are mentioned at the end of the record, including Sandivigraham [Kuladī-
pa]rāyar, as having issued the royal grant. The inscription also adds at the end that
Nallāḍi in Tirunaṟaiyūr-nāḍu was a dēvadāna of the temple.

inscription.head:
No. 273.
(A.R. No. 273 of 1907.)
Tiruviḍaimarudūr, Kumbhakonam Taluk, Tanjavur District
Mahāliṅgasvāmi temple—on the same base
Vikrama-Chōḷa: year 7; 340th day
inscription.p:
The inscription begins with praśasti Pūmālai miḍaindu etc. This is the uḷvari
document giving effect to the orders recorded in No. 272 above, by entering the lands
in the Tax Registers as tax-free, and getting the charter engraved on stone. It is signed
at the end by seven revenue officers, three of them known as Puravuvari-tiṇaikkaḷa-
Nāyakam and the other four, Puravuvaritiṇaikkaḷa-Mugaveṭṭi.

inscription.head:
No. 274.
(A.R. No. 274 of 1907.)
Tiruviḍaimarudūr, Kumbhakonam Taluk, Tanjavur District
Mahāliṅgasvāmi temple—on the same base
Vikrama-Chōḷa: year 9: 1125-26 A.D.
inscription.p:
This is a mutilated and damaged inscription with the inscribed stones disarranged
(in renovation). It seems to register the uḷvari (execution ?) of the document for the
tax-free grant of lands, made by the king in two separate villages Tribhuvana
muḻuduḍaiviḷāgam and Nallāḍi, for burning a number of lamps in the temple and
in the hall called the Vikramaśōḻan-tirumāḷigai (in the temple premises). The income
from these lands was fixed in money from the former village and in paddy in the case
of the latter.
It is possible that parts of two inscriptions have been mixed up in the pieces.

inscription.head:
No. 275.
(A.R. No. 275 of 1907.)
Tiruviḍaimarudūr, Kumbhakonam Taluk, Tanjavur District
Mahāliṅgasvāmi temple—on the north base of the same maṇḍapa
Vikrama-Chōḷa: year 9: 1125-26 A.D.
inscription.p:
This registers an endowment of six vēli of land in three separate plots at
Vikramaśōḻanallūr, made by the king, to meet the expenses of worship to god Vikra-
maśōḻīśvaram-Uḍaiyār newly consecrated by him in the temple. The rent from these
tax free dēvadāna lands is said to be 345 kalam of paddy, as settled in the revenue
survey conducted in the 38th year (of the king's predecessor).

edition.l:
svastiśrī [||*] pūmātu puṇarap puvimātu vaḷara nāmātu viḷaṅka jayamātu
nilavat taṉṉirupatamalar maṉṉavar cūṭa maṉṉiya urimaiyāl maṇimuṭi
cūṭic ceṅkol ceṉṟu ticaitoṟum vaḷarppa veṅkali niṅki mey

varaiyāḻi naṭappa irucuṭaraḷavu[m*] orukuṭai niḻaṟkiḻ virasimhāsa
nattu mu-

nellu nūṟṟeṇpattirukalane kuṟuṇi irunāḻi u[rik]ku niccayitta
nellu nūṟṟaṟupattu mukkalaṉ[e] patakkum innāṭṭu nettoṟpaṭ
ṭikai iṟaiyili enātimaṅkalattukkaḷil tiruvulakaḷanteṟina nilamāyt

ṭāvatu niccayitta nilattāl nellu nūṟṟaimpattirukalane kuṟu

tevatāṉa iṟaiyili yiṭṭapaṭikkum kūṭinapaṭikkum uḷvari kalveṭṭu .

inscription.head:
No. 276.
(A.R. No. 276 of 1907.)
Tiruviḍaimarudūr, Kumbhakonam Taluk, Tanjavur District
Mahāliṅgasvāmi temple—on the same base
Vikrama-Chōḷa: year 10: 1126-27 A.D.
inscription.p:
This is evidently connected with No. 274 above. It is incomplete, large
portions being peeled off at the end. It records the grant of the taxes on certain lands
measuring about 35 vēli in all, by order of the king, for burning two sets of lamps,
one set consisting of 160 and the other of 240 lights, in the hall Vikramaśōḻan-tirumāḷigai
in the temple. A third set of lights under the name Tiruvaṇukkaṉ-tirunundāviḻakku,
to be burnt in the central shrine, is also mentioned as having been endowed for in
the 9th year, by the queen Tribhuvanamuḻuduḍaiyāḷ. The king is said to have been
occupying the royal seat Śōḻakōṉ in the hall known as Tyāgasamudraṉ-tiruveḍuttuk-
kaṭṭil in the temple, at the time of making the grant.

edition.l:
svastiśrī [||*] pūmātu puṇara puvimātu vaḷara nā[mātu] viḷaṅka jayamātu
nilavat taṉṉirupatamalar maṉṉavar cūṭa maṉṉiya urimaiyāl maṇi
muṭi cūṭic ceṅkol ceṉṟu ticaitoṟum vaḷarppa veṅkali niṅka
meyyaṟan taḻaippak kaliṅka miriyak kaṭalmalai naṭātti valaṅkoḷāḻi
varaiyāḻi naṭappa iru cuṭaraḷavum orukuṭai niḻaṟkiḻ virasiṁhāsa
nattu mukkokkiḻānaṭikaḷoṭum viṟṟiruntaruḷiya kopparakecari
paṟmarāna tribhuvanacakrarttikaḷ śrī vikkiramacoḻatevar
uṭaiyār tiruviṭaimarutuṭaiyār śrīkoyilil tiyākacamuttiraṉ

inscription.head:
No. 277.
(A.R. No. 277 of 1907.)
Tiruviḍaimarudūr, Kumbhakonam Taluk, Tanjavur District
Mahāliṅgasvāmi temple—on a stone built
into the floor of the same maṇḍapa
inscription.p:
This is a fragment forming the beginning of an inscription of Rājēndra-Chōḷa I
with only a part of the praśasti preserved, and that too much damaged and worn out.
