edition.p:
The following piece seems to be connected with the above inscription, though
the sequence is not known.

l.ta: —ṭaṉ nilam nikki nikki niṉṟa nilattu nilam
—yivai nellu aiṅkuṟuṇi muṉṉāḻi uḻakku āka nellu

inscription.head:
No. 393.
(A.R. No. 393 of 1907.)
Maruttuvakkuḍi, Kumbhakonam Taluk, Tanjavur District
Airāvatēśvara temple—on the same wall
Parak. Tribh. Kulōttuṅga-Chōḷa III: year 10: 1187 A.D.
inscription.p:
The inscription begins with the introduction Puyal vāyttu vaḷam peruga etc.
The details of date are given as Kaṟkaṭaka, śu. 12, Saturday, Mūlam (=A.D. 1187,
July 18.)

This records the formation of a village under the name Kulōttuṅgaśōḻaṉ
Nyāyaparipāla-chaturvēdimaṅgalam by grouping the several lands purchased from
the surrounding villages to the extent of six vēli by Ediriliśōḻaṉ alias Iruṅgōḷaṉ
of Śēndamaṅgalam in Neṉmali-nāḍu, a division of Rājēndraśōḻa-vaḷanāḍu, who got
the royal order for its entry in the uḷvari register before presenting the village to the
temple as tirunāmattukkāṇi and ūr-iṟaiyili for providing for worship and repairs in
the temple. The land actually under account in the revenue register is said to have
measured 5 vēli and odd.

l.note: Five more lines of signatories names which follow a ehopelessly damaged.

inscription.p:
This is a Telugu inscription recording the construction of the sluice (kaliṅgulu)
by a brother (name lost) of Daḷavāyi Narasappaya and son of Timmappaya of
Bhāradvāja-gōtra and Āśvalāyana-sūtra, who was himself a son of Rāchappayāmātya
alias Hiraṇyaya.

inscription.head:
No. 395.
(A.R. No. 395 of 1907.)
Kīḻavaḷavu, Melur Taluk, Madurai District
Pañchapāṇḍavamalai—on a boulder
inscription.p:
This is a Vaṭṭeḻuttu inscription of about the 10th century A.D. stating that a
certain Saṅkaraṉ Śirivallavaṉ caused an image (of a god) chiselled on the rock and
gave 50 sheep for the supply of ghee for a perpetual lamp to be burnt (before it)
and also provided for the daily supply of 3 nāḻi of rice for offerings.

inscription.head:
No. 396.
(A.R. No. 396 of 1907.)
Kīḻakkarai, Ramnad Taluk, Ramnad District
Chokkanāthasvāmi temple—on the door-post
of the maṇḍapa in front
Śaka 1453, Vikṛiti, Māśi 10
inscription.p:
This records an agreement given by the ‘Padineṇvishayattār’ (corporation) of
four townships, undertaking to contribute half a paṇam on every hundred pearls
sold at Kīḻaikkarai for meeting the requirements of worship and repairs in the
temple of Niṉaittadu-muḍitta-Piḷḷaiyār at Aṉuttogaimaṅgalam in Śembi-nāḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 397.
(A.R. No. 397 of 1907.)
Kīḻakkarai, Ramnad Taluk, Ramnad District
Chokkanāthasvāmi temple—on a slab set up in the premises
Tirumalai Sētupati-Mahārāja (1645 to 1670 A.D.)
inscription.p:
The inscription is damaged. It seems to register a gift of a village (by the
chief) for the expenses of worship to god Mīnākshi Sundarēśvarar at Kīḻaikkarai
alias Niṉaittadu-muḍitta-Vināyakar-paṭṭiṇam.

inscription.head:
No. 398.
(A.R. No. 398 of 1907.)
Kīḻakkarai, Ramnad Taluk, Ramnad District
Chokkanāthasvāmi temple—on a second slab
set up in the same place
Śaka 1467: A.D. 1545-46
inscription.p:
This is also damaged and worn out. It registers the conferment of the right or
kāval (watch) with its remuneration of the maḍichchilai-vari probably collected from
the hawkers of goods at the place, granted by Achchutarāya-Tummuśi-Nāyakkar to
the Padineṇ-vishayattār of Kīḻaikkarai alias Niṉaittadu-muḍittāṉ-paṭṭiṉam.

inscription.head:
No. 399.
(A.R. No. 399 of 1907.)
Koḍumaḷūr, Mudukulattur Taluk, Ramnad District
Kumiḷīśvara temple—on the two door-posts of the entrance
inscription.p:
Portions of the inscription both at the beginning and at the end are lost.
Hence the name of the king is not clear, but the regnal year is given as the 4th,
probably of Māṟavarman Sundara Pāṇḍya I. The record states that at the request of
Aiyaṉ Maḻavarāyaṉ, the king granted three villages Koṟṟūr, Kaṇṇippēri and Uḻaiyūf
as dēvadāṉa-iṟaiyili to the temple of Uttama Pāṇḍya-Īśvaramuḍaiyār at Mēlai-
Koḍumaḷūr alias Uttama Pāṇḍyanallūr in Vaḍatalai-Śembi-nāḍu for meeting the
expenses of the midday service instituted in the temple under the name Vīramiṇḍaṉ-
sandi by Araiyaṉ Yādavarāyaṉ of the place. The rate of taxes inclusive of
several dues like antarāyam, viniyōgam etc was fixed (to be paid to the temple) at
3/8 kāśu on every mā of wet land, 3/4 of the above on kuṟuvai, half on Aippaśi-
kuṟuvai, and one fourth on lift irrigated land, 1(1/4) tiramam on sesamum and similar
crop growing land. The king is said to have been seated on his throne called
Maḻavarāyaṉ in his palace at Madurai while he issued the order.

l.note: 1)The rest of the inscription is lost.

inscription.head:
No. 400.
(A.R. No. 400 of 1907.)
Koḍumaḷūr, Mudukulattur Taluk, Ramnad District
Kumiḷīśvara temple—on the west wall
Immaḍi Achyutadēva-Mahārāya: Śaka 1456, Jaya, Chittirai 17,
Amāvāsyā, Monday, Aśvati (A.D. 1534, April 13)
inscription.p:
This registers the gift by the king, of the village Mēlai-Koḍumaḷūr alias Uttama-
Pāṇḍyanallūr excluding the lands already being enjoyed as the dēvadāya and brahma-
dāya, for meeting the expenses of worship in the temple of Sētu-Mādhava-Perumāḷ
and of repairs to the temple of Rāmanātha, both situated at Dhanushkōṭi. Half the
village is said to have been granted as tiruviḍaiyāṭṭam and half as dēvadāṉa.

edition.l: māka ivar kā[ tyāya] ṉi vāṭk[ai]-
edition.p: The following few lines are also engraved at the end

inscription.head:
No. 401.
(A.R. No. 401 of 1907.)
Peruṅgaruṇai, Mudukulattur Taluk, Ramnad District
Īśvara temple—on the two door posts of the entrance
Kulōttuṅga-Chōḷa I: year 44: 1113-14 A.D.
inscription.p:
This begins with the introduction Pugaḻmādu viḷaṅga etc. It registers a gift of
some pieces of land to provide for worship and offerings daily and for special
observance of important days like the Amāvāsyā every month and the four ayanas of
the year in the temple of Tiruvēḷaikkāṟa-mūṉṟukai-Īśvaramuḍaiya Mahādēvar at
Taḍaṅgaḻi in Puṉaivāyil of Madurāntaka-vaḷanāḍu, a division of Rājarāja-Pāṇḍināḍu,
by Vēḷāṉ Kāḷaiyaviyaṉ alias Kulōttuṅgaśōḻa-[Aḷḷu]-nāḍāḻvāṉ. The inscription is
left incomplete.

edition.l: svastiśrī [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 402.
(A.R. No. 402 of 1907.)
Peruṅgaruṇai, Mudukulattur Taluk, Ramnad District
Īśvara temple—on stones built into the basement
Vīra-Pāṇḍya: year 41
inscription.p:
These are fragmentary parts of an inscription of Vīrapāṇḍyadēva ‘who having
taken Īḻam (Cylon), Koṅgu and Śōḻamaṇḍalam was pleased to anoint himself at
[Perumbaṟṟa]-Puliyūr’. This is evidently the same as Jaṭāvarmaṉ Vīra-Pāṇḍya (A.D.
1253-75) a contemporary of Jaṭāvarmaṉ Sundara-Pāṇḍya I (acc. A.D. 1251). The
details of the date are given as Mithuna, ēkādaśi, Sunday, Kārttikai which would
correspond to A.D. 1267, June 19, f.d.n. 29. The record seems to be a decision of the
assembly of the place (sabhā-vyavasthā) ratifying an earlier document-probably of
some privilege enjoyed by them-which had been in force till the 11th year of the king,
but which seems to have been dishonoured subsequently. Mention is made of
Uyyavandāṉ alias Śōḻiyadaraiyaṉ under whose instruction the assembly seems to
have met for its deliberation.

edition.l:
svastiśrīḥ— śrīmaṟkīrttikkumel śrīk[o]••••••••• vaṉacakravattikaḷ īḻa
muṅ koṅkuñ coḻama[ṇṭala]••••••• ppuli[yū]••••• kam paṇṇiyaruḷiya
śrīvirapāṇṭiya teva•••

yāṇṭu patiṉālāvatu mithuṉa nāyiṟṟu••• pakṣattu ēkādaśiyum nāyiṟṟuk
kiḻamaiyum peṟṟa kā[ rttikai] •••••ṇai•••• kalam iddēvar[k*]ku patiṉoṉṟā
vatu aṟuti••

l.ta: ttile yiṭṭu aṉupavittup porāniṟka kala•••• tukku

millātapaṭiyiṉāle iṟṟainā•••••••• ṭaya••••••• kaṉ uyyavantāṉāṉa
coḻiyata•••

ṭṭiṉeṉ ivvūr ciṟpācāriyaṉ ce•••• ṉāṉa aḻakiyapāṇṭiya ācāriya
ṉeḻuttu [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 403.
(A.R. No. 403 of 1907.)
Peruṅgaruṇai, Mudukulattur Taluk, Ramnad District
Paṭṭābhirāmasvāmi temple—on the west and south walls
Śaka 1481, Siddhārthi, Āṉi [14], Monday śu.
7, Uttiram: A.D. 1559, June 12
inscription.p:
This records the alienation of two villages Śiruguḍi (forming a part of Peruṅ-
garuṇai) alias Tiruvāḷanallūr and Maḍaikkaḻi belonging as the tiruviḍaiyāṭṭam of god
Aḻagar in favour of the temple of Nayinār Peruṅgaruṇai-Perumāḷ as the presentation
from the former.

edition.l:
svastiśrīḥ—cakābdam 1481- ṉ meṟcellā niṉṟa siddhārtthi varuṣam
āṉi mātam [14] tiyati sōmavāramum pūrvapakṣattu saptamiyum variyāṉayoga
mum peṟṟa uttirattu nāḷ cuntarapāṇṭiya vaḷanāṭṭu brahmadēyam
aḻakar tiruvuṭaiyāṭṭam śrī peruṅka[ruṇaicca]turvēdimamkalattu
nayiṉār [rāma]candran āṉa

l.ta:
aḻakar tirukkaivaḻakkam[ā]kat
tiruvuḷam vāḻntaruḷi naṭantu vanta ūr peruṅkaruṇaippaṟṟu [ci]ṟukuṭi
āṉa tiruvāḷanallūrum maṭaikaḻiyum perunāṉkellaiyum ma••••

inscription.head:
No. 404.
(A.R. No. 404 of 1907.)
Peruṅgaruṇai, Mudukulattur Taluk, Ramnad District
Varadarāja-Perumāḷ temple—on the door-post
of the maṇḍapa in front, right of entrance
inscription.p:
This is a fragmentary Grantha inscription probably recording some gift, made
by the Madurai Nāyaka Chief Kṛishṇa-Bhūpa, son of Vīra Bhūpa on the day of
Amāvāsya in Makara month of the cyclic year Manmatha, which would correspond to
Śaka 1517 (A.D. 1595—96).

inscription.head:
No. 405.
(A.R. No. 405 of 1907.)
Peruṅgaruṇai, Mudukulattur Taluk, Ramnad District
Varadarāja-Perumāḷ temple—on the second door-post
in the same maṇḍapa, left of entrance
inscription.p:
This states that (six pieces of ?) paddy fields (kēdāra-shaṭkam) situated in
Kaliśēkharamaṅgalam a hamlet of Pūḷiyagrāma were endowed for the maintenance of
Vēdic scholars residing at Mahākaruṇāgrāma (Peruṅgaruṇai) by (the favour of)
Sundarēśa (King Sundara-Pāṇḍya ?)

inscription.head:
No. 406.
(A.R. No. 406 of 1907.)
Peruṅgaruṇai, Mudukulattur Taluk, Ramnad District
Varadarāja-Perumāḷ temple—on a stone built
into the base of the gōpura
Tribh. Kulaśēkhara: year 2
inscription.p:
This is a fragmentary record registering a gift of land for the worship of Śiva
and his consort Tirukkāmakkōṭṭa-Nāchchiyār. The stone was probably a part of the
Īśvara temple of the place.

edition.l: ṇṭu [2]- vatu maṇa••••

inscription.head:
No. 407.
(A.R. No. 407 of 1907.)
Peruṅgaruṇai, Mudukulattur Taluk, Ramnad District
Varadarāja-Perumāḷ temple—on a second stone
built into the same base
Tribh. Sundara-Pāṇḍya: year 3
inscription.p: This is also a fragment. It mentions Madura-udai-vaḷanāḍu in Pāṇḍi-maṇḍalam.

inscription.head:
No. 408.
(A.R. No. 408 of 1907.)
Hanumantaguḍi, Tiruvadanai Taluk, Ramnad District
Māḷavanātha (Jain) temple—on some stones lying in front
Śaka 1455: Pūraṭṭādi 20
inscription.p:
This is fragmentary. It mentions the village Jinēndramaṅgalam alias Kuru-
vaḍimidi••••• in Muttūṟṟu-kūṟṟam and the god Arha-Paramēśvara Māḷuvanāyakar,
and records some gift (of land and money) to the temple. Two other pieces mention a
kāvaṟ-chuvandiram.

l.ta: yirakaṇṭaṉ kaṇṭanāṭu koṇṭu koṇṭanāṭu kuṭātāṉ pū

edition.l: yaṉa kaliyugasamvatsarattu śakāttam 1455- ṉ mel cella••

l.ta: rūrāṉa patiṉeṇpūmi nallūr eḻaṭaivukkuc camainta kāttāṉāṉa pa••••

ṉum tattaṅkāttāṉāṉa paḻan tirāvakaṅkaḷum kāttāṉāṉa kālikku
veliyu[m*] piccaṉāṉa ku••••

val paṭṭaiyaṅ kuṭuttapaṭi ippaṭi kāval niyamittavaiyāna nañcaiy
puñcai kuḷamu••••

ituvum ental ūṟuṇi toppu toṭṭam turavu koyiṟ cuvantiram
kuḷaccuvantiram••••
••ṉ paṟaiyaṉ uḷpaṭṭa peṟkuk kāṟ paṇamum vai••••

inscription.head:
No. 409.
(A.R. No. 409 of 1907.)
Periyakuḷam, Periyakulam Taluk, Madurai District
Rājēndrachōḷīśvara temple—on the west wall
of the central shrine
Māṟ. Kulaśēkhara I: year 26: 1293-94 A.D.
inscription.p:
Stones lost at the right end. This registers an agreement given by the Muppa-
duvaṭṭattu-Śivabrāhmaṇas (priests) of the temple of Rājēndrachōḷīśvaram-uḍaiya-
Nāyanār at Ālaṅguḷam in Mēneḍuṅgaḷa-nāḍu to burn four lamps in different shrines
for the amount of 40 paṇam received by them from Tāraṉ Uyyavandāṉ
Kāḍuveṭṭi of Podiyil-Puttūr in Śīvala-vaḷanāḍu.

edition.l:
svastiśrī [||*] komāṟapaṉmarāṉa tiripuvaṉaccakravarttikaḷ emmaṇṭalamuṅ
koṇṭaruḷiya śrīkulacekaradēvaṟku yāṇṭu 26- vatu meṉeṭuṅkaḷa
nāṭṭu ālaṅkuḷamāṉa teciya•••• virapaṭṭiṉattu u-

[pakka[l*]paṟṟiṉa

koyilukkuḷḷu puku•••• vārkaḷ tiruviḷakku 4- m

inscription.head:
No. 410.
(A.R. No. 410 of 1907.)
Periyakuḷam, Periyakulam Taluk, Madurai District
Rājēndrachōḷīśvara temple—on the same wall
inscription.p:
The inscription is built in at the end. It is dated in the cyclic year Virōdhikṛit,
Tai 10, and mentions a certain Immaḍi Vayiru-Nāyakkar with the biruda ‘Maṟai
pukkār-kāvalaṉ’ who seems to have been a devotee of Sēṉāpatidēvar and Rājendra-
chōḷīśvaram-uḍaiya-Nāyanār at Periyakuḷam in Neḍuṅgaḷa-nāḍu. Mention is also
made of a Śivabrāhmaṇa of the temple of Tiruvaraṅguḷa-Nāyinār in Virudarājabhayaṅ-
kara-vaḷanāḍu.

l.ta:
naṭuka māmaḻai maṉṉuka meyvirumpiya vaṉpā viḷaṅku
caivvanaṉ neṟitāṉ taḻaittoṅkuka teyva veṇtiruṉiṟu
ciṟakkave [||*]

tiyati neṭuṅkuḷanāṭṭu pe[ri]yakuḷattu
nāyiṉār irāce[ntira]coḷicuramuṭaiya nāyiṉār āṟumukapperumāḷ
tevarc ceṉ[ā*]pati tevar ciṟaimappirāṉ

naṭaikoṭṭairāyaṉ maṟaipukkār kāvalan immaṭi vayiru nāyakkar
virutuirācapayaṅkaravaḷanāṭṭu nāyiṉār tiruvaraṅkuḷa nāyiṉārkoyi
lil pārattuvāca kottaṟattil

inscription.head:
No. 411.
(A.R. No. 411 of 1907.)
Periyakuḷam, Periyakulam Taluk, Madurai District
Rājēndrachōḷīśvara temple—on the south wall
of the Subrahmaṇya shrine
Māṟ. Sundara-Pāṇḍya: year 15
inscription.p: This is a mere fragment. It mentions Ālaṅguḷam and Padiṉeṇvishaya-Īśvara.

edition.l: yāṇṭu 15- vatu aruḷicceyal••••••

inscription.head:
No. 412.
(A.R. No. 412 of 1907.)
Periyakuḷam, Periyakulam Taluk, Madurai District
Rājēndrachōḷīśvara temple—on the west wall
of the Subrahmaṇya shrine
Māṟ. Sundara-Pāṇḍya: year 15
inscription.p:
This is also a fragment stopping with the mention of Padiṉeṇvishaya-Īśvara in
Neḍuṅguḷa-nāḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 413.
(A.R. No. 413 of 1907.)
Periyakuḷam, Periyakulam Taluk, Madurai District
Rājēndrachōḷīśvara temple—on a stone built into the floor
of the ardha-maṇḍapa in the Subrahmaṇya shrine
Māṟ. [Sundara-Pāṇḍya]: year [1]7+1+1
inscription.p:
Like No. 416 below this seems to be an inscription of Sundara-Pāṇḍya of acc.
A.D. 1216, and of the same regnal year. This is only fragmentary and gives no
information beyond mention of Dēśiyaṟiya Erivīrapaṭṭiṉam in which this temple
was situated.

inscription.head:
No. 414.
(A.R. No. 414 of 1907.)
Periyakuḷam, Periyakulam Taluk, Madurai District
Rājēndrachōḷīśvara temple—on stones built into
the floor of the court-yard
Māṟ. Sundara-Pāṇḍya: year [17]+1+1
inscription.p:
This is also fragmentary and mentions a maṭha situated in Dēśiyaṟiya Eṟivīra
paṭṭiṉam.

l.ta: māṉa teciyaṟiya eṟivirapaṭṭiṉattu melai maṭamāṉa tiruva••••••

inscription.head:
No. 415.
(A.R. No. 415 of 1907.)
Periyakuḷam, Periyakulam Taluk, Madurai District
Kailāsanātha temple on the hill—on a stone built into the west wall
inscription.p:
This seems to mark a lineal measure of a cubit as decided by Peṟṟāṉ Śokkaṉ,
probably a leader of the Teṟkil-nilai-Maṟavar Community.

inscription.head:
No. 416.
(A.R. No. 416 of 1907.)
Periyakuḷam, Periyakulam Taluk, Madurai District
Kailāsanātha temple—on the west, south and north bases
Māṟ. Sundara-Pāṇḍya I: year 17+1+1: 1234-35 A.D.
inscription.p:
This begins with a long introduction Pūmaruviya Tirumaḍandaiyum etc. giving
a detailed account of the king's triumphs against the Chōḷa and also others. He is
stated to have celebrated the anointment of heroes at Muḍigoṇḍaśōḻapuram (Jayaṅ-
goṇḍaśōḻapuram) after conquering the Chōḷa country. The inscription registers a gift
of 7(1/2) mā of land to the south of Ālaṅguḷam inclusive of kaḍamai to the temple of
Uyyavanda-Īśvaramuḍaiya-Nāyaṉār on the hill at Vēḻkularāmanallūr in Mēneḍuṅgaḷa-
nāḍu, on the representation to the king made by the authorities of the temple of
Nāyaṉār at Eṟipaḍainallūr while the former was camping at Ālaṅguḷam. Land was
also granted by order of the king for the formation of a tirumaḍaiviḷāgam round the
temple, so that the rents maṉaiyiṟai, kuḍimai etc. from the houses thereon could be
utilised for burning the lights in the temple. The order is signed at the end by (the
officers) Duvārapati Vēḷāṉ and Duvārapati-Brahmādhirājaṉ.

l.ta:
pūmaruviya tirumaṭantaiyum puvimaṭantaiyum puyattiruppa
nāmaruviya kalaimaṭantaiyuñ ceyamaṭantaiyu[m*] nalañciṟappa koḷānta
ciṉappuliyuṅ koṭuñcilaiyuṅ kulaintoḷippa vāḷānta po-

muvakait tamiḻum muṟaimaiyil viḷaṅka nālvakai vetamu-

camaiyamu maḻakuṭaṉ tikaḻa veḻuvakaip pāṭalu miyaluṭaṉ parava veṇṭi
caiyaḷavuñ cakkarañ cellak koṅkaṇar kaliṅkar kocalar mā-

cempiyar pallavar mutaliyar pāṟtivarelā muṟaiviṭa maruḷeṉ

ṟoruvar muṉṉoruvar muṟaimuṟai kaṭava tiṟaikuṇantiṟaiñca ilaṅ
koḷi maṇimu-

tāmarait ticaimukaṉ paṭaitta maṉuneṟi taḻaippa maṇimuṭicūṭip
poṉṉicūḻ nāṭṭiṟ puliyāṇai poyakalak kaṉṉicūḻ nāṭṭiṟ kayalā-

ṇai kaiyvaḷara veñciṉa vivuḷiyum veḻamum parappit taiñcaiyu muṟain
taiyuñ centaḻal koḷuttik kāviyu nilamu niṉṟu kavi niḻaṟṟa v[ā]vi
yum [ci]ṟumaṇi nirnalaṉaḻittuk kūṭamum māmatuḷuṅ kopuramu māṭara-

ṅku māṭamu māḷikaiyu maṇṭapaṅkaḷ pala viṭittut toḻutu vantaṭaiyā
nirupartantevimā raḻutakaṇṇinirāṟu parappi [ka]ḻutai koṭuḻutu kavaṭi
viccic cempiyaṉaic ciramariyapporutu curam pukavoṭṭip paim-

poṉ maṇimuṭi paṟittup pāṇaṉukkuk kuṭuttaruḷip pāṭaruñ ciṟappiṟ
parutivāṉṟoyāṭakap puricai yāyirattaḷiyiṟ coravaḷavaṉ a

ṣekañ ce[y*]tu pukaḻ virittu nāḷum pararācamu
nāmatta

lai piṭiṅkiyiḷūn taṟukaṇ matavāḷai melkoṇṭu nirāḻivaiyamuḻutum
potuvaḻittuk kūrāḻiyuñ ceyyatoḷu meykoṇṭu poyyap
paṭāta arumaṟaiyor [t]entuṇar vāḻ teyvap puliyū-

ṟ tiruvellaiyiṟ pukuntu poṉṉampalam poliya varavor pūvaiyuṭaṉ
maṉṉun tirumeṉi kaṇṭu maṉaṅkaḷittuk kolamalar melayaṉuṅ kuḷir
tuḻāymālu maṟiyā malaṟ cevaṭi vaṇaṅ-

ki vāṅku ciṟakaṉṉan tuyileḻuppa vaṇṭeḻum pūmpūṅkayal vāvicūḻ
poṉṉamarāpatiyi lottulakan tāṅku muyar meruvaik kuṇantu
vaittaṉaiya cotimaṇimaṇṭapattiru-

ṉurimaiyoṭum pukuntu peṟṟa putalvaṉai niṉpereṉ••••ṭṭi veṟṟiyaṭi
yiṇaikkiḻ viḻuntu toḻutirantu tāṉoṭi muṉceṉṟa vemmai
yelāṅ kaiyyakalat tāṉo-

piṭipāṭākavey poṅku tirai ñālattup pūpālar toḷ viḷaṅkuñ
ceṅkaya•• koṇṭu ṉaṟuntirumuka-

mum paṇ••••• vatiyeṉṉu nāmamunto••••••••• ruḷi [te]kkaṭaṟ pārnila
ventar••• kkuṟṟaṅketaṅkaḷ tiṟakuṅkapaḷḷi••••keṉṟeṇṇi taḷai aṟṟa-

kaṇaṅkoḷ tiruttoḷmālai kaḻittel vaḻa••••aruḷiyapiṉ maṟṟoru
nāḷ pa-

ṟṟā muḻaṅ••••••••• ttineṉ kaṇaṅka••••••••• teṉkoṅkaṉ patti paṭṭu taṇ
ṭaṉukkum••• poṅkac cāttiya āparaṇa••••••••• ṭeṉa vaḻaṅki ātta pe-

ru naṇpaṉ••••ciṟaiyum••••••••• mālunāḷ••••••••• ṭavuḷeṉa māṭamaturaiyil
tāṉ ponta pavaṉiyile kūṭṭukkot•••• aṅkum viṭu koṇṭavar-

tam tollaip puvikkup piṇaṅkāman tā••••••••• icaivu tiṭṭeṟpak
koṇṭivvāṟu ceyyātoḻiyil iyamaṉukku vevvel viruntākku-

inscription.head:
No. 417.
(A.R. No. 417 of 1907.)
Periyakuḷam, Periyakulam Taluk, Madurai District
Kailāsanātha temple—on the rock to the west
inscription.p:
This is damaged and is engraved partly in Grantha and partly in Tamil. It
seems to record the gift of a land to provide for worship in the temple for the merit
of king Sundara-rāja by (his minister ?) Dvāravatīpati, called in the Tamil
portion, by the name Aḻagapperumāḷ Śokkaṉār Āṇmaiyaḻagiyāṉ alias Duvārapati-
vēḷāṉ.

inscription.head:
No. 418.
(A.R. No. 418 of 1907.)
Periyakuḷam, Periyakulam Taluk, Madurai District
Kailāsanātha temple—on the same rock
inscription.p: This is also damaged like the above and seems to record the same gift.

inscription.head:
No. 419.
(A.R. No. 419 of 1907.)
Periyakuḷam, Periyakulam Taluk, Madura District
On a slab set up in a field near the village
inscription.p:
This is dated in Śārvari year, Āḍi 19, and is written in modern characters. It
records a sarvamānya gift of land by Bālaya-Nāyakkar to help in the maintenance
of a chattram established by Saśimuka-Mīnākshi-nāyakkar in front of the temple of
Mīnākshi-Sundarēśvarasvāmi at Tiruvālavāy (Madurai).

edition.l: pāvatti-
edition.p: The following five lines are found engraved on the same stone.

inscription.head:
No. 420.
(A.R. No. 420 of 1907.)
Gūḍalūr, Dindugal Taluk, Madurai District
Aḷagiri Perumāḷ temple—on a slab set up in the premises
Kulaśēkharapperumāḷ: Śaka 1591, Kollam 844, Kīlaka,
Āvaṇi 9, Sunday śu. 12, Uttirāḍam (A.D. 1668, August 9)
inscription.p:
This registers an endowment of land at Gūḍalūr by the king for worship in the
temples of Maṅgalādēviyammaṉ, Aḻagarkōyil Vanmīkanāthasvāmi, Śāstā at Periyāṟu
with Brāhmaṇa-feeding, Śāstā at Pūṉaiyāṟu, Bhagavati at Naḍaikkal and for a feeding
house at Ettumānallūr exclusive of the land given to one Neḍumākkal Paṇikkar for
his maintenance. Towards the end of the inscription are incised the following figures:
two strung bows, a mace, a sheaf cutter, a chakra, a lamp-stand, a man with tufted
hair bending towards it and holding a kamaṇḍalu by the right hand and an umbrella
by the left.

edition.l: cakāttam 1591- kku mel cellā-

inscription.head:
No. 421.
(A.R. No. 421 of 1907.)
Kambam, Periyakulam Taluk, Madurai District
Subrahmaṇya temple—on the south wall
Jaṭ. Vīra-Pāṇḍya I: year 10: 1262-63 A.D.
inscription.p:
The king is given the distinguishing epithets ‘who took Īḻam, Koṅgu and
Śōḻamaṇḍalam, and having conquered the Vallāṉ (powerful enemy ?) performed the
anointment of heroes and victors at Tillaimānagar’. The inscription records a gift of
tax-free land (by order of the king ?) for offerings to god Śēvagattēvar Tirchcheṇḍu-
piḍitta-Piḷḷaiyār (Subrahmaṇya ?) and goddess Periyapirāṭṭiyār at Tiruppuduvūr in
Toṇḍaimāṉ-vaḷanāḍu.

edition.l: yaruḷiya śrīvirapāṇḍyadēvaṟku yāṇṭu 10- vatu āṉi mā-

l.ta: [ṉ] naṭuvaṭaiya patiṉāṟaṭik kolāl kāṭṭik kuṭutta nilam
māvukkum epperpaṭṭa iṟaiyun tavintu nālellai ka-

inscription.head:
No. 422.
(A.R. No. 422 of 1907.)
Vīrapāṇḍi, Periyakulam Taluk, Madurai District
Kaṇṇīśvara temple—on a stone lying near
Sundara-Pāṇḍya: year 11+3
inscription.p:
This is an incomplete inscription stopping with the mention of the boundaries.
It records a gift of 4 mā of land tax-free near the channel of Śivallavaṉ-pēraṇai
(dam) (by order of the king ?) to one Eṉṉaiāḷa-uḍaiyār, a disciple of Aghōradēvar
of the Āmuṇḍar-maḍam and Śrī Nandikēśvara-santāna.

edition.l: ndarapāṇṭiyatevaṟku yāṇṭu 11- vatiṉ etir 3- va
ṇaikkāṟ kiḻ maṭippiṟ iṟaiyiliyāka [ cantrā]-

l.ta: ṟpaṭi nilam nālumā innilam nālumāvuk ke-

inscription.head:
No. 423.
(A.R. No. 423 of 1907.)
Vīrapāṇḍi, Periyakulam Taluk, Madurai District
Kaṇṇīśvara temple—on a second stone in the same place
Vikrama-Pāṇḍya: year 5
inscription.p:
This states that the kaṇḍam and paṭṭigai of the maṇḍapa of the Kaṇṇuḍai-
yīchchuramuḍaiya-Nāyaṉār at Pullūr (Vīrapāṇḍi ?) was the contribution of one
Marakatapperumāḷ, a dēvaraḍiyāḷ of the temple.

edition.l: yāṇṭu 5- vatu vikkira[ma*]pā-

inscription.head:
No. 424.
(A.R. No. 424 of 1907.)
Vīrapāṇḍi, Periyakulam Taluk, Madurai District
Kaṇṇīśvara temple—on a third stone in the same place
Kṛishṇadēva-Mahārāya: Śaka 1451, [Sarva]dhāri,
Aṟpaśi 14: A.D. 1528
inscription.p:
This records the gift by three residents of Pullainallūr alias Vīrapāṇḍyanallūr
of the incomes uḷḷāyam and paṭṭaḍaippadivu accruing to them from the village
which was their sarvamānya to the temple of Kaṇṇīśvaramuḍaiya-Nayiṉār.

l.ta: itukku yātāmoruttar akitañ ceyta per keṅkai-

inscription.head:
No. 425.
(A.R. No. 425 of 1907.)
Vīrapāṇḍi, Periyakulam Taluk, Madurai District
Kaṇṇīśvara temple—on a fourth stone in the same place
Vikrama-Pāṇḍya: year 5+1
inscription.p:
This is a fragment with only the first five lines preserved. It mentions
Pulinallūr in Aḷa-nāḍu and one Toṇḍaimāṉār.

edition.l: ku yāṇṭu 5- vatiṉetirāmā-

inscription.head:
No. 426.
(A.R. No. 426 of 1907.)
Vīrapāṇḍi, Periyakulam Taluk, Madurai District
Kaṇṇīśvara temple—on the fifth stone in the same place
Vikrama-Pāṇḍya: year 5+2
inscription.p:
This is an incomplete inscription probably intended to record some gift
to the temple of Kaṇṇuḍai-Īchchuramuḍaiya-Nāyaṉār at Pullinallūr in Aḷa-nāḍu
by a certain Eḍuttapādam Bagavati-Āḻvāṉ alias Poṉmāḷigai-Kālagadē[vaṉ], a resident
of the tirumaḍaiviḷāgam of the temple of Mūlattānamuḍaiya-Nāyaṉār at
Teṉkallaga-nāḍu.

edition.l: svastiśrīḥ— aḷanāṭṭu pullinallūruṭai-
vatiṉ ṉetir 2- vatu mutal teṉkallaka nāṭ-
tiyāḻvāṉāṉa poṉmāḷikaik kālakate Stops here.-

inscription.head:
No. 427.
(A.R. No. 427 of 1907.)
Vīrapāṇḍi, Periyakulam Taluk, Madurai District
Kaṇṇīśvara temple—on a sixth stone in the same place
Sundara-Pāṇḍya: year 13: 1228-29 A.D.
inscription.p:
This is an inscription of Māṟavarman Sundara-Pāṇḍya I ‘who was pleased to
present the Chōḷa country’. It states that the doorway of the ardha-maṇḍapa
of this temple was the gift of a Kaikkōḷa resident of the place by name Śelvaṉ
Tirunāvuḍaiyāṉ alias Tirujñānasambandaṉ.

edition.l: rapāṇṭiyatevaṟku yāṇṭu 13- tu ci

inscription.head:
No. 428.
(A.R. No. 428 of 1907.)
Śiṉṉamaṉūr, Periyakulam Taluk, Madurai District
Rājasiṁhēśvara temple—on the south wall
of the central shrine
Māṟ. Kulaśēkhara I: year 3: 1270-71 A.D.
inscription.p:
The king is given the qualifying epithet ‘who took every country’. The
details of the date Rishabha, śu. 4, Thursday, Punarvasu, gives the English
equivalent A.D. 1270, pril 24. This registers the sale of a mā of land by the
mahāsabhā of Arikēsarinallūr in Aḷa-nāḍu and two other bodies called the paṇṇai-
śeyvār and vēli-śeyvār of the place to a certain Mahādēvaṉ Periyanāyaṉ alias
Vaḻudinārāyaṇaṉ of Mālaṅguḍi in Śembi-nāḍu on behalf of the temple of Rāja
siṁhēśvara for being made over to a dēvaraḍiyāḷ as jīvita.

edition.l:
svastiśrī [||*] komāṟapaṉmarāṉa [tribhu] vaṉacakravattikaḷ emmaṇṭalamuṅ
koṇṭaruḷiya śrīkulaicekaradēvaṟku yāṇṭu [3]- vatu ṛṣabhanāyaṟṟu

hēśvaramuṭaiya nāyaṉār koyilil āticaṇḍēśvaradēvaṟku ivvūr mahā

inscription.head:
No. 429.
(A.R. No. 429 of 1907.)
Śiṉṉamaṉūr, Periyakulam Taluk, Madurai District
Rājasiṁhēśvara temple—on the same wall
Māṟ. Kulaśēkhara I: year 41: 1308-09 A.D.
inscription.p:
The inscription is mutilated. It refers to the 10th year of Sundara-
Pāṇḍyadēva. It seems to register a gift of some impost on articles sold by them
in the bazaars at Arikēsarinallūr and at Śīvallavan-Paḍaivīḍu for meeting the
expenses of worship and festival in Mārgaḻi month in the temple of
Rājaśiṅgēśuramuḍaiya-Nāyiṉār, by the (mercantile) bodies Padiṉeṇvishayattār, the
Seṭṭis of Chōṇāḍu and others.

svastiśrī [||*] komāṟapaṉmarāṉa tribhu[ vaṉaccakravattikaḷ] emmaṇṭalamuṅ
koṇṭaruḷiya kulaicekarateva[ṟ*]ku yāṇṭu 41- vatu āṉi mācam
patiṉaiñcām tiyati uṭaiyār irācciṅkecuramuṭaiya nāyiṉāṟku tiru

inscription.head:
No. 430.
(A.R. No. 430 of 1907.)
Śiṉṉamaṉūr, Periyakulam Taluk, Madurai District
Rājasiṁhēśvara temple—on the same wall
Jaṭ. Vīra-Pāṇḍya (I): year 26: 1278-79 A.D. (?)
inscription.p:
This seems to record a similar gift of an impost on certain articles of
merchandise like betel-nut, pepper and rice agreed to be contributed by the members
of the community Padiṉeṇvishayattār of the four nagaram for a festival in Mārgaḻi
in the temple of Rājasiṁhēśvaramuḍaiya-Nāyaṉār at Arikēsarinallūr in Aḷa-nāḍu.
Mention is also made of a bazaar to the east of Valaṅgai-mīkāmaṉ-tirumaṇḍapam
built by (an officer) Pāṇḍiyadaraiyar in the name of Piḷḷai Kulaśēkhara-Mahābali-
Vāṇarāyar. The members are stated to have met for their deliberation in the
temple of Vikramapāṇḍīśvaramuḍaiya-Nāyaṉār at Śīvallavaṉ-paḍaivīḍu.

edition.l:
svastiśrīḥ— koccaṭaipaṉmarāṉa tribhuvaṉaccakravattikaḷ śrīvirapāṇṭiya
tevaṟkku yāṇṭu 26- vatu aḷanāṭṭu arikecarinallūr uṭaiyār
śrī rājasimhiśvaramuṭaiya nāyināṟku nālu nakarap patiṉeṇviṣayat
tārum civallavaṉ paṭaiviṭṭil vikramapāṇṭiśvaramuṭaiya nāyiṉār
ko••••••••• ṟakkūṭi tiruppaṇikku tirukkaimalaril nirvāttuk kaṭṭit
tanta muṉṉāḷ yeṭuttup potu••••••••• ppākkup potikku orumāp
paṇamum miḷaku potikku iraṇṭu māppaṇamum melait tiruvācalil
patiṉeṇ viṣayak kopurattukku uttamacoḻapurattil tuṟaiyil
eṭukkum paṭṭaṇappakuti aricip potikku oru māppaṇamum pākkup
potikku orumāppaṇamum miḷaku potikku iraṇṭu māppaṇamum
patiṉeṇviṣayattār eḻuntaruḷapaṇṇip potukiṟa mārkaḻit tiru
nāḷukku nāṭṭil paṇṭacālaikaḷil koḷḷumavai kuṭukkumavaiyiṟṟu
kkup paṇam oṉṟukku araikkāṇiyāka vanta paṇam eṭuttuppotavum

kkum pākku[p potikku a]raimā paṇamum miḷaku potikku orumāp
paṇamum āka ivvakaippaṭi

cellak kalveṭṭik kuṭut
tom nālu nakarap patiṉeṇviṣa••••••••• ivvaṉaivom ivarkaḷ
icaiṉtapaṭikku patiṉ[e]ṇviṣeyakaṇa[k*]ku te[ṉ*]ṉavataraiyaṉ
eḻu[t*]tu [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 431.
(A.R. No. 431 of 1907.)
Śiṉṉamaṉūr, Periyakulam Taluk, Madurai District
Rājasiṁhēśvara temple—on the north wall
of the central shrine
Māṟ. Kulaśēkhara I: year 41: 1309 A.D.
inscription.p:
The king is given the title ‘who was pleased to take every country’. The details
of date Āṉi 14, śu. 1, Punarvasu, Monday would give the English equivalent
A.D. 1309, June 9.

It is left incomplete with the mention of the fact that the Nālunagaram
Padiṉeṇvishayattār, the Padineṭṭurājya-Padiṉeṇvishayattār, the brokers, the
Nāṭṭuchcheṭṭis and Taḷachcheṭṭis all assembled together in the temple of Vikrama-
pāṇḍīśvaramuḍaiya-Nāyaṉār (also mentioned in No. 430 above) for conducting
some business evidently in connection with a gift to the temple of Tirupūlānduṟai-
Uḍaiyār alias Rājasiṁha[chōḷī]śuram-Uḍaiyār at Arikēsarinallūr in Aḷa-nāḍu.

edition.l:
yemmaṇṭalamu[ṅ] koṇṭaruḷiya śrīkulajekaradēvaṟku yāṇṭu 41
- vatu āṉi māta 14tiyati pūrvapakṣattu prathamaiyum puṇar-

l.ta:
pūcamum peṟṟa tiṅkaḷkiḻamai nāḷ aḷanāṭṭu arikecari nallūr uṭai
yār tiruppūlāntuṟai (uṟai) uṭaiyārāṉa

ṟku nālunakaram patiṉeṇvi[ṣaya]ttomum patiṉeṭṭirācciyattiṟ
patiṉeṇviṣayattomum tarakaro[mu*]m nāṭṭuc ceṭṭikaḷomum
taḷacceṭṭikaḷomum

muṭaiya nāyaṉār tiruma••••til niṟaivaṟa niṟai
ntu••••••••• niccayitta kāriyamāvatu uṭaiyār tiruppūlāntu

inscription.head:
No. 432.
(A.R. No. 432 of 1907.)
Śiṉṉamaṉūr, Periyakulam Taluk, Madurai District
Rājasiṁhēśvara temple—on the same wall
Māṟ. Kulaśēkhara I: year 26: 1293-94 A.D.
inscription.p:
The king is given the same title as in No. 431 above. The inscription
registers the assignment of 21 mā of land in one block by the māhāsabhā, the
paṇṇai-śeyvār and the vēli-śeyvār of Arikēsarinallūr and its entry in the tax-register
(oḻugu) as dēvadāna under the ūr in favour of the temple of Rājasiṁhēśvaram-
Uḍaiyār of the place in exchange (parikshētra) for three separate plots of dēvadāna
land, one measuring 18 mā to the east of the stream called the Śuruḷiyāṟu, 2 mā
belonging to goddess Durgā and 1 mā which had been sold already for the formation
of tirumaḍai-viḷāgam. Arikēsarinallūr is referred to as a brahmadēya in Aḷa-nāḍu.

edition.l:
svastiśrī [||*] tiruvāy keḷvikkumel śrīkomāṟapaṉmarāṉa tribhu[va*]ṉa
cakravattikaḷ śrī e[m*]maṇṭalamu[m*]koṇṭaruḷiya śrīkulacekaradē
vaṟku yāṇṭu [2]6- vatu vaikāci mātam aḷanāṭṭu brahmadēśam
śrīarikecarinallūr mahāsabhaiyum paṇṇaiceyvārkaḷum velicey
vārkaḷum ivūr rājasi[m*]hēśvara[m*]

l.ta:
[ṟ*]ku kalveṭṭi kuṭutta paricāvatu
tenpiṭākai aravārmaṅkalattiniṉṟum ivūṟpaṟṟu vaṭatalai aṟuti
āka cūruḷi āṟṟukku kiḻakku uḷḷa tevatāṉam tūṇinila ākaiyāl
iṉnila oḻukiṉpaṭi patiṉeṭṭaṭi nālviral kolāl nilam patiṉ
eṭṭu māvum

vatāṉam nilam iraṇṭu māvum tirumaṭa
viḷākattukku viṟṟa nilam orumāvum āka nilam irupattorumāvum
ūṟpaṟṟāka oḻukil māṭṭeṟintu pari

ha kalveṭṭi kuṭutta
nilam irupattorumāvukkum perunāṉkellai āvatu erikkiḻ ciṟu
maṭaipokkil kuṭutta

nilattukku perunāṉkellai āvatu kiḻ ellai kuḷakkaraikku meṟ[kum]
teṉṉellai teṅkam tiruvitikku vaṭakkum melellai oḷḷattukku
kiḻakkum vaṭaellai paṉaiyoṭu cey vaṭavarampil niṉṟum meṟku
nokkippoṉa vāykkālukku teṟkum āka icainta peruṉāṉ
kellaikkuṭpaṭṭa

yum paṇṇai ceyvārkaḷum veliceyvār
kaḷum ippaṭikku tirupputtūr moci tirumāliruñcolai nārāyaṇa

ippaṭikku mokiliyaṉ cikaṇṭaṉ tiruppūlāntuṟaiyuṭaiyāṉāṉa tiru
vallavāḷ nampi eḻuttu ivai niṉṟa nārāyaṇaṉ āḷkoṇṭa
piḷḷai eḻuttu ivai cikaṇṭaṉ mātavappiḷḷai eḻuttu ivai cika
ṇṭaṉ uyyakkoṇṭāṉ

inscription.head:
No. 433.
(A.R. No. 433 of 1907.)
Śiṉṉamaṉūr, Periyakulam Taluk, Madurai District
Rājasiṁhēśvara temple—on the same wall
Jaṭ. Sundara-Pāṇḍya: year 6
inscription.p:
This records the gift of the proceeds of imposts levied on certain export
articles of merchandise like yarn, cloth, arecanut, pepper, turmeric and ginger, by
the padiṉeṇvishayattār of Nālunagaram and the taragar (brokers) assembled in the
tiruvōlakka-maṇḍapam of the temple of Tiruppūlānduṟai-uḍaiya-Nāyaṉār to meet the
requirements of the śā tuppaḍi and tirupparivaṭṭam (apparel) of the deity.

edition.l:
śrīkoṟcaṭaipaṉmarāṉa tribhuvaṉaccakravattikaḷ śrīsundarapāṇṭiyate
vaṟku yāṇṭu 6- vatu mārkaḻimātam 15 tiyati uṭaiyār tiruppūlāntuṟai
yuṭaiyaṉāyaṉār koyilil tiruvolakka maṇṭapattil nālu nakarattu
patiṉeṇ••••••••• m tarakarum ṉiṟaivaṟaṉiṟaiyntu kuṟaivaṟak kūṭiyiru
ntu iṉṉāyanāṟkku cāttuppaṭikkum tirupparivaṭṭattukkum tiruk
kaimalaril nirvārttuk kaṭṭikkuṭuttu eṭukkumpaṭi kiḻaivaṭamaiyil
patiṉeṇviṣayattārk kiḻakku•••••

l.ta:
kuyoru māppaṇamum ṉūlpiṭavai••••••••• vukku yoru kācum kiḻakkup
po[ku*]m pākkum miḷaku mañcaḷ cukku uḷḷiṭṭa mārppukku pata
kku[k*]ku melpaṭṭa mā•••• yoru kācum āka yivvakaippaṭi ca

vaṟ cella tirumalaiyi••••••• mpilum veṭṭik koḷvatāka tirukkai
malaril nirvārttuk kuṭuttom nālu nakarattup patiṉeṇviṣayat
to[mu]m tarakarum ivvaṉaivarumñ colla yippiṭipāṭu eḻutiṉa
maikku patiṉeṇviṣayakkaṇakku tirukkaivel•••••••

inscription.head:
No. 434.
(A.R. No. 434 of 1907.)
Śiṉṉamaṉūr, Periyakulam Taluk, Madurai District
Rājasiṁhēśvara temple—on the same wall
Jaṭ. Sundara-Pāṇḍya: year 7
inscription.p:
The details of date are given as Makara, śu. 3, Friday, Mṛigaśīrsha which
are however found to be irregular if we take the record to be assignable to Sundara
Pāṇḍya of acc. A.D. 1276.

The inscription records another gift of contribution by toll by the same
mercantile bodies mentioned above for the maintenance of a flower-garden called
Rājākkaṇāyaṉ-tirunandavaṉam for the temple. The donors are said to have met
in the temple mentioned in No. 430 above.

edition.l:
svastiśrīḥ— koṟcaṭapaṉmarāṉa tribhuvaṉaccakravattikaḷ śrīsundarapāṇṭiya
tevaṟku yāṇṭu 7- vatu makaranāyaṟṟu pūrvapakṣattu muṉṟāntiyati
yum veḷḷikkiḻamaiyum peṟṟa mṛgaśiriṣattu nāḷ kiḻaivaṭṭai
patiṉeṇmiṣaiyattomum nālunakarap patiṉeṇmiṣaiyattomum
tarakaromum civallavaṉ paṭaiviṭṭil uṭaiyār vikramapāṇṭiśvara
muṭai-

l.ta:
yanāyiṉār koyilil niṟaivaṟa niṟaintu kuṟaivaṟak kūṭiyiruntu uṭai
yār tiruppūlāntuṟai uṭaiya nāyiṉāṟku tirunantavaṉamāṉatu tirup
pūlāntuṟaik kuḷakkiḻ kalmaṭaikku meṟkil āṭkoṇṭatevar
taṉmamāṉa irācākkaṇāyaṉ tirunantavaṉattukku tirunantavaṉappuṟamāka
kalveṭṭik kuṭutta paṭṭaṇappakuti kiḻakku vaṭakku eṟṟuppokiṟa pāk
kup poti oṉṟukku

araimāppaṇamum miḷaku poti oṉṟukku orumāppaṇamum ivvāṇṭu
mutal koṇṭu yittirunantavaṉañ ceyvatākavum ippaṭi cammatittu
uṭaiyār tiruppūlāntuṟai uṭaiya nāyiṉār koyilil kal veṭṭi

[yattār*] paṇikka pākaṉūr••••
vallavapurattu ciṟṟampūr kiḻavaṉ tirumukkūṭalāṇṭāṉ kūttaṉ pati
ṉeṇ•••••••••

inscription.head:
No. 435.
(A.R. No. 435 of 1907.)
Śiṉṉamaṉūr, Periyakulam Taluk, Madurai District
Rājasiṁhēśvara temple—on the south wall
of the shrine of goddess Śivakāmi
Māṟ. Kulaśēkhara I: year 3: 1270-71 A.D.
inscription.p:
This is a damaged and disconnected record of four different pieces, which
seems to register an endowment of 5 mā of land to provide for the expenses of a
service called the Tirunīlakantan-śandi in the hrine of Tirukkāmakkōṭṭam Periyanāch-
chiyār in the temple. Two persons are mentioned in connection with this gift evidently
as donors viz. Tirukkaivēlammaidēvaṉ and his brother Tirunelvēli-uḍaiyāṉ of
[Śīval]lavaṉ-paḍaivīḍu.

edition.l:
śrī komāṟapaṉmarāṉa tiripuvaṉaccakkaravattikaḷ emmaṇṭalamum
koṇṭaruḷiya śrī kulace[karatevaṟku yāṇṭu]3- vatu aḷanāṭṭu
brammatecam

muṭaiya nāyaṉār koyil
śrī[uruttira] śrīmāhe••••••••• lavaṉ paṭaiviṭṭut tirukkaivel a-

mmait tevaṉukkum ivaṉ tampi tirunelveli uṭaiyāṉukkum••••••

nūṟṟaimpatiṉ kala minnellu nūṟṟaimpati[nka]lattu••••••••• coṟu••••
rakkālāle

aḷantu taruvārāka innellu tirunilakaṇṭaṉ cantikku aḷakkum arici
patak••• ḻiyum nācciyār amutuceytu tecāntirikkuk kuṭu-

kkum tirumuṉṉoṭukku nālukku arici nāṉāḻi kuṭuppatākavum••••• ivai

inscription.head:
No. 436.
(A.R. No. 436 of 1907.)
Śiṉṉamaṉūr, Periyakulam Taluk, Madurai District
Rājasiṁhēśvara temple—on stones built into
the walls of the maṇḍapa in front
[Māṟ.]. Sundara-Pāṇḍya I: year 10: 1225-26 A.D.
inscription.p:
This is fragmentary containing only a part of the introduction of the king and
of the subject matter. The latter seems to be a gift of land as dēvadāṉa to the
goddess Tiruppaḷḷiyaṟai-Nāchchiyār Śāntāṇḍār after celebrating thē kalyāṇōtsava
by Muduguḍināṭṭuvēḷāṉ Irāśiṅgan of Pāṇḍimaṇḍalam.

l.ta:
••••••••• ṟattu po[ṉ*]ṉivaḷavaṉ urimaiyuṭaṉ tāṉ pukuntu peṟṟa
putalvaṉai niṉpeṟeṉṟuṭaṉ kāṭṭi veṟṟi aravaṇaikkiḻ viḻuntu
toḻutu irappatā••••••

••••••••• ṅki viṭaikuṭuttu viṭṭaruḷi vākaikatirvel vaṭaventartam
pātamekat taḻaiyaṇiya virakkaḻal paṇintu viḷa[ṅ*]kiya maṇimuṭi
vira

edition.l:
••••••••• tiribhuvaṉaccakkaravattikaḷ śrī coṇāṭu vaḻaṅkiyaruḷiya cuntara
pāṇṭiyatevaṟku yāṇṭu 10- vatu ci pāṇṭimaṇṭalattu mutukuṭi
nāṭṭuveḷāṉ irāciṅkan•••••••

l.ta:
yum nāyaṟṟukkiḻamaiyum peṟṟa atta[t]ti nāḷ tiruppaḷḷi
yaṟai nācciyār cāntāṇṭāṟait tirukkalliyāṇam paṇṇiviccut
tevatāṉamākak kuṭuttut tiruccūla•••••••••

kolāl tevatāṉam nila•••••••••

edition.p:
The following six lines, in slightly different characters are found in the
intervening slab.

inscription.head:
No. 437.
(A.R. No. 437 of 1907.)
Śiṉṉamaṉūr, Periyakulam Taluk, Madurai District
Lakshmīnārāyaṇa temple—on the south wall
Tribh. Parākrama-Pāṇḍya: year 15
inscription.p:
The king might possibly be Jaṭāvarman Parākrama I of acc. A.D. 1315. The
inscription records a gift of 120 kāśu left with three temple servants for burning a
perpetual lamp in the temple of Naḍuviṟkōyil-Perumāḷ at Arikēsarinallūr by a certain
Mōchcharāmaṉ Aṉantanārāyaṇaṉ of Tirupputtūr.

edition.l:
svastiśrī || tribhuvaṉaccakkravarttikaḷ śrīparākkiramapāṇṭiyadēvaṟku
yāṇṭu 15- vatu śrīpāṇṭimaṇṭalattu aḷanāṭṭu brahmadēyam
arikesarinallūr naṭuviṟkoyilp perumāḷukku ivvūr tirupputtūr
rukantu••• kiḻār makaṉ moccarāmaṉ aṉantanārāyaṇaṉ

iṭṭa tirunontāviḷakku 1 oṉṟukku iṭṭa ku [kācu] 120 nūṟṟirupatum
ikkoyil tiruvaṭippiṭikkum ātrēyaṉ dāśarati periyāḷ[vā]ṉum
ivaṉ tampi dēva[ dēvaṉ maka] ṉ ka[ri]māṇikkamum nārāyaṇaṉ ciriḷa[ṅ]
kovum immuvomum ipporuḷ kaik-

koṇṭu nittamum [ tiruviḷakku ca] ntrādittavaṟ erippomāka kal-
veṭṭik kuṭuttom ||—ittiruviḷakku erikka iṭṭa tirunilai—

l.ta:
viḷakku tulāttirupa••• nārāca••••||—ivarkaḷ••••• cayya i[kka]l
ve[ṭ*]ṭu eḷutiṉeṉ ivvūr maṅka[lanakaram]

inscription.head:
No. 438.
(A.R. No. 438 of 1907.)
Śiṉṉamaṉūr, Periyakulam Taluk, Madurai District
Lakshmīnārāyaṇa temple—on the same wall
inscription.p: Published in S.I.I. Vol. XIV, No. 54, foot note.

inscription.head:
No. 439.
(A.R. No. 439 of 1907.)
Śiṉṉamaṉūr, Periyakulam Taluk, Madurai District
Lakshmīnārāyaṇa temple—on the west wall
Rājēndra-Chōḷa I: year 5: 1016-17 A.D.
inscription.p:
This begins with the introduction Tirumaṉṉivaḷara etc., which stops with the
mention of Maṇṇaikkaḍakkam. This registers the sale of some land by the sabhā
of three places viz. Arikēsarinallūr. Arapadaśēkaramaṅgalam and Mandaragaura-
vamaṅgalam to Śōlai Appi of Perumaṅgalam for 15 kāśu, (for his donating the
same) for burning a perpetual lamp in the temple of Durgā-Paramēśvari. The land
is said to have belonged to them in common. Aḷanāḍu is said to be a division of
Rājarājamaṇḍalam.

edition.l:
svastiśrī ||— tirumaṉṉi vaḷara irunila maṭantaiyum śrītaṉiccelviyum poṟ
ceyappāvaiyuntaṉ peruntēviyarāki iṉpuṟa neṭintuvaru ūḻiyuḷ
iṭaituṟaināṭu ntuṭarvaṉavelip paṭar va-

l.ta:
ṉavāciyum cuḷḷiccūḻ matiṭ koḷḷippākkaiyum naṇṇaṟkarumuraṇ
maṇṇaikkaṭakkamum māpperu

kopurattuk kūṭṭakkuṟaiviṉṟik kūṭiyiruntu iññāṭṭu perumaṅkalattu
••• colliyāṉa colaiappi nammūr tuṟkāparame

kecavavayakkal taṭi oṉṟu a[ḷa]vupaṭi nilaṉ mākāṇiyum viṟṟukkuṭut
tom ivvūrkaḷil

inscription.head:
No. 440.
(A.R. No. 440 of 1907.)
Śiṉṉamaṉūr, Periyakulam Taluk, Madurai District
Lakshmīnārāyaṇa temple—on the same wall
Śaḍaiyamāṟan: year 46
inscription.p: Published in S.I.I., Vol. XIV, No. 78.

inscription.head:
No. 441.
(A.R. No. 441 of 1907.)
Śiṉṉamaṉūr, Periyakulam Taluk, Madurai District
Lakshmīnārāyaṇa temple—on the same wall
Śaḍaiyamāṟaṉ: year 2+[8]
inscription.p: Published in S.I.I., Vol. XIV, No. 54.

inscription.head:
No. 442.
(A.R. No. 442 of 1907.)
Śiṉṉamaṉūr, Periyakulam Taluk, Madurai District
Lakshmīnārāyaṇa temple—on the same wall
Tribh. Kulaśēkhara: year 9
inscription.p:
This records a gift of 50 sheep and lamp-stand by Naṅgai, daughter of
Arugan Āramōśiḷaiyavandāḷ(?)of Tirupputtūr for a perpetual lamp in the Naḍuvil-
kōyil at Arikēsarinallūr.

l.ta:
rinallūr tirupputtū rarukan
āramociḷaiyavantāḷ makaḷ naṅk

ṉ ivvūr naṭuvil koyilil iṭṭa tirunantā viḷakku oṉṟukku iṭṭa
āṭu aimpatu tuṭarviḷakku oṉṟu itu
vaṟ celvutākak kalveṭṭiṉeṉ itu śrī vai

inscription.head:
No. 443.
(A.R. No. 443 of 1907.)
Śiṉṉamaṉūr, Periyakulam Taluk, Madurai District
Lakshmīnārāyaṇa temple on the north wall
Parakēsarivarman: year 36
inscription.p: Published in S.I.I., Vol. III, No. 109.

inscription.head:
No. 444.
(A.R. No. 444 of 1907.)
Śiṉṉamaṉūr, Periyakulam Taluk, Madurai District
Lakshmīnārāyaṇa temple—on the same wall
inscription.p:
This is a fragment of an inscription dated in the 2nd year of a king whose
name is lost. It seems to record a gift of land as tiruviḍaiyāṭṭam. Mention is
made of Iraṇḍagattār. (?)

edition.l: ṇṭu 2- vatu cittirai māsattu yintacantiya kuṭṭaiyār cāla-

inscription.head:
No. 445.
(A.R. No. 445 of 1907.)
Śiṉṉamaṉūr, Periyakulam Taluk, Madurai District
Lakshmīnārāyaṇa temple—on the same wall
Jaṭ. Kulaśēkhara (I): year 3+1: 1193-94 A.D.
inscription.p:
This is a mere fragment mentioning a certain Śīvallavaṉ Tirunīlakaṇṭaṉ alias
Toṇḍaimāṉ making some gift to the mahāsabhā of Arikēsarinallūr probably on
behalf of the temple.

l.ta:
••••••••• maṉuneṟi tikaḻa aṟaneṟic camaiyaṅkaḷ••••••••• ntu miṉaṅ kaṉakā
caṉattu viṟṟiruppa

eṇkiri cūḻ••••••••• poḻil veṇkuṭai niḻaṟṟa ceṅkol naṭappa••••••
••• ccempiyar virāṭar varāṭar pallavar

tiṟaiyuṭaṉ muṟai muṟai paṇiya virunemiyaḷavu moru nemi yoṅka
viṉṉamutākiya viyalicai nāṭaka[m*] maṉṉi vaḷara maṇimuṭi cūṭi viḷaṅ
kiya maṇiyaṇi vira

edition.l:
viṟṟiruntaruḷiya māmutal matikkulam viḷakkiya komutaṟ koccaṭaiyapanta
rāṉa tribhuvaṉaccakravattikaḷ śrīkulaśēkharadēvaṟku yāṇṭu 3- āvatiṉ
etirāmāṇṭu pāṇṭima-

inscription.head:
No. 446.
(A.R. No. 446 of 1907.)
Śiṉṉamaṉūr, Periyakulam Taluk, Madurai District
Lakshmīnārāyaṇa temple—on the same wall
Tribh. Kulaśēkhara: year 11
inscription.p:
This records a gift of a perpetual lamp to the temple by one Aṇiyaṉ Appaṉ of
Aḻiśirai alias Paranirupakēsarinallūr in Aḷanāḍu.

edition.l: yāṇṭu 11- vatu pāṇṭimaṇṭa-

inscription.head:
No. 447.
(A.R. No. 447 of 1907.)
Śiṉṉamaṉūr, Periyakulam Taluk, Madurai District
Lakshmīnārāyaṇa temple—on the same wall
Śaḍaiyamāṟaṉ: year 2+7
inscription.p: Published in S.I.I., Vol. XIV, No. 52.

inscription.head:
No. 448.
(A.R. No. 448 of 1907.)
Śiṉṉamaṉur, Periyakulam Taluk, Madurai District
Lakshmīnārāyaṇa temple—on the same wall
Tribh. Kulaśēkhara: year 9
inscription.p:
This records a gift of 50 sheep for a lamp in the temple Naḍuvil-kōyil at
Arikēsarinallūr by one Āttiraiyaṉ Nakkapirāṉ Udaiyaṉ of Vaṅgimaṅgalam near
Tirupputtūr.

l.ta:
lūr tirupputtūr vaṅkimaṅkalattu āttiraiyaṉ nakkapirāṉ utaiya
ṉeṉ ivvūr naṭuvir koyilil iṭṭa ti-

runantāviḷakku oṉṟukku iṭṭa āṭu aimpatuṉāl viḷakku oṉṟum can
tirātittavaṟ celvatāka śrīvaiṣṇavarakṣai [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 449.
(A.R. No. 449 of 1907.)
Śiṉṉamaṉūr, Periyakulum Taluk, Madurai District
Lakshmīnārāyaṇa temple—on the same wall
Mār. [Sundara]-Pāṇḍya I: year 11: 1226-27 A.D.
inscription.p:
This is a damaged fragment of an inscription of Sundarapāṇḍya who
presented the Chōḷa country. It seems to record some endowment made by
Śīvallavaṉ Tirunīlakaṇṭaṉ Toṇḍaimān mentioned in No. 445 above for offerings to
god Vaṇḍuvarāpatippiḷḷai (Kṛishṇa ?) and the goddess (Pirāṭṭiyār) in the Naḍuvil-
tirumuṟṟam at Arikēsarinallūr.

edition.l:
••••••••• [p]āṇṭiya tevaṟku yāṇṭu 11- vatu pāṇṭimaṇṭalattuc civa
llavaṉ tirunila

l.ta:
••••••••• ṉ koyilil vaṇṭuvarāpatippiḷḷaikkum pirāṭṭiyāṟkum tirup
poṉaka

inscription.head:
No. 450.
(A.R. No. 450 of 1907.)
Śiṉṉamaṉūr, Periyakulam Taluk, Madurai District
Lakshmīnārāyaṇa temple—on the same wall
[Jaṭ.] Kulaśēkhara I: year 2: 1191-92 A.D.
inscription.p:
The introduction commences with the words Pūdalavaṉidai. This is damaged.
It seems to register a gift of land made by Tirunīlakaṇṭaṉ alias Toṇḍaimāṉ
mentioned above to the temple through the mahāsabhā and the Paṇṇaiśeyvār
of Arikēsarinallūr.

l.ta:
pūtalavaṉitai metakaviḷaṅka mantaramārpiṉi lintira ṉiruppa
puyalvarai taḻuviya vayamakaḷ cuḷippa mayaluṟu ciṟappiṉ māmuṉi••••viya
licai nāṭaka meḻil peṟa vaḷara viñciṉaṅ kūṟa matakaḷiṟtta veñ
ciṉaveṅkai•••• patikkaṭu paṭuttuc cakkarañcella pauvamāṉila
pārttivarpotuvaṟat teyva••••

viḷaiyāṭa veṉṟu purineñcattu iruvakaip piṟappiṉ muṉṉūl mār
piṉ nālmaṟaiyāḷar mātakavicumpiṉ vāṉavaṟkkamaitta yākave••••
toṟu muyala aṉalan kaṭakku marumai caṉṟulavum maṟuvakai ccempo
ruḷccamai••••••••• tirunilavu corinta virunivaṟaippi•••••

kaṭintu ceṅkol naṭappa veṇperuñcikara mātira veṇkoṭṭu
yeṇperuṅkaḷiṟiṉum yicaimakaḷā reṟivoṉpatu kaṇṭat•••• ven
tarum vaṉpuṭaṉ vaṇaṅki yaruntiṟaikāṭṭi yaṇitaṭa muṭimelaṭimalar••••
muṭicūṭi vaḷaṅkeḻu kavari cer-

paṭa kaṭavuḷmeṉi kaṟpputtiḻavuṭaṉ muṭicūṭi vulakam poṟṟa cerumali
tāṉaippa[ri]ppuraventar āṭakatoḷum māramum piriyā•••• makaḷirt
tilataṅkaḷ mel cevaṭivaikum vulakamutuviṉi•••• m viṟṟiruntaruḷiya
māmatikkula[m*] viḷakkiya•••••••••

puvaṉaccakkaravattikaḷ cikulacekaradēvaṟkku yāṇṭu 2- vatu aḷanāṭṭu
brahmadēyam arikecarinallūr mahāsabhaiyum paṇ••••••••• ṉa tirunila
kaṇṭaṉṉāṉa toṇ[ṭaimā]ṉ cittirai māta[t*]tu pa••••• yivvūrp
perunāṉ kellaikku••••

yiṟaiyiṟukka[kka*]ṭava nilaṅkaḷum toṭṭaṅkaḷukkum puñcaikaḷukkum
patiṉeṭṭaṭi kolāl nilam moru māvil muntirikaiyāl va•••• kku
ca

ṉam paṇṇikkuṭutteṉ yivūr••••
paṇṇaiceyvārum veliceyvārkku puṉṉūr ma•• ṭaiyāṉ tirunila
kaṇṭaṉāṉa toṇ••••••

inscription.head:
No. 451.
(A.R. No. 451 of 1907.)
Śiṉṉamaṉūr, Periyakulam Taluk, Madurai District
Lakshmīnārāyaṇa temple—on the north,
west and south walls
Jaṭ. Kulaśēkhara I: year 13+6: 1208-09 A.D.
inscription.p:
The stones are out of order and some are lost. The inscription is also damaged.
The introduction is the one commencing with the words Pūvīṉ kiḻatti. This
registers a gift of land by the same Śīvallavaṉ Tirunīlakaṇṭaṉ mentioned above who
is said to have also got it exempted from kaḍamai payment.

l.ta:
—[pū]viṉ kiḻatti mevi viṟṟiruppa metiṉi mātu nitiyiṟpuṇara va•••
pormaṭantai ceyappuyattiruppa ma•••• kkiṉil viḷaṅka ticai yirunāṉ
ku micai nilāveṟippa maṟaineṟi vaḷara maṉuṉeṟi tikaḻa aṟaneṟica
••••••• cattuva•••• toḷippa villavar cempiyar virāṭar varāṭar pal
lavar••••••• kuṭai niḻaṟṟa ceṅkol naṭappa••• ṅkali naṭuṅka ṉeṭumpilat
•••••• muṟaimuṟai paṇiya iruṉemiyaḷavu moru••• yoṅka•••• iyallicai
nāṭaka•••• maṉṉavarāy

edition.l:
—ḷaṅkiya virasiṅhāsanattu kaṟpaka niḻalil kalaivallorkiḻ maṉṉavar
teviyar vaṇaṅka meṉṉaṭai avaṉi muḻutuṭaiyāroṭum viṟṟirunta
ruḷiya māmutal matikkula[m*]viḷakkiya komutaṟ koccaṭai•••••• tribhu
vaṉaccakkaravattikaḷ śrīkulacekara•••••••• 13- vatiṉ etir•••••• kaṇṭaṉāṉa
toṇṭaimāṉār dharmmadāṉam paṇṇik•••••• dēyam arikecarinallūr
mahāsa[bhai]•••• davar śrīvallavaṉ tirunila••••••• tu idadēvaṟku yāṇṭu
muṉṟāvati[ṉe ti]rāmāṇṭu patiṉeṭṭaṭikkolāl nilam oru māvil
i-

l.ta:
—tu muntirikai nilaṅ kaṭamai tavittu niroṭaṭṭi tantu [piṉpu] patiṉ
muṉṟāvatiṉ etir āṟāmāṇṭu•• tavintaruḷiṉa patiṉeṭṭaṭik kolāl
nilamuntirikaiyum āka nilam araikkāṇi araikkāṇi•••• tavintaruḷiṉa
ikkaṭamai tavintaruḷiṉa••••• ṇikkum kaṭamai••• tirumukappaṭi kallil
veṭṭiṉeṉ ivvūr cilpāca••••••••• m puṉcaiyum uṭpaṭa muṉpilā
•••••yum

nam paṇṇi aruḷi••••• koṇṭān colaiyaraicāṉa ti•••
nāṭṭiyūr vāriyaṉ eḻuttu ||.

inscription.head:
No. 452.
(A.R. No. 452 of 1907.)
Śiṉṉamaṉūr, Periyakulam Taluk, Madurai District
Lakshmīnārāyaṇa temple—on a stone built
into the wall of the kitchen
Rājēndra-Chōḷa I: year 4
inscription.p:
This is damaged and incomplete. It mentions the sabhās of Arikēsarinallūr, a
brahmadēya in Aḷa-nāḍu of Rājarāja-maṇḍalam, Mandaragaurava-maṅgalam and
Arapadaśēkaramaṅgalam, who are stated to have met in the mukha-maṇḍapa of
Naḍuvūr-Dēvar (temple) probably in connection with some gift made to the temple
by Āttiraiyaṉ Śaṅkaran Tirumāliruñjōlai-Nārāyaṇaṉ and Nārāyaṇaṉ Śaṅkaraṉ-
Bhaṭṭan. both sons of (?) Maṅgalaṅkiḻān of Tirupputtūr, residing at this place
(Arikēsarinallūr).

edition.l: [svastiśrī] śrīrāje[ ntracoḻa]-

inscription.head:
No. 453.
(A.R. No. 453 of 1907.)
Śiṉṉamaṉūr, Periyakulam Taluk, Madurai District
Lakshmīnārāyaṇa temple—on a stone lying in front
inscription.p:
This is a Vaṭṭeḻuttu fragment mentioning Tirunaḍu[vil]-Baṭārar and Pallava-
[rai]yaṉ.

inscription.head:
No. 454.
(A.R. No. 454 of 1907.)
Śiṉṉamaṉūr, Periyakulam Taluk, Madurai District
Lakshmīnārāyaṇa temple—on a pillar lying
in the same place
inscription.p:
This is also in Vaṭṭeḻuttu and states that this pillar was set up by Arugan
Bāradvāji Nārāyaṇa-Nakkaṉ of Tirupputtūr.

inscription.head:
No. 455.
(A.R. No. 455 of 1907.)
Periyakōṭṭai, Palani Taluk, Madurai District
Chōḷēśvara temple—on the door-post of the
central shrine, left side
Vikrama-Chōḷa: year 17+3
inscription.p:
This states that this post was the gift of Muḻaiyan Vēḷāṉ alias Tamiḻadaraiyan,
a Veḷḷāḷa of Pulariyūr in Aṇḍa-nāḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 456.
(A.R. No. 456 of 1907.)
Periyakōṭṭai, Palani Taluk, Madurai District
Chōḷēśvara temple—on the door-post, right side
Vīrarājēndradēva year 15+3
inscription.p:
This is stated to be the gift of Kaṇṇapērtti alias Jayaṅgoṇḍa-gāmiṇḍan, a
veḷḷāḷa of Pulariyūr in Poṅgalūrkkā-nāḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 457.
(A.R. No. 457 of 1907.)
Periyakōṭṭai, Palani Taluk, Madurai District
Chōḷēśvara temple—on the north wall of the central shrine
Vikrama-Chōḷa: year 22
inscription.p:
This is fragmentary. It records a gift of a Śrī yakki-paḻañjalāgai-achchu to
the temple by Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻa-Śilaśeṭṭi. Close to this is another fragment (of a
Pāṇḍya king) dated in the 9th year and mentioning the temple of Māndaranīsuram-
Uḍaiyār and the village Pulariyūr.

l.ta: śrīyakki paḻañcalākai accu oṉṟuṅ kuṭutteṉ ivva••••••

edition.l: —rapāṇṭiyatevaṟku yāṇṭu 9- vatu aṇṭa

inscription.head:
No. 458.
(A.R. No. 458 of 1907.)
Periyakōṭṭai, Palani Taluk, Madurai District
Chōḷēśvara temple—on the south wall of the central shrine
inscription.p:
This is a fragment of a Koṅgu inscription with another of a Pāṇḍya king and a
third dated in the 11th year of a king (name lost), The first records the gift of a
lamp by one Pūmaṉ Araiyaṉ to the temple of Nachchiṉāṟkiṉiyār. The second
seems to provide for offerings and a lamp in the shrine of Tirukkāmakkōṭṭa.
nāchchiyār in the same temple.

edition.l: —yāṇṭu 11- vatu

inscription.head:
No. 459.
(A.R. No. 459 of 1907.)
Periyakōṭṭai, Palani Taluk, Madurai District
Chōḷēśvara temple—on the rock north of the shrine
Jaṭ. Sundara-Pāṇḍya: year 4
inscription.p:
This records a gift of 12 paṇam deposited with the Śivabrāhmaṇas of the
temple of Ariyapirāṭṭīchchuramuḍaiyār, for offerings to the god on the day of
Tīruvādirai in Mārgaḻi month and for taking out the deity in procession, by one
Nallamaṅgaibāgaṉ Piṟaiśūḍum perumāṉ, a veḷḷāḻa of Tiruppākkiyam (village ?) in
Aṇḍa-nāḍu.

edition.l: ṇṭu 4- vatu aṇṭa nāṭṭil tiruppākki-

l.ta: rapaṭṭanum ivarkaḷ eṉ pakkal vāṅkiṉa pa-
itukkuc cilavu amutukku nel mukkuṟuṇi pūcai-

inscription.head:
No. 460.
(A.R. No. 460 of 1907.)
Periyakōṭṭai, Palani Taluk, Madurai District
Chōḷēśvara temple—on the south wall
of the Bṛihadāmbā shrine
Kōnēriṉmaikoṇḍāṉ: year 9
inscription.p:
This is incomplete. This seems to record the renewal of some grant by the
king as the original document is said to have been lost during some disturbance in
the nāḍu in the 9th year. This is signed by (the officer) Ellāmvallaśōḻa-Mūvēnda-
vēāḷṇ at the end, and is addressed to the Ūrār of Kariyadēvanallūr. It refers to a
land by name Śembaṭṭaikkuḷam.

l.ta:
tiripuva[ṉa]ccakkara[va*]tti koner[iṉmai*]koṇṭāṉ kariyatevanal
lū[rū*]rāṟku nam olai kuṭuttapaṭiyāvatu cempaṭṭaikkuḷattukku aiy
yaṉ•••••••••

mukam o[ṉ]patāvatu nāḷil nāṭṭil kalakamāyk keṭukaiyil taṅkaḷukku
aiyyaṉ tanta tirumukam keṭukaiyil aiyyaṉum•••••••••

llaiyākavum ippaṭikkup piṭipāṭāka nam olai kuṭuttom ivai ellām
valla coḻa muventaveḷāṉ eḻuttu [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 461.
(A.R. No. 461 of 1907.)
Periyakōṭṭai, Palani Taluk, Madurai District
Chōḷēśvara temple—on the same wall
Rāj. Vīrarājēndradēva
inscription.p:
This is also incomplete like the above. It seems to record the installation of
the image of Chaṇḍēśvara in the temple and gift of a paḻañjalāgai-achchu for a
twilight lamp before the deity, by a Veḷḷāḷa by name Avanāśi Puḷiyaṉ.

l.ta:
llūril veḷḷāḻan cerilāril avanāci puḷiyaṉ taṇṭecurappiḷḷaiyā
rai eḻuntaruḷuvitte•••••••••

tiyacoḻicuvara paṭṭaneṉ avaṉāci puḷiyaṉ kaiyyāl koṇṭa paḻañca
lākai accu oṉṟu iv•••••••••
ṇṭecuvarap piḷḷaiyāṟku cantiyā tipam iṭuvānākavum [i*]vai pa

inscription.head:
No. 462.
(A.R. No. 462 of 1907.)
Periyakōṭṭai, Palani Taluk, Madurai District
Chōḷēśvara temple—on the same wall
Tribh. Vikrama-Chōḷa: year 8
inscription.p:
This records the gift of one Śiriyakkipaḻañjalāgaī-achchu for burning a
twilight lamp in the temple of Tiruppāṅḍēśuramuḍaiyār by a Veḷḷāḷa of Āḻvāṉkōyil
alias Vīraśōḻanallūr in the Aṇḍa-nāḍu.

l.ta:
tiripuvaṉaccakkaravattikaḷ śrīvikkiramacoḻatevaṟku tiruveḻut
tiṭṭuccellā niṉṟa tiru(n)elli yāṇṭu

- āvatu aṇṭa nāṭṭil
āḻvāṉ koyillāṉa viraco-

ḻanallūril veḷḷāḷaril collikaḷil karumpiḷḷai uttamaṉāṉa uyya
••••••• tiruppāṇṭicoramuṭaiyāṟkku cipaṇṭārattukku oṭukkiṉa
ciriyekki paḻañcalā[kai]

uṭaiyār tiruppāṇṭicoramuṭaiyāṟ koyil kāṇi uṭaiya
civappirāmaṇa [rom] mokalliya kottirattil••• yāṉa civamaṅkai
āḻvi āṉa arumarai ātittac cakkaravattiyu mavaṉ tam[pi]

civamaṅkai āḻviyum yivvaccu oṉṟukkum cantiyātipam oru viḷakku eri
ppomākavu[m] cantirātittavarai kuṭaṅk[oṇ]ṭu [ku]cci koṭupukkār
ericcuvaruvomākavum yitu

inscription.head:
No. 463.
(A.R. No. 463 of 1907.)
Periyakōṭṭai, Palani Taluk, Madurai District
Chōḷēśvara temple—on the same wall
Kōnēriṉmaikoṇḍāṉ: year 13
inscription.p:
This is fragmentary. This seems to record a royal order issued through (the
officer) Kurukulattaraiyaṉ to the residents of Kariyadēvanallūr conferring certain
social privileges on them like construction of double-storied houses, sounding of
drums on occasions auspicious and otherwise and some others the nature of which is
not clear.

l.ta:
••••••••• vatti koṉeri[ṉ*]mai koṇṭāṉ aṇṭanāṭṭu kariyateva
nallūrāṉa añcāta••••

•••••••• makku iracatarāyaṉ eṟavum taṇṭeṟavum viṭu eṭukkumiṭattu ira
ṇṭānilai eṭukkavum••••

••••••••• makku kūṭaṅkaṭṭavum acaivu iṭavu[m*] naṉmai tiṉmaikku ukac
cukkoṭṭavum i••••

edition.l:
••••••••• vai kurukulattaraiyaṉ eḻuttu yāṇṭu 13- vatu nāḷ irunūṟṟu
irupatteḻu ivai••••

inscription.head:
No. 464.
(A.R. No. 464 of 1907.)
Periyakōṭṭai, Palani Taluk, Madurai District
Chōḷēśvara temple—on the same wall
Rāj. Vīrarājēndra: year lost
inscription.p:
This is also fragmentary. It records some gift of money made to the temple
by a lady by name Mallammai, daughter of Mayipālar and wife of some chief whose
name is lost.

l.ta:
•••• ṭān maṉaikiḻatti mayirpālar makaḷ mallammaiyen aṇṭanāṭṭil āḻ
vār••••••

•••• āna aruvantic cakkaravattiyum ivan tampi [ci]vamaṅkaiyāḻviyum ko
ṇṭa ciriyak•••••••••

•••• m nāṅkaḷ cantirātittavarai celuttak kaṭavomākavu[m*] ik
koyil kuṭaṅko[ṇ*]ṭu ko•••••••••

inscription.head:
No. 465.
(A.R. No. 465 of 1907.)
Periyakōṭṭai, Palani Taluk, Madurai District
Chōḷēśvara temple—on the same wall
inscription.p:
This is also fragmentary and is damaged besides. It mentions the ūravar of
[Vīraśō]ḻanallūr and a gift of a perpetual lamp to god Nachchiṉāṟkiṉi[yār*]. It also
mentions the village Kariyadēvanallūr in Aṇḍanāḍu.

l.ta: tāviḷakku oṉṟukkum ivvāṭṭai āṭi m[āta mutal]••••

inscription.head:
No. 466.
(A.R. No. 466 of 1907.)
Periyakōṭṭai, Palani Taluk, Madurai District
Chōḷēśvara temple—on the west wall
of the Bṛihadāmbā shrine
inscription.p:
This is badly damaged. It seems to record a gift of 15 Varāhaṉ for the
several requirements of worship and offerings in the temple. The amount was
deposited with the Śivabrāhmaṇas who undertook to manage the affairs. The
donor's name is lost.

kāṇi uṭaiya civappirāmaṇaril uṭai-

inscription.head:
No. 467.
(A.R. No. 467 of 1907.)
Periyakōṭṭai, Palani Taluk, Madurai District
Chōḷēśvara temple—on the same wall
Tribh. Tribhu[vanavīra]dēva: year 3
inscription.p:
This is also much damaged. It seems to record a gift of money for a
twilight lamp in the temple by a Veḷḷāḷa.

l.ta:
tiripuva••• vattika [ḷ*] śrītiripu••••tevaṟku tiruveḻuttiṭṭu

inscription.head:
No. 468.
(A.R. No. 468 of 1907.)
Periyakōṭṭai, Palani Taluk, Madurai District
Chōḷēśvara temple—on the north wall
of the Bṛihadāmbā shrine
Tribh. Vīra-Pāṇḍya: year 5
inscription.p:
This records a gift of one achchu by a kaikkōla named Māṇikkaṉ Pāṇḍi for a
twilight lamp in the temple of Tiruppāṇḍīśvaramuḍaiyār at Āḻvāṉkōyil alias
Vīraśōḻadēvanallūr. The amount was left with the Śivabrāhmaṇas of the temple.

edition.l: [vaṟku ti]ruveḻuttiṭṭuc cellā niṉṟa tirunalliyāṇṭu 5- vatu

l.ta: lūril uṭaiyār tiruppāṇṭicuramuṭaiyār koyilil kāṇiyuṭaiya ci-

inscription.head:
No. 469.
(A.R. No. 469 of 1907.)
Periyakōṭṭai, Palani Taluk, Madurai District
Chōḷēśvara temple—on the same wall
Jaṭ. Sundara-Pāṇḍya: year 29
inscription.p:
This seems to record a gift of land after purchase for offerings during the early
morning service in the temple of Pāṇdīśuramuḍaiya-Nāyaṉār at Āḻvāṉkōil in Aṇḍa-
nāḍu by one Śrī Parañjōti Sūryadēvaṉ of Kāñaiyirukkai Śōḻaṇallūr of
Pāṇḍimaṇḍalam.

edition.l:
[ṭiya]tevaṟku yāṇṭu 29- vatu aṇṭanāṭṭu āḻvāṉkoyil veḷ
ḷāṉ ūr

ttu kāñaiyiruk

inscription.head:
No. 470.
(A.R. No. 470 of 1907.)
Periyakōṭṭai, Palani Taluk, Madurai District
Kadali-Narasimha-Perumāḷ temple—on the north,
west and south walls
inscription.p:
This is dated in the cyclic year Jaya and Chittirai 5, and introduces some chief
by name Vīrappa-Nāyakkarayyaṉ with the grandiloquent Vijayanagara titles Mahā-
maṇḍalēśvara etc., ānd claiming to have extracted tribute from every country. His
agent, Nayināttā-Nāyakkar, son of Dāsiri-Nāyakkar and grandson of Chinnappa-
Nāyakkar of Virūpāṭshi in Aṇḍa-nāḍu is said to have constructed the temple of
Kadirinātha-Perumāḷ, dug a tank and given 4 mā of land irrigated by this tank for
the upkeep of the temple, He also endowed lands for the maintenance of twelve
Brāhmaṇas (doing worship therein) and 3 other persons for other services.

l.ta:
tiyati makāmaṇṭalecuraṉ ariyataḷavipāṭaṉ pāṣekku
tappu rāyakaṇṭaṉ muvarāyakaṇṭaṉ kaṇṭaṉāṭu koṇṭu koṇṭaṉāṭu
koṭātāṉ emmaṇṭalamun tiṟaikoṇṭaruḷiya virappaṉāyakkaraiy
yaṉ kāriyattukku kaṟtarāṉa irāpuram cirmai aṇṭaṉāṭṭil virupā
ṭṣi ciṉṉappaṉāyakkar makaṉ tāciṟi ṉāyakaṉ makaṉ nayiṉāttā nāyak
kar katiriṉā[ta]p perumāḷukku koyilum muṇṭākki ko[ḻa]mum
muṇṭākkiviccu anta koḻattil nā[lu]mm[ā*]nilamum payirṟetti
koyilukkut tāṉam parikalamum muṇṭākki anta koḻattila-

ntilu nampiy rāma[yya]ṉukku yirukala vitai pā[ṭum] ciñka perumāḷ
ayyaṉṉukku yirukalavitaipāṭum yintapaṭi paṉṉiraṇṭu pirāmaṇa[ṟ*]ku
yiruvattu nākkal vitaippāṭum taṉma ciṉṉatātaṉ makaṉ tirumalaiya
ṉṉukku yirukalavitaippāvum cantaṇakkottaṉṉukku yirukalavitai
[pā*]vum cakkarakkottaṉukku yirukalavitaippāvuum piḻiccaiyyāka
viṭṭom yinta pirāmaṇarukkum tātaṉukkum kottakaḻukkum vāram
vāram cai[ka]y yillai yinta kaḻattukku

The inscription stops here. Probably the following piece which is engraved
above the record is its continuation:—

nal[la]ttai cilaviṭṭa perum kārām pacuvai keṅkaikaraiyile koṇ

tile p[o*]kavum kuruttoram paṇṇiṉa tocattilē p[o*]
kavum [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 471.
(A.R. No. 471 of 1907.)
Periyakōṭṭai, Palani Taluk, Madurai District
Kadali Narasimha-Perumāḷ temple—on a boulder to the north
Kaṇḍa[ku]ṭṭaṉ: year 2+1+1
inscription.p:
This is a damaged inscription in Vaṭṭeḻuttu. It records some provision made
by one Bāradāyaṉ Brahmaśrīrājaṉ for burning a lamp in the temple of Bhaṭāra and
probably for offerings and worship on festival days and for a lamp in a shrine of the
goddess (?) The sabhā of the place seems to have taken this responsibility.

edition.l: ••mel••• ko[yil]haṭāra[ harkku] brahmaśrīrājaṉ vaitta tiru-

inscription.head:
No. 472.
(A.R. No. 472 of 1907.)
Periyakōṭṭai, Palani Taluk, Madurai District
On a rock in a field to the west of the village
inscription.p:
This is incised in modern characters, and is damaged, It is dated in Jaya,
Vaigāśi 17, and seems to regīster a gift of land to a certain Śivala Peddinā[ya*]kkaṉ,
son of Bommināyakkaṉ who was a servant of Tirumalai Chinna Pāppanāyakkar.

edition.l: ceyavaruṣam vaiyyācimātam 17- tiyati

inscription.head:
No. 473.
(A.R. No. 473 of 1907.)
Rishiyūr, Tirutturaippundi Taluk, Tanjavur District
Kailāsanātha temple—on the south wall
Kulōttuṅga-Chōḷa III: year 38
inscription.p:
This records a gift of 300 kāśu for a kāḷāñji to the temple of Tiruvagattīśva-
ramuḍaiyār at Paḻiśūr by a lady by name Periyanāchchi alias Nāṟpatteṇṇāyira-
māṇikkam.

l.ta:
yār tiruṉāmattaṭiyāril keṅkā makaḷ periya nācciyāṉa nāṟpatteṇ
ṇāyira māṇikkam iṉṉāyanāṟkku pa-
ṇṇukiṟa tirukkāḷāñcikku uṭalāka oṭukkiṉa kācu muṉṉūṟu [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 474.
(A.R. No. 474 of 1907.)
Rishiyūr, Tirutturaippundi Taluk, Tanjavur District
Kailāsanātha temple—on the same wall
[Kulōttuṅga III]: year 3[8]: 1215-16 A.D.
inscription.p:
The king is given the epithets “who took Madurai etc. This is a mutilated
inscription recording a gift of land and services as tirunāmattukkāṇi to the temple.

l.ta:
tiripuvaṉaccakkaravattikaḷ maturaiyum īḻamum karuvūrum
pāṇṭiyaṉ muṭittalaiyum•••••••••

••••• niṉṟu eṅkaḷukku veṇṭum elattevai ceyyakkaṭavarka[ḷā]
kavum [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 475.
(A.R. No. 475 of 1907.)
Rishiyūr, Tirutturaippundi Taluk, Tanjavur District
Kailāsanātha temple—on the same wall
Kulōttuṅga III: year 25: 1202-03 A.D.
inscription.p:
The king is given the same epithets as in the above. This records a gift of
160 kuḻi of land tax-free by one Nariyañjikkūttaṉ Vayanāṭṭuvēḷāṉ, a kaṉmi (agent)
of Kaḷattūr-Kiḻavar Tirumāgāḷattāṇḍār a kāṇiyāḷaṉ (landlord) of the place. This
is said to have comprised 60 kuḻi newly purchased by the donor and 100 kuḻi
already granted by him and now consolidated in the document. Piḻiśūr is
stated to be in Pāmbuṇi-kūṟṟam, a division of Śuttamali-vaḷanāḍu.

edition.l:
svastiśrī [||*] tiripuvaṉa[ccakkaravattikaḷ maturaiyu]m īḻamum pāṇṭiyaṉ
muṭittalaiyuṅ karuvūruṅ koṇṭaruḷiya śrīkulottuṅkacoḻatevaṟku
yāṇṭu 25- āvatu cuttamalivaḷanāṭṭup pāmpuṇikkūṟṟattup piḻicūr
uṭaiyār tiruvakattiśvaramuṭaiyārukku

ivvūṟ kāṇiyuṭaiya kaḷattūr kiḻavar tirumākāḷattāṇṭār kaṉmi
kaḷil nariyañcik kūttaṉ vayanāṭṭu veḷāneṉ ivvūṟ kāṇiyuṭaiya
kaḷattūr kiḻavan taḷarānilai kākku nāyakaṉ u[y*]yavantān pakkal
nāṉ innāyaṉārukku tirunantāviḷakkuppuṟa-

māka ivvūril ittaḷarānilai kākku nāyakaṉ uyyavantāṉ kāṇiyāy
varukiṟa teṟkil teruvil teṟkil ciṟakil kiḻpāṟkellai comanāṉa
(ta?)veḷāṉ maṉaikku meṟkum teṉpāṟkellai cuntaracoḻapāṇṭi
yavāy [k*]kālukku vaṭakkum melpāṟkellai piḷ-

ḷaiyār kuṇṭukkuk kiḻakkum vaṭapāṟkellai teruvukkut teṟkum
ākap peruṉāṉ kellaikku naṭuvupaṭṭa nilaṅkuḻi aṟupatum innilattu
kku meṟkum peruvitikku kiḻakku ivvayanāṭṭu veḷāṉ munnāḷil
tanta iṟaiyiliyāka koṇṭu viṭṭa viḷainilaṅ kuḻi nūṟum āka kuḻi
nūṟṟaṟupatum i-

nnāyanārukku nāḷoṉṟukku[oru tirunantā]viḷakku eriya iṟaiyiliyā
kac cantirātittavarai ittiruviḷakku[k*]ku ik kuḻi nūṟṟaṟupatum
koṇṭu viṭṭa nariyañcikkūttanāna vayanāṭṭu veḷāṉeṉ in nila
ṅkuḻi nūṟṟaṟupatum u-

paiyamāka kaikkoṇṭu ittirununtāviḷakku oṉṟum cantirātitta
varai iṉnilam kaikkoṇṭu erikkac cammatittom tevakulatilata
paṇṭita[nu]m cattiya civapaṇṭitanum āḷuṭaiyānpaṭṭanum periyān
paṭṭanum nampi-

inscription.head:
No. 476.
(A.R. No. 476 of 1907.)
Rishiyūr, Tirutturaippundi Taluk, Tanjavur District
Kailāsanātha temple—on the north wall.
Kulōttuṅga III: year 33: 1211 A.D.
inscription.p:
The king is called Tribhuvanavīradēva ‘who took Madurai etc, The details
of date are given as Mīna, ba, 13, Monday, Pūraṭṭādi yielding the equivalent A.D.
1211, March 14: f.d.n. 19. This is stated to be a renewal deed (puṉapramāṇa-iśaivutīṭṭu)
given by Arayaṉ Tiruvāṉaikkāvuḍaiyāṉ alias Malaiyappiyarāyaṉ of Perumūr in
Veṇṇikkūṟṟam, a division of Śuttamali-vaḷanāḍu to the temple of Tiruvāṉai-
kkāvuḍaiyār at that place (Perumūr), by which he confirmed the gift of 4 vēli of land
(out of seven situated at Piḻiśūr) which he had made in the 30th year of the king
for the expenses of worship in that temple. The supporting document for that
grant having been worn out (and lost) in the meanwhile the tānattār of the temple
applied to him for another authority. The seven vēli of land is said to have been
the strīdhana of the donor's deceased wife Arayaṉ Āḷavandāḷ from her father
Kaḷattūr Kiḻavaṉ Tillaiyuḷvilli Periyāḻvār alias Kulōttuṅgaśōḻa-Vāṇakōvaraiyar and
to have become his property by marriage. Of the remaining 3 vēli, two are said to
have been presented by him to Brāhmaṇas as śrādaha-dāna in memory of his wife,
half a vēli for worship of god Periya-Nāyaṉār installed in the temple of Tiruvagat-
tiśvaramuḍaiyār at Piḻiśūr by Vāṇakōvaraiyar's wife (donor's mother-in-law) and 3 mā
and odd of land for worship of goddess Sarasvatiyār consecrated by Vāṇakōvaraiyar
himself. The gift to the two deities in the temple evidently accounts for the
existence of this comprehensive grant at this place.

l.ta:
tiripuvaṉaccakkaravattikaḷ maturaiyum īḻamum karuvūrum pāṇṭiyaṉ muṭit
talaiyum koṇṭu virar apiṣekamum vijayar a

ḷiya tiripuvaṉaviratevaṟku yāṇṭu muppattumuṉṟāvatu miṉa ṉāyaṟṟu
aparapakṣattu

van nitiyāḻvāṉ mutaliyāṟku naṉṟāka eḻuntaruḷivi-

kkā uṭaiyāṉāṉa malaiyappiyarāyaneṉ eḻuttu ippaṭikku ivai

ṉeṉ eḻuttu ippaṭikku ivai kaḷattūr kiḻavaṉ arayaṉ tillaiyuḷ
villiyāṉa kulottuṅkacoḻa vāṇakovaraiyaṉ eḻuttu ippaṭikku
ivai kaḷattūr kiḻavaṉ arayaṉ

tirumākāḷa muṭaiyāṉ eḻuttu ippaṭikku ivai kaḷattūr kiḻavaṉ
arayaṉ tāyapirāṉ eḻuttu ippaṭikku ivai teṅkaṅkuṭaiyan irā
[ca*]rā[ca*]tevanāṉa aḻakiya coḻaveḷāṉ eḻuttu: .

inscription.head:
No. 477.
(A.R. No. 477 of 1907.)
Kuttālam, Mayurum Taluk, Tanjavur District
Chōḷēśvara temple—on the south wall
of the central shrine
inscription.p:
This is dated in the 4th regnal year of an unnamed king. This records an
order of Kuṟṟālamuḍaiyāṉ Ishabadēvaṉ Vikkaviṉāyakar alias Jayadarappallavaraiyar
to a certain Kavaṇūruḍaiyāṉ asking him to re-enter into the tax-remission register
4 vēli of land which had been included in the brahmadēya granted by him and to
reallot the land at 2 vēli to the temple of Vikrama-Chōḷīśvaramuḍaiyār and two to
the Brāhmaṇas. Of the land found in excess in the survey, one vēli was to be
alloted as tirunāmattukkāṇi to the same temple. Kuṟṟālam is said to be situated in
Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻa-vaḷanāḍu. The chief figures again below in No. 479 (of
Kulōttuṅga III).

l.ta:
kuṟṟālamuṭaiyāṉ [i]ṣapatevaṉ vikkaviṉāyakarāṉa
ceyatarappallavaraiyar olaippaṭi kavaṇūruṭaiyāṉukkuc cellumpaṭi

teyattoṭum
niṅkal [nila*]ttukkūṭip paḻampaṭiye niṅkalāka nikkiṉa nilam nāl
veliyum

vikkiramacoḷicvaramuṭaiyārkku iruveli nilamum pirāmaṇaṟkku iru
veli nilamum piṟikkavum ivvūr aḷan teṟṟamāṉa nilattile it
tevaṟku tirun[ā]-

mattukkāṇiyāka veli nilamum piṟikkavum ippaṭi nālāvatu pacāṉa
mutal piṟittuk kuṭukkavum paṇṇuvate ivai ceyatarap pallavarai
yar eḻuttu ||-

inscription.head:
No. 478.
(A.R. No. 478 of 1907.)
Kuttālam, Mayuram Taluk, Tanjavur District
Ōṅkārēśvara temple—on the south wall of the central shrine
[Kulōttuṅga-Chōḷa III] year lost
inscription.p:
This is mutilated and fragmentary. The eulogistic appellation of
Kulōttuṅga III begins the inscription, which records a gift of land evidently to the
temple of Ōṁkāra-Īśvara-muḍaiya-Paramasvāmi.

l.ta:
tiripuvaṉaccakkaravattikaḷ maturaiyum īḻamum pāṇṭi
yaṉ muṭittalaiyuṅ koṇ•••••••••

purattu ellaikku meṟkum paṭṭa paṭukai[ni]lattu ivvūrkkuṭalāṉa
taramili nilamum pākāciriyattu nāṇalan•••••••••

inscription.head:
No. 479.
(A.R. No. 479 of 1907.)
Kuttālam, Mayuram Taluk, Tanjavur District
Ōṅkārēśvara temple—on the same wall
Kulōttuṅga-Chōḷa III: year 25: 1203 A.D.
inscription.p:
The inscription states that it begins with the introduction Puyalvāyttu maṇ
vaḷara, etc., and proceeds with the appellation ‘who took Madurai, Īḻam and the
crowned head of the Pāṇḍya’. The details of the date are given as Makara, pañ-
chamī, Saturday, Uttiram (=A.D., 1203, January 4, the paksha being bahuḷa). This
is incomplete. It seems to record the exemption granted by the Mahā-sabhā of
village Kuṟṟālamuḍaiyāṉ-Brahmadēśam in Tiruvaḻundūr-nāḍu, a division of
Jayaṅgoṇḍaśoḻa-vaḷanāḍu, on some lands endowed as ūrkīḻiṟaiyili by Piḷḷai
Jayadarappallavaraiyar to the temple of Ōṅkārīśvaramuḍaiyār founded by him on
the north bank of the village-tank. Reference is made to a land purchased from
him by the Sabhā for pasture of the cattle and to another piece granted to them
free by the same chief.

edition.l:
. haraḥ—svastiśrī [||*] puyal vāyttu maṇ vaḷara eṉṟa pāṭṭeḻuti tiri
puvaṉaccakkaravattikaḷ maturaiyum īḻamum pāṇṭiyaṉ muṭittalaiyuṅ
koṇṭaruḷiya śrīkulottuṅkacoḻatevaṟku yāṇṭu 25- āvatu
makara[nāyaṟṟu]••• pakṣattu pañcamiyum caṉikkiḻamaiyum peṟṟa uttarattu
nā[ḷ jayaṅko]ṇṭacoḻa vaḷanāṭṭu tiruvaḻuttūr nāṭṭu akaram
śrīkuṟṟālamuṭaiyāṉ brahmatecattu mahāsabhaiyār eḻuttu [||*] nam
mūrkkumelai ūrkkuḷattu vaṭakaraiyil piḷḷai jayatarappallavarayar
eḻuntaruḷuvitta uṭaiyār oṅkāriśvaramuṭaiyār koyil tevarkaṉmi
yum koyil kaṇakkaṉum kaṇṭu

innāyaṉāṟku ūrkiḻ iṟaiyiliyāka viṭṭa śrīteviva[ti]kku kiḻakku kaṇa
vativāykkālukku teṟku iraṇṭāṅ kaṇṇāṟṟu mutaṟ catirattu

innila[m*] mukkāṇi
muntirikaikkiḻ mākāṇiyum ūrkkiḻiṟaiyiliyāka viṭṭum piḷḷai

inscription.head:
No. 480.
(A.R. No. 480 of 1907.)
Kuttālam, Mayuram taluk, Tanjavur District
Ōṅkārēśvara temple—on the same wall
Kulōttuṅga-Chōḷa III: year 35: 1212-13 A.D.
inscription.p:
The details of date are Mīna, śu. 4, Tuesday, Kārttigai, which are however
irregular. This also begins with the same indication about the introduction Puyal
vāyttu etc. Along with the main part are found four other disconnected pieces
which however should have belonged to this inscription, which seems to register
the remission of taxes granted by the ūravar (assembly) of Pallavanārayaṇapuram
in Tiruvaḻundūr-nāḍu on some land endowed by them as tirunāmattukkāṇi and
ūrkkiḻ-iṟaiyili to the temple of Ōṅkārīśvaramuḍaiyār.

l.ta:
puyal vāyttu maṇ vaḷara eṉkiṟa pāṭṭu eḻuti tiripu
vaṉac cakkaravattikaḷ maturaiyum īḻamum pāṇṭiyaṉ muṭittalaiyum
koṇṭaruḷiya śrīkulottuṅkacoḻa

yum
cevvāykkiḻamaiyum peṟṟa kāttikai nāḷ jayaṅkoṇṭacoḻavaḷa
nāṭṭu tiru[vaḻun]tūr nāṭṭup pallavanā-

rāyaṇapuramāṉa ulakamātevipurattu ūravarom kuṟṟālamuṭaiyāṉ
araiyaṉ āḷu••••••••• yappallavaraiyar kuṟṟālamuṭaiyāṉ

ramuṭaiyārkku tirunāmattukkāṇiyum ūrkkiḻ iṟaiyili
yum••••••••• ttukku

ḷvāykkālukku vaṭakkum melpāṟ••••••• ccaṉ iṟaiyilikku kiḻakku
vaṭapāṟkellai kuḷattūruṭaiyāṉ tiru[mañ]caṉa kollai nilattukku
tteṟkum āka icai-
nta innāṉ kellai uḷ naṭuvuppaṭṭa nilam

innilam orumāvaraiyum kāṇiyum ūrkkiḻ iṟaiyiliyum
āka viṭṭu innilantukku ūrmāvinta cilvari peruvari uḷḷiṭṭaṉa
ūrile nāṅkaḷe eṟṟi iṟuttu anta rāyampāṭ

ṭam tavirakkaṭavomākavum innilam oru māvaraiyum innāyaṉārkku
ca

vaṟ kāṇiyum ūrkkiḻ iṟaiyiliyumākac ceytu kuṭut
tom ippallavanā[rā*]yaṇapurattu ūravaro

kuṟaivuḷḷaṭaṅkak kāṇiyum ūrkkiḻ iṟaiyiliyumāka nirvārttuk
kuṭuttom innilattukku

tukku ivarkaḷ veṇṭiṉapote piramāṇam paṇṇikkuṭukkak kaṭa
vomākavum kuṭukka māṭṭuttilomākil ippira

māṇa icaivutiṭṭu piramāṇa māvutākavum ippaṭi sammatittuk kāṇi
yum ūrkkiḻ iṟaiyiliyuñ ceytu kuṭuttom iv oṅkāri

nārāyaṇapuramāna ulakamātevipurattu ūravarom iva[r]kaḷ paṇikka

ṭaiyāṉ coṉṉavāṟṟivā[ṉ]ṉakkapirāṉ eḻuttu iṉṉam—

inscription.head:
No. 481.
(A.R. No. 481 of 1907.)
Kuttālam, Mayuram Taluk, Tanjavur District
Ōṅkārēśvara temple—on the west wall of
the central shrine
Kōnēriṉmaikoṇḍāṉ: year 38
inscription.p:
This is evidently an inscription of Kulōttuṅga III whose officer (tiruma
ndira-vōlai) Rājanārāyaṇa-Mūvēndavēḷāṉ has signed the royal order granting a
paḍugai land in the village as tirunāmattukkāṇi and ūrkīḻiṟaiyili to the temple
of Ōṅkārīśvaramuḍaiyār.

edition.l:
haraḥ-svastiśrī [||*] tiripuvaṉaccakkaravatti koṉerimeṉ koṇṭāṉ
jayaṅkoṇṭacoḻa vaḷanāṭṭu kuṟṟālamuṭaiyāṉ brahmadēśattu u-

l.ta:
•••• la coḻapurattellaiyil kāverikkulaikku vaṭakku vikkirama coḻaṉ
mātirimaṅkalattellaikku kiḻakku pallavanārāyaṇapu•••

•••• katevipurattu ellaikku me[ṟ*]kkuppaṭṭa paṭukai nilam inā[y]yaṉār
[k*]ku tirunāmattuk kāṇiyum ūrkiḻ iṟaiyiliyum ākavum ivūra
var cāta•••

m•• kaṇakkuṭuttu••••• tu varākaṉ[?]oṉṟum ivarkaḷ tanta cātaṉappaṭiye
aṉupavikka•••• veṇum eṉṟu••••

•••••capparayaṉ namakku coṉṉamaiyil ippaṭi ceyyakkaṭavatākac coṉ
ṉom ivviṭam ivarkaḷ tanta cātaṉappaṭiyē tirunāmattukkā

ṇiyum ūrkiḻ iṟaiyiliyum āka muṉpu aṉupavittu varukiṟapaṭiye
aṉupavikka eḻutiṉāṉ tirumantiraolai irācanārāyaṇa muventave
ḷāṉ valla-

[vataraiyar] eḻuttu viḻiñattaraiyaṉ eḻuttu vayaṉāṭṭaraiyaṉ eḻuttu
cittaraiyaṉ eḻuttu aṅkarāyaṉ eḻuttu toṇṭaimāṉ eḻuttu
iyāṇ-

inscription.head:
No. 482.
(A.R. No. 482 of 1907.)
Kuttālam, Mayuram Taluk, Tanjavur District
Ōṅkārēśvara temple—on the north wall of
the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine
Kulōttuṅga III: year 25: 1203 A.D.
inscription.p:
The inscription begins with the same preamble giving only the beginning
words of the introduction Puyalvāyttu maṇ vaḷara like Nos. 479 and 480 above, and
ending with the appellation ‘who took Madurai, Īḻam and the crowned head of the
Pāṇḍya’. The details of date are given as Mīna, śu. 1, Sunday, [Rēvati]
corresponding to A.D. 1203, March 16, Nak. ending ·84 the previous day. The
record is incomplete and stops with the mention of some land belonging to
Jayadharappallavaraiyar at Kāvirinallūr a dēvadāna village of god Śoṉṉavāṟāṟivār at
Vīṅgunīrtturutti and which the ūravar (assembly) of the place made into an
ūrkīḻ-iṟaiyili.

l.ta:
puyal vāyttu maṇvaḷara eṉṟa pāṭṭeḻuti tiripuvaṉaccakka
ravattikaḷ maturaiyum īḻamum pāṇṭiyaṉ muṭittalaiyuṅ koṇṭaru
ḷiya śrī[kulottuṅka]coḻatevaṟku [yāṇ]ṭu

maiyum nāyaṟṟukkiḻamaiyum peṟṟa [re
vati]nāḷ jayaṅkoṇṭaco•••••• ttūr nāṭṭu viṅkunirtturutti
uṭaiyār coṉṉavāṟṟivār tevatāṉam kāviri nallūr ūravaro
m innāṭṭuk kuṟṟālamuṭaiyāṉ

māṇa icaivu tiṭṭuk kuṭutta
paricāvatu innāyaṉārkkuk kāvirinallūr ūrkkiḻ••••• innilam iru
māvarai yitil piḷḷai jaya

rappallavaraiyar kāṇiyāṉa nilattu in
nāyaṉārkku ivar tirunāmattuk kāṇiyāka viṭṭa nilam orumā

inscription.head:
No. 483.
(A.R. No. 483 of 1907.)
Kuttālam, Mayuram Taluk, Tanjavur District
Ōṅkārēśvara temple—on the same wall
Kōnēriṉmaikoṇḍāṉ: year 29
inscription.p:
This records an order of the king (Kulōttuṅga III ?) to (his officer)
Vāṇādarāyaṉ for remitting the taxes on the several pieces of land granted as
tirunāmattukkāṇi to god Ōṅkārēśvaramuḍaiyār and as kāṇi to Brāhmaṇas settled
by him in the place by Jayadharappallavaraiyaṉ who is said to be of Kuṟṟālam in
Paṭṭiṉakkūṟṟam, a division of Gēyamāṇikka-vaḷanāḍu. These lands were situated in
different villages like Mēl-Tattaṅguḍi. Pallavanārayaṇapuram alias Ulagamādēvi-
puram and Āyaṅguḍi alias Kulōttuṅgaśōḻaṉ-Kuṟṟālam in Mērkkā-nāḍu a
division of Virudarājabhayaṇkara-vaḷanāḍu and amounted in all to about 29(1/2) vēli
and odd in extent. Mention is made of some lands belonging to the temple of
Vikramachōḷīśvaramuḍaiyār of the village, which were purchased by the donor for
building the tirumaḍaiviḷāgam round the Ōṅkārīśvara temple and for the formation
of a flower garden for the temple. As the inscription is mutilated in places and
stops incomplete it is not possible to follow the sense of the transaction in detail.
The revenue survey of the 16th year of Kulōttuṅga-chōḷa ‘who abolished the
tolls’ is referred to while describing the nature of the lands.

l.ta:
. tiripuvaṉaccakkaravatti koṉerimeṉ koṇṭāṉ
vāṇātarāyaṉukku keyamāṇikkavaḷanāṭṭup paṭṭiṉakkūṟṟattuk kuṟṟā
lamuṭaiyāṉ arayaṉ āḷuṭaiya nāyaṉāṉa ceyatarappallavarayaṉ jayaṅ
koṇṭacoḻavaḷanāṭṭut tiruvaḻuttūr nāṭṭuk kuṟṟālamuṭaiyāṉ

ttu uṭaiyār oṅkāricvaramuṭaiy[ār]kkut tirunāmattuk
kāṇiyākavum ivūril ivaṉ vaitta pir[āma]ṇarkku kāṇiyākavum

teyam meṟtatta[ṅkuṭi]yilum pallavaṉnārāyaṇapura

tirunāmattukkāṇi

[kuṭu]ttapaṭiye ittevarkkut tirunāmattukkāṇiyum

kaṇakkuppaṭi••••••••• ṇi muttirikaik kiḻaraiye mu[ṉ]ṟu•••••• cuṅkan
tavitta kulottuṅkaco-

rukku•••• ṟaikkum iṟuttu varukiṟa kārilum aṟpaciviṣuvi

inscription.head:
No. 484.
(A.R. No. 484 of 1907.)
Kuttālam, Mayuram Taluk, Tanjavur District
Ōṁkārēśvara temple—on the north and west walls
of the Chaṇḍēśvara shrine
Kulōttuṅga III: year 25: 1202-03 A.D.
inscription.p:
This also begins like some previous records with the same short preamble.
The details of the date Mīna, śu. 5, Wednesday, Rōhiṇi, would give the equivalent
A.D. 1203, March 19.

This records a gift of a mā and half of land as kāṇi and ūrkīḻ-iṟaiyili by the
ūrār (assembly) of Aṉapāyapuram in Tiruvaḻundūr-nāḍu a division of Jayaṅ-
goṇḍaśōḻa-vaḷanāḍu, to the temple.

cakkaravattikaḷ maturaiyum īḻamum pāṇṭiyaṉ muṭittalaiyuṅ koṇṭa
ruḷiya śrīkulottuṅka coḻadēvaṟku yāṇṭu 25- āvatu miṉanāyaṟṟu
pūrvapakṣattu pañcami-

inscription.head:
No. 485.
(A.R. No. 485 of 1907.)
Kuttālam, Mayuram Taluk, Tanjavur District
Uttaravēdīśvara temple—on the north wall of the central shrine
Rājēndra-Chōḷa I: year 9: 1021-22 A.D.
inscription.p:
This begins with the introduction Tīrumannivaḷara etc. It records the sale
of land and house site by the sabhā of Kaṇakkaiyūr alias Kāmakkamaṅgalam in
Pērāvūr-nāḍu a division of Uyyakkoṇḍār-vaḷanāḍu to the temple of Māhādēva of
Vīṅgunīr-Tirutturutti in Tiruvaḻundūr-nāḍu for 75 kāśu inclusive of the capital for
payment of taxes by the sabhā on behalf of the temple.

inscription.head:
No. 486.
(A.R. No. 486 of 1907.)
Kuttālam, Mayuram Taluk, Tanjavur District
Uttaravēdīśvara temple—on the same wall
Par. Rājēndradēva year 5: 1055-56 A.D.
inscription.p:
The inscription begins with the short introduction mentioning the king's
conquest of Iraṭṭapāḍi, planting of his pillar of victory at Kollāpuram and his
anointment of victors having captured the elephants, horses, harem and treasury of
the terrified Āhavamalla at Koppam. Portion at the end of the inscription is lost.
It registers an endowment of 25 kāśu by a merchant of Gaṅgaikoṇḍaśōḻapuram
by name Veṇkāḍaṉ Āḍavallān, for feeding with the interest thereon some itinerant
Śivayōgins after the daily midday service in the temple of Śoṉṉavāṟaṟivār at
Vīṅgunīr-Tirutturutti in Tiruvaḻundūr-nāḍu, a division of Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻa-vaḷanāḍu.
The amount was deposited with the śivabrāhmaṇas of the temple. The gift is said
to have been made to bring strength of arms to the king.

[m*] nāṭṭi perāṟṟaṅkarai koppat tāhavamallaṉai añcuvit

inscription.head:
No. 487.
(A.R. No. 487 of 1907.)
Kuttālam, Mayuram Taluk, Tanjavur District
Uttaravēdīśvara temple—on the east wall of the central shrine
Rājēndra-Chōḷa I: year 8: 1020-21 A.D.
inscription.p:
The inscription begins with introduction Tirumanni vaḷara etc. This
registers a sale of half vēli of land (made tax-free) by the sabhā of Villavaṉ-
mahādēvi-chaturvēdimaṅgalam a brahmadēya in Tiruvaḻundūr-nāḍu to the temple
of Tirukkaṟṟaḷi-Mahādēva at Vīṅgunīr-Tirutturutti for 45 kāśu.
