inscription.head:
No. 488.
(A.R. No. 488 of 1907.)
Kuttālam, Mayuram Taluk, Tanjavur District
Uttaravēdīśvara temple—on the south wall of the central shrine
inscription.p:
This is a single verse in Sanskrit in Grantha characters stating that this is an
edict of Śambhu having his residence at Kalyāṇālaya.

l.gr: svastiśrī [||*] thatkriḍā kila lōkasya samhāra sthiti sṛṣṭayaḥ

inscription.head:
No. 489.
(A.R. No. 489 of 1907.)
Kuttālam, Mayuram Taluk, Tanjavur District
Uttaravēdīśvara temple—on the north wall of the maṇḍapa
in front of the central shrine
Vikrama-Chōḷa: year 6: 1123-24 A.D.
inscription.p:
The inscription states that the introduction is taken as beginning with the
words Pūmādu puṇara etc. The details of date are given as Siṁha, ba.*., Thursday,
Śadayam (=A.D. 1123. August 9). The tithi was ba. 1. This registers the sale of a
quarter vēli and odd of land tax free for 10 kāśu (inclusive of 7(1/2) kāśu towards the
taxes) by the assembly of Uttamaśōḻa-chaturvēdimaṅgalam a brahmadēya in
Tiruvaḻundūr-nāḍu which is here stated to be a division of Rājanārāyaṇa-vaḷa-
nāḍu, to a certain Eṟichchēri-uḍaiyāṉ Rājaśikhāmaṇi-Viḻupparaiyaṉ of Ādaṉūr for
his making it over to the temple of Śoṉṉavāṟaṟivār at Vīṅgunīr-Tirutturutti as
Śemmaṇḍaippuṟam (?) The names of ten members of the sabhā are given as
party for the sale and they are said to have met in the temple of Kailāsam-
uḍaiyār in their village for the purpose.

l.ta:
pūmātu puṇarap puvimātu vaḷara nāmātu viḷaṅka ceya
mātu ni-

lavat taṉṉirupatu meṉṟu tiruvāykkeḻvi eḻuti tiripuvaṉac cakkara
vattikaḷ

śrī vikkirama coḻatevarkku yāṇṭu āṟāvatu ciṅkanāyaṟṟu apara
pakkattu•••

peruṅkuṟiyāy

coṉṉavāraṟivār koyil mu-

[ecca]kumārapaṭṭarum kārampicceṭṭu ka-

ṟutta p[o]caṉ ciki[ṣ]ṇappaṭṭarum karippuṟattu aṉantipaṭṭarum
kārampicceṭṭu tiruvaraṅka nārāyaṇapaṭṭaru[m]

kārampicceṭṭu kaṟuttapocaṉ paramāṟmapaṭṭarum kuravaceri ecca
kumārapaṭṭarum kārampicceṭṭu kaṟuttap[o]-

caṉ iḷaiyaciki[ṣ*]ṇapaṭṭarum kārampicceṭṭu kaṟuttapocaṉ uyyak
koṇṭāṉ paṭṭarum kārampicceṭṭu kaṟutta-

pocaṉ ne[y*]yuṇṭāṉpaṭṭarum araṇaippuṟattu iḷaiya yaccakumāra
[paṭṭaru]m u[ḷ]ḷiṭṭa makācapaiyār paṇiyāl i[nta]

inta āticaṇṭecuratevar[k*]ku cemmaṇṭaippu[ṟa]m* ceytukuṭutta
nilattukku kiḻpāṟkellai neṉmalaicceri nilattukku meṟkum

teṉpāṟkellai viṟkiṉṟoṉilattukku vaṭa[k*]kum melpāṟkellai
toḻu[t]tālaṅkuṭiyil uṭaiyār coṉṉavāraṟivār tevatāṉam

mā[ṉa ni]lattukku kiḻakkum vaṭapāṟkellai nelmalaicceri nilattukku
teṟkum āka icainta perunāṉkellaiyuḷ naṭu-

vuppaṭṭa nilam kālearaikkāṇiyum ātaṉūruṭaiyāṉ eṟicceri
yuṭaiyāṉāṉa ir[ā*]ccikāmaṇi viḻupparayaṉu-

kku viṟṟukkuṭuttu in[nila]ttukku vilaiyāka ivaṉ pak[kal*] koṇṭa
kācu iraṇṭaraiyum innilattukku iṟaikkuṭalāka

meṟpaṭiyāṉ iṭakkoṇṭa kācu eḻaraiyum āka iṟaikkum vilaip
poruḷukkum āka ivaṉ pakkal koṇṭa kācu pa-

ttum inta ātaṉūruṭaiyāṉāṉa eṟicceriyuṭaiyāṉ irācacikāmaṇi
viḻupparaiyaṉ orunāḷāl oru kiḻiyākak kuṭuttu

iccoṉṉavāraṟivār koyil mulaviruttiyarākiya āticaṇṭecira
tevaṟku iṟaiyiliyāka ce[m*]maṇṭaippuṟam

ceytu kuṭuttom inta uttamacoḻac caruppetimaṅkalattu makā
capaiyom inta nilattu meṉokkiṉa maramu[m*]

kiḻnokkiṉa kiṇaṟum nirurimai paṇpāṭu paḻa[māṉār] pāyavum vāravum
maṟṟum eppeṟpaṭṭa urimaikaḷum akappaṭa nāṅka-

ḷe iṟaiyili ceytukuṭutta nilam kālearaikkāṇiyum nokki
uḷḷa peruvari cilvariyum tiruvācalāl ponta kuṭi-

m[ai]kaḷum cantirāyittavaṟ i•••• pokkaṟukkakaṭavomāka innilam
cātakamāka iṟaiyiliye aṉupavikka peṟukaikku [u]-

ṭaiyār coṉṉavāraṟivār koyilil kallilum cempilum veṭṭik
koḷḷappeṟuvatāka cammatittu icc[e]mmaṇ-

ṭaippuṟam kāle [a]raikkāṇiyum iṟaiyili ceytu kuṭuttom
ittirutturuttic coṉṉavāraṟivā(r)ṟku mulaviruttiya-

rākiya āticaṇṭeciratevaṟku inta uttamacoḻac caruppeti
maṅkalattu makācapaikkuc camaintu muṟcollappa[ṭ]ṭa ara[ṇai]-

ppuṟattu eccamu[rt*]ti cikumārapaṭṭarum kārampicceṭṭu [kaṟu]tta
pocaṉ ci[kiṣ]ṇapaṭṭarum karippuṟattu aṉṉantippaṭṭaru[m*]
uḷḷiṭṭār paṇik-

ka ivarkaḷ paṇiyāl inta uttamacoḻac caruppetimaṅkalattu
karaṇattāṉ muvalūruṭaiyāṉ tiruppūvaṉamuṭaiyāṉ tiru[cci*]-

ṟṟampalamuṭaiyāṉ eḻuttu ippaṭikku [araṇai]ppuṟattu ecca
kumā[rapa]ṭṭa[ṉ] eḻuttu
ṉ eḻuttu ippaṭikku ivai kuravaceri eññaku-

inscription.head:
No. 490.
(A.R. No. 490 of 1907.)
Kuttālam, Mayuram Taluk, Tanjavur District
Uttaravēdīśvara temple—on the same wall
Vikrama-Chōḷa: year 5: 1122-23 A.D.
inscription.p:
The details of the date viz. Simha, śu. 13, Wednesday, Hasta are found to
be irregular. This registers the sale, by the Mahā-sabhā of Bhūlōkamāṇikka-
chaturvēdimaṅgalam a brahmadēya in Virudarāja-bhayaṅkara-vaḷanāḍu of 8
mā of land by two separate plots of 4 mā each, to two persons by name
Śeyyapādam-śēr-kaḻal alias Poṟkōyil-pichchaṉ and Kuḻalūruḍaiyāṉ Madurān-
taka-Araiyaṉ, who presented the lands as maḍappuṟam (for the maintenance of a
maṭha attached) to the temple of Śonnavāraṟivār at Vīṅgunīr-Tirutturutti. The
cost of the lands by itself is not specified, but the assembly is stated to have
received from the temple a sum of 30 kāśu through Poṟkōyil-pichchaṉ towards
cost and tax payment combined, and agreed to pay śilvari, peruvari and also the
kuḍimai themselves on behalf of the temple. The land was entrusted to Poṟkōyil-
pichchaṉ for conducting this charity. Among the boundaries are mentioned two
lands, one endowed for a watershed by Śōḻappallavaraiyaṉ and the other for a lamp
by Tiruvēḻvikkuḍi Maṇavāḷanambi.

l.ta:
yom viṅkuni[r*]t tirutturutti uṭ[aiyār c]onṉavāraṟi
v[ār ko]-

ratevaṟku iṟaiyili maṭappu[ṟam cetu] kuṭukkaikku
capai[yuḷḷiru]-

ntu paṇi[t*]tār irāyūr cirāmapaṭṭarum yirāyūṟ nā[rāyaṇa] paṭ
ṭarum kuṇṭur ti(ṭṭai)

llaiṉāyaka pa[ṭṭa]rum irāyūṟ koṇṭaya paṭṭarum a[raṇai]ppuṟattu
koṇṭai[ya paṭṭarum]

tirappil puruṣottama pa[ṭ*]ṭarum cakkaramāṭipaṭṭa[rum i]vvaṉai
vo[m*] ivvāti[ccaṇṭe]

ratevaṟku iṟaiyili maṭappuṟam ceytu kuṭutta ni[lamāvatu] ip
pūlokamāṇi[kkac caruppetima]-

ṅkalattu tiruvaraṅkavatikkuk kiḻakku karuṇākarav[āk]kālukku teṟku
yiraṇṭāṅkaṇṇāṟṟu yi-

raṇṭāñcatirattu irāyūr iḷaiya toṇṭayapaṭṭaṉpakkal vilai
koṇṭu uṭaiyomāy eṅkaḷutā-

y irunta nilam eṭṭu māvukku melpāṟkellai coḻappallavaraiyan
taṇṇiṟpan[tal]puṟattuk-

ku kiḻakkum vaṭapāṟkellai tiruveḻvikkuṭi maṇavāḷa nampi tiru
nuntāviḷakkup puṟattukku teṟkum ki-

ḻpāṟkellai kaṇṭiyūrvākkālukku meṟkum tenpāṟkellai maṇa
vāḷa nampi tevatāṉattukku vaṭakkum āka yi-

caiñca peruṉān kellaiyuḷ naṭuvupaṭṭa ni[la]m eṭṭu mā itil ceyya
pātañcerkaḻalāṉa poṟkoyil piccaṉ ko-

tevaṟku iṟaiili maṭappuṟamākak ko-

maramum kiḻṉokkina kiṇaṟum akappaṭa iṟaiyili

ce[y*]tu kuṭuttu iṟaiyilip poruḷukkum vilaipporuḷukkum vikku
niṟt tirutturutti uṭaiyār conṉavāraṟi-

vār koyil ceyyapātañcerkaḻallāṉa poṟkoyil piccan kaiy
vaḻiyākak koṇṭa kā[cu]

pavikkak kaṭavarkaḷākavum yinṉilattukku ta-

laimaṭaiyāle nirpāyavum kaṭaimaṭaiyāle [viḻa]vum peṟavum in
ṉilattukku cilvari peruvari cantirāyittavaṟ nāṅka-

ḷe iṟukkakaṭavomākavum tiruvācalponta kuṭimaiyum nāṅkaḷe
iṟukkakaṭavomākavum innilam eṭṭu-

tikamāka iṟaiili anupavikka con
ṉavāraṟivā[r*] koyil ka[l*][lilu]m cempilum ve[ṭ*]ṭik-

piccaṉum yivaṉ vaṟkkattārum ko-

ppuṟattuk koṇṭayapa[ṭ]ṭarum vaṟṟalū[r*]c cakkaramāṭipaṭṭarum

yaṇaṉeṉ ivai eṉṉeḻuttu ippaṭikku ivai kuṇṭur tiṭṭai

eḻuttu ippaṭikku ivai irāyūr iḷaiya toṇṭa-

yapaṭṭar eḻuttu ippaṭikku ivai kiṭampuṟattu puruṣottamapaṭṭaṉ
eḻuttu ippaṭikku ivai irāyūr maṇa••••••

eḻuttu ippaṭikku ivai••••

patimaṅkalattu komapuṟattup puruṣottamapaṭṭaṉ eḻuttu ippaṭikku
ivai caṅkaranārāyaṇapaṭṭaṉ eḻuttu•••

māṇikka caruppe••••

l.note: Lines 38 to 40 are cut in bigger characters than the previous portion.

inscription.head:
No. 491.
(A.R. No. 491 of 1907.)
Kuttālam, Mayuram Taluk, Tanjavur District
Uttaravēdīśvara temple—on the same wall
Vikrama-Chōḷa: year 3: 1120-21 A.D.
inscription.p:
The inscription states that the introduction (tiruvāykkēḻvi) Pūmālai
puṉaindu etc. has been taken as written before the king's name and date. The
details of the date are given as Makara, ba. 7, Monday, Hastā which are however
irregular. This registers a similar sale of one vēli of land as Maḍappuṟa-iṟaiyili by
the sabhā of Bhūlōkamāṇikka-chaturvēdimaṅgalam, here stated to be in Nallāṟṟūr-
nāḍu of Virudarāya-bhayaṅkara-vaḷanāḍu to the same temple of Śōnnavāraṟivār
for 91 kāśu received from the temple, through one Dēvaṉ Tirutturutti-pichchaṉ
a servant of the temple (Śripādam-tāṅguvāṉ). The assembly is stated to have met
for this transaction in the courtyard of Aḻagiya-Maṇavāḷa-Āḻvār temple in
their place.

l.ta: karanāyaṟṟu aparapakṣattu cattamiyum tiṅkaḷkiḻamaiyum peṟṟa atta-
meṟkoyil aḻakiyamaṇavāḷa āḻvār koyil tirumuṟṟattil kūṭṭam

ntūrnāṭṭu viṅkuṉir tirutturutti uṭaiyār coṉṉavāraṟivār
ko-

yil āticaṇṭeciratevar[k*]ku iṟaiyiliyāka maṭappuṟañ ceytu
kuṭuk[k*]ai

ykku capayuḷḷiruntu paṇittār irāyūrt toṇṭayapaṭṭaṉum irā
yūr cirāma-

paṭṭaṉum irāyūr kūttāṭipaṭṭaṉum coṭṭaic cirāmapaṭṭaṉum
araṇaippuṟattu ko-

ṭampaṭṭaṉum paṇiyāl inta āticaṇṭeciratevar[k*]ku iṟaiyili
maṭappuṟañ ceytu kuṭu-

veli iṉṉilam velik-

kellai kaṇṭiyūrvatikku meṟkum

ccatirattukku kiḻakkum āka icainta peruṉāṉkellaiyuḷ irāyūr
cirāmapaṭṭaṉ pak-

[ka]ppaṭa iṟaiyili maṭappuṟamāka iṟaiyili ceytu kuṭukka iṟaip
poruḷukkum akappaṭa

koṇṭa aṉṟāṭu naṟkācu toṇṇūṟṟoṉṟum inta viṅkuṉirt
tirutturutti uṭaiyār

c[o]ṉṉavā[ra*]ṟivār koyil cipātantāṅkuvāril tevaṉāṉa tirut
turuttippiccaṉ kaivaḻiyāl

koṇṭu iṉṉilam veliyum cantirāyittavaṟ aṉupavikkakaṭavatākavum
iṉṉilattukkut

[ta]laimaṭaiyāle nirpāyavum kaṭaimaṭaiyāle vāravum peṟakkaṭava
tākavum iṉ-

[ṉi]lattukkuc cilvari peruvari nāṅkaḷe iṟukkakaṭavomākavum tiru
vācal ponta kuṭim-

[ai]yum nāṅkaḷe iṟukkakaṭavomākavum innilam veliyum cantirā
yittavaṟ cā[cuva*]tika-

[m]āka iṟaiyili yaṉupavikka coṉṉavāraṟivār koyi[li*]l kallilum
cempilum veṭṭi koḷ-

[ḷa]k kaṭavatākavum ippaṭi cammatittu iṟaiyili maṭappuṟañ ceytu
kuṭuttom makā ca[p*]aiyuḷ

ṉum vaṟṟalūr cak-

ttu puruṣot-

inscription.head:
No. 492.
(A.R. No. 492 of 1907.)
Kuttālam, Mayuram Taluk, Tanjavur District
Uttaravēdīśvara temple—on the same wall
Vikrama-Chōḷa: year 8: 1125-26 A.D.
inscription.p:
The details of date are given as Makara, śu. 9, Saturday, Anusham (and
found to be irregular). This registers still another sale of land, a little more than a
vēli in extent, by the same assembly as iṟaiyili maḍappuṟam to the temple of
Śonnavāraṟivār for 92 kāśu received by them from a certain Paḷḷikoṇḍāṉ Kūttaṉ
Araiyaṉ Nandiyarāyaṉ of Śiṟṟāmūr in addition to 8 kāśu paid for pariśaṭṭam.
This land is said to have been assigned to one Sabhāyōga-Geṅgādharaṉ Uyyaniṉ-
ṟāḍuvāṉ, a resident of Tiruchchiṟṟambalach-chaturvēdimaṅgalam for the service he
was to conduct (daily ?) in the temple and specially on a festival day in the
month of Vaigāśi.

l.ta: ḻatevaṟku yāṇṭu eṭṭāvatu makara nāyaṟṟu pūrvapakṣattu na-
vamiyum canikkiḻamaiyum peṟṟa aṉuḻatti nāḷ virutarāyapaya-
ṅkaravaḷaṉāṭṭu nallāṟṟūr nāṭṭu prammateyam pūloka māṇikkac

le kūṭṭam kuṟaivaṟak kūṭi iruntu kuṭippeṟkiḻ capaip potuvāka
vilai-

koṇṭuṭaiyomāṉa nilattu [t]eṉkarai [i]rāca nārāyaṇa vaḷa
nāṭṭut ti-

ruveḻuntūrṉāṭṭu viṅkunir tirutturutti uṭaiyār conṉavāraṟi
vāṟku

mulaviruttiyarākiya āti caṇṭecira tevaṟku nāṅkaḷ iṟaiili maṭap
puṟañ cey-

tu kuṭutta paricāvatu pūlokamāṇikkac caruppetimaṅkalattu tiruva
raṅkavatikku-

k kiḻakku karuṇākaravā[y*]kkālukku teṟku mutaṟkaṇṇāṟṟu mutaṟ
ca(tirat)tirattu nilam••••

rai māvaraikkāṇi innilam veliye yaraimāvaraikkāṇikkum kiḻ
pāṟ ke[l*]lai

maṭappuṟattukku meṟkum teṉpāṟkellai kaṇṇāṟṟukku vaṭakkum
melpāṟkellai vatikku kiḻa-

kkum vaṭapāṟkellai kaṇṭiyūr nattattukku teṟkum āka [i*]cainta
perunāṉkellaiyuḷ naṭuvupaṭṭa nila-

m veliye yaraimāvaraikkāṇiyum innilattu meṉokkiṉa maramum
kiḻ nokkiṉa kiṇaṟu makappaṭa maṭappuṟam iṟai-

yili ceytukuṭuttu iṟaiyilipporuḷukkum vilaipporuḷukkum ciṟ
ṟāmuruṭaiyāṉ paḷḷi koṇṭā[ṉ*]kūttaṉ a-

raiyaṉ nantiyarāyaṉukkāka itteva[r t]evatāṉam tirucciṟṟampalac
caruppetimaṅkalattu irukkum capaiyoka

keṅkātaraṉ uyyaniṉṟāṭuvāṉ kai[yva]ḻi koṇṭa aṉṟāṭu naṟkācu
toṇṇūṟṟiraṇṭu ikkācu toṇṇūṟṟiraṇ-

ṭum paricaṭṭattukku koṇṭa kācu [eṭṭu] āka kācu nūṟum koṇṭu
viṟṟuk kuṭutta nilam veli yaraimāvaraikkā-

ṇi itil uṭaiyār coṉṉavāraṟivā[r*] tiruvaikācit tirunāḷ
tiruveḻa[rai]kku vaṇṇāḷaṉcey nilam kā-

lum kumpiṭavantu pūcittu amutu ceykaykku nilam mukkāle arai
māvaraikkāṇiyum āka nilam[o]-

ṉṟe araimāvaraikkāṇiyum capāyoka keṅkātaraṉ uyyaniṉṟāṭu
vāṉum veṟka[t*]tārum kaikkoṇ-

ṭu cantirāyittavaṟ iṟaiyiliyāka aṉupavittu ippaṭi ceyyak kaṭavar
kaḷākavum innilam nokki uḷḷa cil-

vari peruvari ceṉṉirveṭṭi tiruvācal ponta kuṭimaikaḷ nāṅkaḷe
iṟukka kaṭavomākavum innilam veli-

raṟivār koyil kallilum cempilum veṭṭikkoḷḷakkaṭavatākavum
ippaṭi cammatittu iṟai-

yili maṭappuṟañ ceytu kuṭuttom ivvaṉaivom capaiyuḷḷiruntu
paṇittār irāyūraruḷāḷapaṭṭarum ku-

ṇṭur tiṭṭai akkumāra paṭṭarum vaṟṟalūr cakkaramāṭi paṭṭarum
kiṭā[m]pil purucottama paṭṭarum irāyūr nā-

[r]āyaṇa paṭṭarum araṇaippuṟattu koṇṭiyapaṭṭarum paṇikka keṭṭu
inta iṟaiyili maṭappuṟap piramāṇam eḻuti-

ṉeṉ ivvūr mattiyattan kaṇṇamaṅkala muṭaiyāṉ ayyāṟaṉ caṅkara
ṉārāyaṇaṉ e[ḻu*]ttu ippaṭikku ivai

cirāmapaṭṭaṉ eḻuttu ippaṭikku ivai araṇaippuṟattuk koṇṭiya
paṭṭaṉ eḻuttu ippaṭikku ivai kuṇṭurt

tiṭṭai akkumārapaṭṭaṉ eḻuttu ippaṭikku ivai vaṟṟalūṟ cakkara
māṭi paṭṭaṉ eḻuttu ippaṭikku ivai u-

ṟumputtūr veṇṇayapaṭṭaṉ eḻuttu irāyūr kūttāṭipaṭṭaṉ eḻuttu
ippaṭikku ivai iḷaiya-

koṇṭapaṭṭaṉ [eḻu*]ttu ippaṭikku ivai pāmpuṟattu puruṣottama
paṭṭaṉ eḻuttu ippaṭikku ivai irā-

yūr maṇavāḷapaṭṭaṉ eḻuttu ippaṭikku ivai yiḷaiya nārāyaṇapaṭ
ṭaṉ eḻuttu .

eḻutiṉeṉ ivvūr mattiyattaṉ kaṇṇamaṅkalamuṭaiyāṉ ayyāṟaṉ
caṅkara nārāyaṇaṉeṉ [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 493.
(A.R. No. 493 of 1907.)
Kuttālam, Mayuram Taluk, Tanjavur District
Uttaravēdīśvara temple—on the north wall of the maṇḍapa
in front of the central shrine
Kṛishṇadēvarāya. Śaka 1440: 1518 A.D.
inscription.p:
This is dated in the cyclic year Dhātu, Siṁha, śu. Paurṇami, Friday, Maghā
(Mahā-Maghā) which probably corresponds to A.D. 1518, August 20; The cyclic
year was Bahudhānya and the Nak-Śatabhishaj. This records a sarvamānya gift
of the taxes jōḍi, śūlavari, kallaṇai, Nāṭṭukkāṇikkāi, rāyavarttanai, and avasara-
varttanai amounting to 90 poṉ and due to the king from Mēṟkāvēri-Māgaṇai in
favour of the temple of Śoṉṉavāraṟivār at Tirutturutti Kuṟṟālam, on a represen-
tation made to him by Karaṇikam Vaśavarasar, son of Sōmarasar of Tiruppeyaṟṟūr
in Eyil-nāḍu, a division of Toṇḍai-maṇḍalam. This amount was to be utilised
for offerings, adornments, repairs and for special festivals every month, to the god.

rājaparamēśvaran bhūṟuva dēkṣa[ṇa*] paccima uttara catu camuttirādhipati

inscription.head:
No. 494.
(A.R. No. 494 of 1907.)
Kuttālam, Mayuram Taluk, Tanjavur District
Uttaravēdīśvara temple—on the south wall of the same maṇḍapa
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1303, Durmati, Rishabha, ba. 6, Wednesday, Aviṭṭam
(A.D. 1381, May 15). and mentions Kampaṇa-Uḍaiyar at about the end. This is
much damaged and seems to record a remission of some taxes consequent on a
draught and famine (?) in the country during the (previous) year Raudri.

edition.l: śrīśakābdam 1303- mel durma[ti] varuṣam ṛṣabha nāyaṟṟu

mutalikaḷu

inscription.head:
No. 495.
(A.R. No. 495 of 1907.)
Kuttālam, Mayuram Taluk, Tanjavur District
Uttaravēdīśvara temple—on the same wall
Tribh. Rājēndra Chōḷa (III): year 15
inscription.p:
The date is given as Tulā, śu. 9, Thursday, Aviṭṭam equivalent to A.D. 1260,
October 14.

This registers a sale of 2 adjacent house-sites measuring 3 kōl and 9 kōl east
to west with a depth of 14 kōl situated on the north side of the tirumaḍai vīḷāgam
of the temple of Tiruchchattimuṟṟam-Uḍaiyār at Kīḻ-Paḻaiyāṟu alias Rājarājapuram
in Kulōttuṅgaśōḻa-vaḷanāḍu to a maṭha at that place belonging to the lineage of
Meyñānaśivar, for 700 kāśu by the temple of Śoṉṉavāraṟivār.

inscription.head:
No. 496.
(A.R. No. 496 of 1907.)
Svāmimalai, Kumbhakonam Taluk, Tanjavur District
Subrahmaṇya temple—on the side of the first gōpura, right of entrance
Kṛishṇadēvarāya. Śaka 1436: 1514-15 A.D.
inscription.p:
The inscription is dated in Bhāva, Tulā. śu. Utthāna-dvādaśi, Monday.
Rēvati (A.D. 1514, October 30). The month should be Vriśchika.

This is damaged both at the beginning which contains one or two Sanskrit
verses and at the end. It seems to register a remission of some taxes by the king
in favour of god Svāminātha. At the top of the inscription are engraved the figures
of a triśūla, vajra and a peacock with a serpent held in its mouth.

edition.l: rēkaṣṭhāja••••••• [||*] cuvattu ciṟi cakātta-
m 1436- mel cellā niṉṟa pā-
rāca[ parameśvara] śrī virappiṟatāpa śrī ki-

inscription.head:
No. 497.
(A.R. No. 497 of 1907.)
Svāmimalai, Kumbhakonam Taluk, Tanjavur District
Subrahmaṇya temple—in the same place
Śevvappa-Nāyaka. Śaka 1495: 1573-74 A.D.
inscription.p:
This is built in the middle and also incomplete. The first and second sections
together seem to register a gift of 2 vēli of land by Śevvappa-Nāyakkar, son of
Timmappa-Nāyakkar of the Chaturtha-gōtra and a native of Neḍuṅguṉṟam in Toṇḍai-
maṇḍalam to the temple of Kumārasvāmi at Svāmimalai in Innambar-nāḍu Parāntaka-
nāḍu, a division of Vikramaśōḻa-vaḷanāḍu of Tiruchchirāppaḷḷi-chāvaḍi for the
abhishēka service of the god and for burning a ‘ghee-lamp’ in the temple, and
also for the maintenance of a maṭha called the Vēlāyudhachchivaṉ-maḍam.

A third piece which seems to be part of another inscription is also found
close by which mentions Timmuguḍi in Vēmbattūr-śīrmai and gift of a quarter
(vēli) of land for the sandhi-abhishēka (of the same god ?) and for a lamp.

The date of the inscription (of Śevvappa-Nāyaka) is given as Tulā, śu.
Paurṇamāsi, Saturday, Aśvati (=1573 A.D., October 10).

edition.l:
śubhamastu svastiśrī [||*] cakāttam 1495 itaṉmel cellāniṉ— vaa
sarattu tulā nāyaṟṟu pūrvapakṣattu pavurṇamāsiyum taiy mantavāra-

inscription.head:
No. 498.
(A.R. No. 498 of 1907.)
Vijayanagara (Hampi), Hospet Taluk, Bellary District
Kṛishṇasvāmi temple—on a slab set up in the premises
Kṛishṇadēvarāya. Śaka 1435
This is the same as No. 25 of 1889
inscription.p: Published in S.I.I., Vol. IV, No. 254

inscription.head:
No. 499.
(A.R. No. 499 of 1907.)
Vijayanagara (Hampi), Hospet Taluk, Bellary District
Chaṇḍikēśvara temple—on the broken slab built into the floor
Sadāśivarāya. Śaka 1467
This is the same as No. 35 of 1889
inscription.p: Published in S.I.I., Vol. IV, No. 265

inscription.head:
No. 500.
(A.R. No. 500 of 1907.)
Vijayanagara (Hampi), Hospet Taluk, Bellary District
On a broken slab in the underground temple
Kṛishṇadēvarāya. Śaka 1435
inscription.p: Published in S.I.I., Vol. IX, No. 491

inscription.head:
No. 501.
(A.R. No. 501 of 1907.)
Vijayanagara (Hampi), Hospet Taluk, Bellary District
On a rock near the Jaina temples
inscription.p:
This records the gift of a dīpamāle (lights) to a god (name not clear) by
Bukkayave the ardhāṅgalakshmi (queen) of Vīra Harihararāya (Harihara II) for the
merit of her spiritual teacher Bhāskaradēvayya. The date is given as Īśvara
(Śaka 1319), Mārgaśira śu. [9], Tuesday = A.D. 1397, November 29, Thursday
(not Tuesday).

inscription.head:
No. 502.
(A.R. No. 502 of 1907.)
Madurai, Madurai Taluk, Madurai District
Madanagōpāla temple—on a detached stone lying in the premises
Name of King lost. year 38
inscription.p:
This is a fragment of a bigger inscription in Vaṭṭeḻuttu alphabet, recording
the gift of a mā and half of land and 100 sheep to provide for the ardhayāma
(midnight) service and for burning a perpetual lamp in the temple of Tirutturutti-
Mahādēva. The donor's name is not known. Perumūr in Aṇḍa-nāḍu and another
village Pāṇḍiyūr are mentioned.

inscription.head:
No. 503.
(A.R. No. 503 of 1907.)
Madurai, Madurai Taluk, Madurai District
Madanagōpāla temple—on another stone
Śaḍaiya-Māṟaṉ. year 2+1
inscription.p:
This is a damaged Vaṭṭeḻuttu fragment of an inscription of Śaḍaiya-Māṟaṉ
(Rājasiṁha) recording a gift of land to the deity Tirutturutti-bhaṭāra in Perumūr in
Aṇḍa-nāḍu.
Published in S.I.I., Vol. XIV, No. 48

inscription.head:
No. 504.
(A.R. No. 504 of 1907.)
Madurai, Madurai Taluk, Madurai District
Madanagōpāla temple—on a third stone
inscription.p:
This is also a Veṭṭeḻuttu inscription dated in the * +1+1st year of a king
whose name is lost. It records a gift of 100 sheep for a perpetual lamp in the
temple at Tirutturutti a dēvadāna in Aṇḍa-nāḍu, by a lady named Kuṉṟañjundari of
Koṟṟavāyil-śēri in Māḍa-Madirai.

inscription.head:
No. 505.
(A.R. No. 505 of 1907.)
Madurai, Madurai Taluk, Madurai District
Madanagōpāla temple—on a fourth stone
inscription.p:
This is a fragment in Veṭṭeḻuttu recording a gift of cows by a Military officer
(paḍaīttalaīvaṉ).

inscription.head:
No. 506.
(A.R. No. 506 of 1907.)
Madurai, Madurai Taluk, Madurai District
Madanagōpāla temple—on eight other stones in the same place
inscription.p:
These are disconnected pieces of a few inscriptions. One of these is dated in
the 11+1st year of a king whose surname Māṟavarman alone is found. King
Parākrama-Pāṇḍyadēva is mentioned in a piece which might be a part of this record,
and which registers a royal order granting some land as dēvadāna to a temple.
Another piece mentions a certain••• Pāṇḍya-Nṛittappēraiyaṉ. A still different piece
seems to be a record of a gift of land made by (?) one Śivagōchari-Mahādēvaṉ of
the Bhāradvāja-gōtram known by the appellation Śaivāchārya.

l.ta:
ko[m]ā[ṟapa]ṉmarāṉa tiripuvaṉacca—vaṟku yāṇṭu patiṉoṉ
ṟā[vatiṉ] etirāmā—

pūrvapakṣattu••••••••• peṟṟa [a]ttattuṉāḷ—racaṅ k[ā*]ñaiyirukkai me
nallūr nāyaṉār•• yuṭaiya pa-

kkalum aruḷāḷaṉ ke••••••••• ṭṭār pakkaluñ ciritaraṉeriyi—••• ṉeri
yum parākkiramapāṇṭiya tevar pā-

ṉ kelkaikkuṭpaṭṭatum••••ṉārkkut tevatāṉa[m*] ākak kalve—
••• ḷaṅkaḷukkut tiruvāykkeḻviyun tirumukamun

—tukkuk kaṭamai antarāyam viṉiyokaṅ kaṟpūravilai poṉvari

inscription.head:
No. 507.
(A.R. No. 507 of 1907.)
Madurai, Madurai Taluk, Madurai District
Madanagōpāla temple—on the 13th stone in the same place
Tribh. Śrīvallabha—year 3
inscription.p:
This records a gift of land after purchase, as maḍappuṟam by queen Ulaga-
muḻuduḍaiyār. The name of the temple is not given, neither the details of
the land.

inscription.head:
No. 508.
(A.R. No. 508 of 1907.)
Madurai, Madurai Taluk, Madurai District
Madanagōpāla temple—on the 14th stone in the same place
inscription.p:
This states that this (i.e. the place where the stone should have originally
stood) is the flower-garden of (endowed by) Ēḻulagumuḻudumuḍaiyāḷ. The stone
bears at the top the Pāṇḍya crest viz., two fish with a triśūla between them and above
them all the figures of the crescent and Sun.

inscription.head:
No. 509.
(A.R. No. 509 of 1907.)
Madurai, Madurai Taluk, Madurai District
Madanagōpāla temple—on the 15th and 16th stones in the same place
inscription.p:
These are two mere fragments, the first of which seems to mention a
dēvadāna with kaḍamai and kuḍimai and the second, a temple at••••• ḍainallūr.

edition.l: (1/2)- vatu

inscription.head:
No. 510.
(A.R. No. 510 of 1907.)
Madurai, Madurai Taluk, Madurai District
Madanagōpāla temple—on 13 other stones in the same place
inscription.p:
There are actually 15 stones (not 13) on which these inscribed portions are
engraved. Their sequence with each other is not very clear partly because the
stones are all dis-arranged and some of them are mutilated and damaged. They
have been, however, so grouped into four sections, each with a few pieces, that the
conten's of each line of a piece may be read in continuity with the corresponding
line of other pieces. The inscription is dated in Kaliyuga 4[6]47, corresponding to
Śaka 1468 and cyclic year Parābhava and purports to give the genealogy of
Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Rāmarāja-Tirumalaya-Mahārāja i.e., Tirumala I, the Karnāṭa
king beginning from Vishṇu and coming down through a long line of chiefs. The
object of the inscription seems to be the establishment of a hundred villages for
Brāhmaṇas—both Vaishṇava and others—and apportionment of shares of land
for their living; by Tirumala who is stated also to have provided for worship in Śiva
and Vishṇu temples and for their repairs. The temples and the lands are not
specifically known.

inscription.head:
No. 511.
(A.R. No. 511 of 1907.)
Madurai, Madurai Taluk, Madurai District
Madanagōpāla temple—on the west wall
inscription.p:
This is dated in Śaka 1520. Hēviḷambi, Vṛiśchika 22, Saptami, Saturday,
Pushya corresponding to A.D. 1597, November 21(?). It was a Monday and the
Nakshatra was Maghā. It is an unfinished inscription purporting to register an
order issued to the king (?) as the Son (Naṅkumāraṉ), by saint-goddess Gōdā while
she was seated with her lord of Śrīraṅgam (Vaḍaperuṅkōyil) on the paḷḷikkaṭṭil
throne called “Aḻagiya-Maṇavāḷaṉ” in her home at Villipputtūr. The object of
the record is not known as it stops abruptly. In the Sanskrit verse with which the
inscription opens, she is called the daughter of Vishṇuchitta (Periyāḻvār).

l.gr:
hitam dēvyā śrī raṅkanāthasya
gōdāyāḥ di[vya]śāsanam | yathāsthānya
l.ta:
māṉa meṉṉaṭaiyaṉṉam parantu
viḷaiyāṭu villiputtūril namviṭṭil muttuṭaittā••• rai tāḻnta
pantalkkiḻ aḻakiyamaṇavāḷaṉ paḷḷikkaṭṭil nāmum nam vaṭa-

edition.l:
peruṅkovil paḷḷikkoṇṭaṟuḷiya paramasvāmikaḷum nam aṭiyāṟku
aḻakolakkam kolakkam koṭuttuk koṇṭi(i)rukkaiyil śakābdam
1520- l mel cellāniṉṟa ēviḷampi samvatsarattu vṛśika
nāyaṟṟu irupattiraṇṭān tetiyum

inscription.head:
No. 570.
(A.R. No. 570 of 1907.)
Nandalūru, Rajampet Taluk, Cuddapah District
Saumyanātha temple—on the south base of the maṇḍapa
in front of the central shrine
inscription.p:
This begins with the Sanskrit introduction Charaṇasarōruha etc. It records an
order of (the Telugu Chōḍa chief) Madhurāntaka Pottappichchōḻa Nallam Siddharasa
to the Mahājanas of Nirandaṉūr which he calls his brahmapuri, restoring to them
the village within the four boundaries, which had been originally granted to them by
Vatsarāja and Siddharasa's ancestors and declaring that the Veḷḷāḷas (probably then
in illegal occupation) had no title to the village. The charter of possession is said to
have been made by the chief in the presence of god Śokkapperumāḷ. There seems
to be some gap in the body of the record.

l.gr:
svasti caraṇasarōruha vihita pilōcana trilōcana pramukhākhila
pṛthviśvarakārita kāvēritira karikālakularatnapradipāhita kumārāmku-

eṉpiḷḷaikaḷum ivvūr catu

iṭai kumari iṭai kurālpacu kuttiṉāl pāvattil
paṭuvāṉ [||*]
l.gr:
svadattām paradattām vā yō harēti vasundharām ṣaṣṭim varṣa
sahasrāṇi

eḻuttu

neḻuttu ivai ca

inscription.head:
No. 571.
(A.R. No. 571 of 1907.)
Nandalūru, Rajampet Taluk, Cuddapah District
Saumyanātha temple—on the same base
Rājādhirāja (II) year 12: A.D. 1175
inscription.p:
The date is given as Hēviḷambi, Makara. śu. 1. Saturday, Aviṭṭam corres-
ponding to A.D. 1178, January 21.

The ends of the first two lines are lost. The inscription records the gift of 62
kuḻi of arecanut garden by one Paṭṭinaṅkiḻāṉ Dēvar to provide for the offering of
tiruppōnagam to the god Kulōttuṅga[śōḻa-Viṇṇagar-Āḻvār] at Kulōttuṅgaśōḻa-
chaturvēdimaṅgalam an agara-brahmadēya of Niṟandanūr in Mēṟpākkai-nāḍu, a
division of Adhirājēndra-maṇḍalam.

edition.l:
svastiśrī [||*] rājādhirājadēvaṟku yāṇṭu 12- āvatāṉa hēmalambisampatsa
rattu makaraṉāyaṟṟu pūrvapakṣattu prathamaiyum canikkiḻamaiyum peṟṟa
aviṭṭattunāḷ adhirājēntra maṇṭalattu meṟpākkai nāṭṭu niṟanta
nūr akarabrahmadēyam śrīkulottuṅkacoḻac catuṟvetimaṅkalattuk
kulottuṅka•••••••••

m [pu]ṟkilattu śrī••••vakkum vaḻinaṭaiyāka
cellakkaṭavitākavum itukku ākac ceyya veḷ marumakaṉ [pa]ṭṭiṉaṅ
kiḻāṉ tevareṉ ivvūr nampai koval(l)paṭ[ṭar] uḷḷiṭṭār
pakkal vilaikoṇṭu uṭaiyeṉāy eṉnutāyiru

kiḻakkaṭaiya araikkālil vaṭakkaṭaiya kuḻi

inscription.head:
No. 572.
(A.R. No. 572 of 1907.)
Nandalūru, Rajampet Taluk, Cuddapah District
Saumyanātha temple—on the 2nd, 3rd and 4th tiers of the south base
Kulōttuṅga II year 8: 1139-40 A.D.
inscription.p:
The inscription begins with the introduction Pūmēvu Tirumagaḷ etc. Portions
are built in. This is stated to be the charter of the dēvadāna lands of god
Kulōttuṅgaśōḻa-Viṇṇagar-Āḻvār and of the demarcation of the four boundaries of
the village Kulōttuṅgaśōḻa-chaturvēdimaṅgalam granted to the Sabhā by the chief
Madhurāntaka Pottappichchōḻa Siddharasa. A list of the former is given in which
are included wet and dry lands coconut and areca garden, flower garden and deserted
land (nattappāḻ) and residential areas. (Of the latter?) the charter confirms 52
share-holders in their possession as sarvanamasya recorded as granted in
No. 573 below.

inscription.head:
No. 573.
(A.R. No. 573 of 1907.)
Nandalūru, Rajampet Taluk, Cuddapah District
Saumyanātha temple—on the 4th tier of the south base
inscription.p:
This is built in at the beginning and is also incomplete. In the body of the
inscription is mentioned the 8th year of Kulōttuṅga-Chōḷa with details of date
Kumbha, ba. 14, Friday, Śrāvaṇa, Śivarātri corresponding to A.D. 1141, February 7.
The nakshatra was of the previous day when it ended at ·88 of that day.

On this date Madhurāntaka Pottappichchōḻa Siddharasaṉ is stated to have
granted house-sites and lands to 52 Brāhmaṇas and a flower garden (for the temple).
With this is found a two line piece of a different inscription dated in Virōdhikṛit year,
recording some gift (of money) to the temple at Kulōttuṅgaśōḻa-chaturvēdi-
maṅgalam by a certain Malaikiniyaniṉṟāṉ Ādinādaṉ of Ōymā-nāḍu to secure for
himself ‘long life, health and prosperity’.

inscription.head:
No. 574.
(A.R. No. 574 of 1907.)
Nandalūru, Rajampet Taluk, Cuddapah District
Saumyanātha temple—on the 6th tier of the same base
Kulōttuṅga year 4 (Plava)
inscription.p:
This is an incomplete inscription with stones peeled off to a great extent in the
middle. It seems to register a gift of land to provide for offerings to Śokkapperumāḷ
by cheyya Śivapūdaṉ Madusūdaṉaṉ of Kōyambēḍu in Māṅgāṭṭu-nāḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 575.
(A.R. No. 575 of 1907.)
Nandalūru, Rajampet Taluk, Cuddapah District
Saumyanātha temple—on the lower most portion of the south wall
of the same maṇḍapa
inscription.p:
This gives a list of the tiruviḍaiyāṭṭam lands at Pottappi belonging to god
Śokkapperumāḷ of Nirandaṉūr. These comprised seven plots measuring in all
7050 kuḻi in extent.

inscription.head:
No. 576.
(A.R. No. 576 of 1907.)
Nandalūr, Rajampet Taluk, Cuddapah District
Saumyanātha temple—on the same wall
Kulōttuṅga III year 33: 1209—10 A.D.
inscription.p:
The king is given the appellation “who took Madurai and the crowned head of
the Pāṇḍya”. The details of date are given as Prajāpati year, Vaigāśi month. This
states that one Tirunāḍuḍaiyāṉ Ponnaṉ alias Kulōttuṅgaśōḻa-Kāḍuveṭṭi of
Veṇkaṟchēṟṟuppēḍu in Veṇkala-nāḍu a division of Puḻarkōṭṭam alias Vikramaśōḻa-
vaḷanāḍu in Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻa-maṇḍalam presented to the temple of Śokkapperumāḷ the
karmārchchanai images of god Aḻagapperuṁāḷ and his two consorts together with an
aureola and with the additional copper that remained he made other articles of
worship like tīrtta-maṇḍai, śrībalittālam, tirumaṇḍai, etc.

l.ta:
kalattuc cokkapperumāḷ koyilāṉa śrī kulottuṅkacoḻa viṇ-
ṇakarkku

kala nāṭṭu veṇkaṟceṟṟu[p*]peṭaṉ tirunāṭuṭaiyāṉ poṉṉaṉāṉa
kulottuṅ-

ceṣamākak kuṭuttaṉa cempāl tirttamaṇṭai oṉṟum śrīpalittāla
mo-
ṉṟum tirumaṇṭai yoṉṟum caṅkoṉṟum kāloṉṟum caṭṭuvamo-

iraṇṭum(ṉ) tanikkāḷa moṉṟum ciṉṉamiraṇṭum
paṇṇa iṭṭā[r*] [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 577.
(A.R. No. 577 of 1907.)
Nandalūru, Rajampet Taluk, Cuddapah District
Saumyanātha temple—on the 2nd, 3rd and 4th tiers of the
west base of the same maṇḍapa
inscription.p:
The inscription comprises three disjointed pieces. This is much damaged. It
purports to record a charter given by Bhujabala Vīranārāyaṇa Āhavamalladēva-
Mahārāja to all the mahājanas of Pottapi-nāḍu, the nature of which is not clear.
Mention is made of two families of Śōḻappāḍi supplying oil to the temple for lamps,
who seem to have been deprived of some privileges enjoyed since the time of their
forebears and hence threatened to commit suicide (?) and were dissuaded from such
a step by Āhavamalla himself by restoring them now. We are not vouch-safed the
details of this incident by the mutilated state of the record. The record is signed at
the end by three officers of the king, two of whom are given as Divyādhikāri
Bhāskara-Bhaṭṭōpādhyāya of the [Va]sishṭha-gōtra, son of Nārāyaṇārya and Vāsudēva-
Bhaṭṭa, son of Appaṇa-Bhaṭṭōpādhyāya. The third whose name is lost is called the
dharmādhikāri of Āhavamalladēva.

l.ta:
lai kiḻppakal taṅkaḷukku•••••• ṭiṇaṅka uṅkaḷukku mulakā•••••• ḷḷutum
kaṇattā•••••••••

rya sūnu•••••

inscription.head:
No. 578.
(A.R. No. 578 of 1907.)
Nandalūru, Rajampet Taluk, Cuddapah District
Saumyanātha temple—on the north base of the same maṇḍapa, 1st tier
Kulōttuṅga III year 26: 1202-03 A.D.
inscription.p:
The King is given the appellation “who took Madurai and the crowned head
of the Pāṇḍya”. The inscription gives the cyclic year Raktākshi and month Āṉi
and registers the gift of three villages as agrahāra exempting them from taxes like
silvari and peruvari, by Nallaṉ Siddharasa, son of Madhurāntaka Pottappichchōḻa
alias Eṟama-Siddharasa. These villages were Nirandanūr alias Kulōttuṅgaśōḻa-
chaturvēdimaṅgalam, Āṇḍappūr alias Tyāgasamudra-chaturvēdimaṅgalam and
Mantram alias Bhujabalasiddhi-chaturvēdimaṅgalam.

l.gr:
jayanti siddharājasya caṇḍādōrdaṇḍavikramāḥ viśvaviśvambharā rakṣādīkṣāddhvaradhura
ndharāḥ—

l.ta:
varai eppeṟpaṭṭa cilvari peruvari
taviṟntu akaramākak kuṭutteṉ itukku aḻivuceyvār

k
karai āyiraṅ kurāṟpacuk kuttiṉāṉ pāpattilp paṭuvār-ippaṭik
kivai nallaṉ

inscription.head:
No. 579.
(A.R. No. 579 of 1907.)
Nandalūru, Rajampet Taluk, Cuddapah District
Saumyanātha temple—on the 2nd and 3rd tiers of the north base and
on the three tiers of the west base of the same maṇḍapa
Vikrama-Chōḷa. year 8, Śaka 1047 Viśvāvasu: A.D. 1125-26
inscription.p:
The inscription begins with the introduction Pūmādu puṇara etc. This
registers the gift of the village Maṇṇūr alias Vikramaśōḻa-chaturvēdimaṅgalam in
Mēṟpākkai-nāḍu, a division of Adhirājēndramaṇḍalam, as an agrahāra of 70 shares
to several learned Brāhmaṇas, for securing success to the king's arms, by the chief
Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Vimalādityadēva who is stated to have been ruling Pottappi-nāḍu.
He was the son of Siddharasa and was called Madhurāntaka Pottappichchōḻa. A list
of all the donees is given with the number of shares each got, being specified.

l.ta:
pūmātu puṇara puvimātu vaḷara nāmātu viḷaṅka jayamātu
virumpat tantiruppatu malar maṉnavarcūṭa maṉniya vuri[m]aiyil maṇi
muṭi cūṭic ceṅkol ticaitoṟuñ cella veṅkali kaṭi

ḻaippak kaliṅka miriyakkaṭarmalai naṭātti valaṅkolāḻi varai
ūḻi naṭātti iricuṭaraḷavu morukuṭainiḻaṟṟac cempoṉvira

l.gr:
sarōruha vihitavilōcana pramukhākhila prithiviśvarakārita kāvērī tīra
karikā

maṅkalam koḷḷi
miṟuvuḷp paṭṭu vūr nālppāle[l*]laikkumuḷḷuḷḷa nañcaipuñcai
nilamuḷḷutum kāṭummeṭum malaikaḷum kuḻikaḷum

l.gr:
śvamurvagnibhīṣaṇam viduṣāmidam ||—svastī śrī siddhibhūpasya sū
nūnā sūrya tējasā pimalāditya dēvēna śāsanam śāśvatīkṛtam ||—
jayanti vimalādityadēvasya pṛthukīrttayaḥ dhava-

bīkrata vistīrṇa viśvabrahmāṇamaṇḍalāḥ ||—svadattām paradattām vā yō
harata vasundharām ṣaṣṭim varṣasahasrāṇi viṣṭāyām jāyatē kṛmiḥ ||—
bahūbhi rvasudhā dattā rājabhi ssagarādibhiḥ
yasya yasya yadā bhūmi stasya tasyatathā chalam ||—

inscription.head:
No. 580.
(A.R. No. 580 of 1907.)
Nandalūru, Rajampet Taluk, Cuddapah District
Saumyanātha temple—on the 5th tier of the north base, the north and
west walls of the same maṇḍapa and the north wall of the central shrine
Rājēndra-Chōḷa III year 13: 1257-58 A.D.
inscription.p:
The inscription is also dated in Saka 1179, Piṅgala, Mēsha-Saṅkrānti. It is
written in three languages, the first part being in Sanskrit; the next comprising
mostly of the names of the donees with the number of their shares of land, is in
Telugu but in Grantha alphabet like the first part; and the last portion of about
20 lines, giving the main gist of the record is in Tamil. The epigraph begins with a
eulogy of a king by name Triṇētra (Trilōchana-Pallava ?) who founded many
agrahāras to the east of the Tripurāntaka hill. A successor of his of the same name-
Mukkaṇṭi-Kāḍuveṭṭi established and gave the village Peruṅgaṇḍūr in Paśchima-
Pāka-nāḍu a division of Adhirājendra-Chōḷa-maṇḍalam to 52 Brāhmaṇas in Saka 723
(mistake for Śaka 730 ?), Sarvadhāri, Mēsha-Saṅkramaṇa, ba. 6, Wednesday, Mūlā
(A.D. 808, March 21, Tuesday ?). The donees were in enjoyment of their shares for
a long time, when some Velumas from Inumbrōlu escaping from the māri-jvaram of
their place, settled in fields near them, agreeing to pay rents for their lands, along-
side the residents of Sakali-Kōḍūra who had also emigrated from their place on
account of some riots. During a famine that followed the Brāhmaṇas left their places
and when they returned found themselves supplanted in their possessions by the new
comers who had in the meantime named their new settlement as Kōḍūru, and refused
to give the rent due to the Brāhmaṇa landlords. The latter then made a represen-
tation to the chief Manumasiddhi, whose genealogy is here introduced viz Dāyabhīma,
Bētabhūpa, Eṟasiddhi, Manmasiddha, and Tikkanṛipa the father of the ruling chief.
This Manumasiddhi is said to have conquered a chief named Vijaya and tried to
secure the friendship of Kākatīya-Gaṇapati by fighting a battle for him on the banks
of the Gōdāvari. He was a feudatory under the Chōḷa king Rājēndra-Chōḷa III
whose regnal year is quoted.

Manumasiddhi sent for the cultivators against whom the complaint was made
and after due enquiry with witnesses, decided the case in favour of the dispossessed
Brāhmaṇas, to whom he renewed the grant of the village Kōḍūru for the merit of his
father. Then follows a detailed list of all the share-holders, in Telugu. The concluding
portion of the inscription which is in Tamil registers an order of Madurāntaka-
Pottappi-Chōḷa Manumasiddhi, granting the village Kōḍūru a hamlet of Peruṅgaṇḍūr
to the Brāhmaṇas, thus restoring their ancient possessions, when the inhabitants
from Inumbuḍōl (Inumbrōlu) could not prove their case.

l.gr:
svasti [||*] lakṣmī kaustubha śītāmśu gaurī sahacaram mahaḥ brāhmaṇānām
gavāñcaiva jāgartti svasti karmmaṇi | nētratrayēṇa sabhayastrīpurāntakasya
nētrādajāyata jagattraya hṛttṛ tīyā[t] nāmnā triṇētraiti sa tripurā
ntakādrēḥprācyāndiśi vyadhita saptaśatāgrahārān āsthāni maṇimaṇṭapa
ñjayavatē[ṣvarajñaḥ]kēbibhūḥ kīrttērjja nm[ā]graham parākramasarit purasya
mēghōdayaḥ vēlāvairicamū taraṅga puṣa

tasyakṣma••••••• laya

maṇē dinē sthi[tē] adhirājēndracōḷamaṇḍalē paścimapāka ••••

karttā tatkulē samajāyata tasyānvavāyē mahati bijjaṇō nāma sajjanaḥ
śūraśśauryyavatām••• •

—pōbhavat [d]āyabhīmastatō jajñē bētabhūpastatōbhavat bētabhūpāda
bhūdērasiddha ssiddhimatām varaḥ tasmādajani rājēndrō manmasiddhamahīpatiḥ yaśaḥ
pāṇḍaritāśēṣadigantasthāsitā satām tasyā bhūttanaya ssamastajagatī dāmpatya
sampattimā ṅēvastikkanṛpaḥ parājitamasavṛtya ruṣapōṣōdayaḥ yaddōrvikrama
vastavistṛtakathāgāthā kṣarāla[mkṛ]tamhastē pustakamambūjāsanavadhū
rddhattēdya vidyārtthinī kāñcīm vikramasimhanāmanagarī nāthaḥ praśāsat balāccam
pāyām puridōrbalādyudhi śaraissōmēśvara[m] pīḍayan karnnāṭapramadāvilō
canabhavaiścakrē bhavērātmajā ntōyaiḥ kajjaḷakābikar[ā]lubitaissadyaḥ
kaḷa••• tmajām-tasmādajāyata jagattraya—

—yamaulimālālōlābijālaparicāritapādapīṭhaḥ yasmāt parājitimatānvitajayēna
rājñā bhītyā tvavāhibahuyōjana sammitō ddhvāmārggistathā manunarēśvaracōdi
taśca samcōdyamāna hṛdayō jijīviṣābhiḥ yassāhāyyam vidhitsurggaṇapati
nṛpatē ssvēcphayā saṅgaragrē gōdāvaryyām sariti nṛpatiñca[mū]yaṣṭyā
nivṛtya kāliṅgam svā•• liṅgānabhimukhamakarō dēkavira stadānīntaṭhēstha kasmai-
cidasmai jagati narapati sthiṣṭhatē saṅgarāgrē asmin vikramasimhākhyē nagarē
[yām praśāsati manmasidyābhidhē r[ā*]jiñ[ja]nayajyāyasi tējasā-pēnuṅga-
puri catusimmanuka mōdalu tūrpuna kōṇḍūri-

drōvam īvaṭṭa dēkṣaṇāna vikkalamaḍamaḍa paḍumaṭa vēduru[gu]mi agnēyamuna
iṇḍūrikini yūrikini ēl

ī vēdurugumi paḍamaṭayuta udaka pāti

niguṇṭa ī[da]kṣi

maddi dōrapu paḍumaṭa siddāpurānakunu īyūriki ē

la ī dōrapu
uttarānarā[ṣa]nnayiruti uttarāna kēdāradēvariguḍi uttarāna pāti nayiruti
paḍumaṭa mūṅka māmiḷḷu māmiḷḷa u—

maṅgalidōrapu dēkṣaṇāna i[nda]pakhi[p]ādamulu kōla-
nu kōlanu ēḍu kōlalu viḍucina pakkapakkitūrpuna[guṇṭa] dōrapu

lakupōṅgadrōva īśānyamunagunu prōlikini īyūrikini ēl

īka
ṇḍay[ē]•• dakṣiṇāna guñjani paḍumaṭi dharamuku•• na

mma āgnēyamu na
pāṣāṇa pa[ñci] āgnēyamuna vēdurugumi idi īyūri catussimānamukaṇṭi
kāḍuvēṭṭi[ndra] viḍicinanu īyūri ēbhavirēṇḍu vṛttilunu ībhaṅga
pēddakālamu anubhaviñcucunuṇḍaṅgānu sakalikōḍurivāru tamadēśamu
kalahamayina vala savaccicē

—tambhukō[vi]nanu inumprōli vēlumalu tama ūru mārijvaramu ayina
māku ayēdaṭa niluvarādu ēmu īgaḍusalalōm kāpu uṇḍi mikṣētra
muḍuna mīku arikoru vēṭṭi uṇḍadamani arikōru pēruvānuṇḍaṅgāna
antakuminaśanikṣāmmuna niluvarāka dēśāndramu pōyinanu kōḍūra ni pēru
[p]ēṭṭi[k]ōni uṇḍaṅgānu ēmu kṣāmam tēliyaṅgāvicci mākṣētramu ēmpadi
arikō[ru]pēṭṭumanna pēṭṭaka māsāsanahinamavaṭa ēṛiṅgi rācādhinamu
sēsi cārika arikōru paṭṭakōni uṇḍaṅgānu manumasiddhirājanaku ēmu
ī paṭṭāṅgu vinnapamu cēsina rājuna avadharimci inabrōli vēlumala
piluvampanni pākanāṭi bhūmivārini ōddapēṭṭikōnipra—

—ma kṣētramu avunaṭṭukānu maḍḍapaṭṭadamaniyada rūmirupiri paṭṭiñca•••••
••••••••• ri anaṅgānu rājuna bhūmivārun idi brāhmala kṣētramu avu
nani nirnayiñcī śakābamula vēyi[nūṭi ēḍu]va tōmmidi pēṭṭana virarā-
jēndracōḷa dēvaniki pammaḍavaga śrāhi ayina [piṅgala] samvatsaram mēṣasam
[krānti]••• taṇḍri tirukāḷadēva mahārājulaku dharmmavukāna ī prāpa
ayina kōḍūru dhāravōsi iccī sāsanamu iccī māku pūrvamu vṛttulu
ēbhavirēṇṭu lōna danu ākālamu

ri samudāya mukkam krayamu kōnna
vṛttulu śrīvatsagōtramu kuṇḍi dāmōdara bhaṭṭacēta krayamu kōntabhāgamu
ōkkaṭini—

—kagōtramu nārāyaṇa appayacētakrayamu kō••••••••• kṛmikandanāricētam
krayamu kōnna[bh]āgamu ōkaṭiku ī nāluku bhāgamuluku u•••••••••
dravyamu valasi

upāyamu•••••••• kāla ucitamayinamūlyamunaku ammina
bhāgamulu muppaviā

nu pūrvavṛttulu nālupavi ēnumidini iṭṭuka
barasi ēnambhavi nālugu cēsigṛhakṣētramulu ōkkabhaṅgicēsi uṇḍya-
payiccīkōni anubhavi—

—bhāgamu ōkkaṭi kōṇḍēri sarvaṇa••••••••• tyabhaṭṭa bhāgamu ōkkaṭi [mu]ga-
ga[ṇa]sōmiśvarabhaṭṭa bhugamulu [rēṇḍu]••••••••• cōkkabhaṭṭa bhāgamu•••••••••
ṇama bhāgamu ōkkaṭi māriya śrī dharapēddi bhāgamulu rēṇḍu mārē ṣaḍaṅgara
bhāgamu ōkkaṭi cinnēri nārāyaṇa pēddi bhāgamu ōkkaṭi vē[rē]
mu ōkkaṭi pēmmanamañci bhāgam rundhatma vēntayabha-
bhāgam ara-mārayakamacci bhāgamu kālu pālaya kōṇṭani
ḍamannaya bhaṭṭubhāgamu ara-gajjula bhāskara dēvani bhāgamu ō-
kaṭi nārāyaṇa bhaṭṭa sōmiśvara appayabhāgamu ara-ca-
ṇayabhaṭṭu vēnnayapēddi bhāgamu kālu ādityabhaṭṭu īśva-
yaṇa appaya bhāgamu kālu-kōṇḍapēddi bhāgamu kālu-kō-
bhaṭṭu bhāgamu ōkkaṭi-āditya caturvēdula bhāgamu ōkkaṭi vi-
pēddi samkiśvarappēddi bhāgamu ara-aṇṇama narasimha kūcci
bhāgamu ara-kēśavamañci nārāyaṇani bhāgamukālu aṇṇama apva-
ya bhāgamu ara-tripurayapēddi ādityani bhāgamukālu-pālaya a-
ppanappēddi bhāgamu kālu-[va]rakuccī pālayabhaṭṭa bhāgamu kālu-ja-
madagni vatsagōtram kāmaṇamu adityappēddi bhāgamu ōkkaṭi-ku-

edition.l: ṭṭu allāḷapēddi bhāgamu mukkālu- kumaraya

l.gr: mu ōkkaṭi bhāradvāja gōtram janārddana bha
ṇaya bhaṭṭubhāgamu ōkkaṭi appaṇasōmēdula bhāgamalu rē-

mañci trivikramabhaṭṭubhāgamu ara-pōttē bhaṭṭu prōlēya appaya

edition.l: bhāgamu mākāṇa- ātraya gōtram dōṇaya bhaṭṭu bhāgamu ōkkaṭi

l.gr: kandaṇa kramitai bhāgamu ōkkaṭi-kandarpa kēśavappēddi bhāgamu
nnamku cēluvē bhāgamu ōkkaṭi-gautama gōtram hē-

l.gr: tān bhāvinaḥ pārtthivēndrān bhūyō bhūyō yācatē rā-
dānāt svargamavāpnōti pālanādacyutam padam [||*] svadattām pa[ra]-
dattām vā yō harēta vasundharām [ṣa*]ṣṭim varṣasahasrāṇi vi-

inscription.head:
No. 581.
(A.R. No. 581 of 1907.)
Nandalūru, Rajampet Taluk, Cuddapah District
Saumyanātha temple—on the 4th tier of the same base
Kulōttuṅga-Chōla III. Year 31: 1208-09 A.D.
inscription.p:
The cyclic year is also given as Vibhava. This records a gift of two lamp-
stands worth 15 māḍai and 180 kuḻi of land for burning two perpetual lamps in the
temple of Kulōttuṅgaśōḻa-Viṇṇagar-Emberumāṉ Sokkapperumāḷ for the merit of his
deceased mother Mēḍasāni by Bayirappi-Reḍḍi son of Bhūmi-Reḍḍi a maṉṟāḍuvār
(assembly member) of Taṅgaṭṭūr in Mēṟpākkai-nāḍu.

edition.l:
svastiśrī [||*] kulottuṅka coḻatevaṟku yāṇṭu 31- vatu āvatāna
vibhavasamvatsarattu āṉimāca matirācentiramaṇṭalattu meṟpākkai
ṉāṭṭut taṅkaṭṭūr maṉṟāṭuvār pūmireṭṭiyār makan payirappi
reṭṭiyen yeṅkaḷ tāyār meṭacāniyār cuvaṟkasta rāna piṉpu
avarukku naṉṟākaveṇumeṉṟu kulottuṅkacoḻaviṇṇakar em
perumān cokkapperumāḷukku iraṇṭu tirunaṉtāviḷakku nitta
cittamākac cellakkaṭavitāka ivvemperumān

tiruviṭaiyāṭṭamāna ṉāyakattevik kamukantoṭṭa ttukku vaṭakku tiru
viṭaiyā[ṭ*]ṭamāna maṭuttūrattuk kuṭutta nilam kuḻi 180 yinnilam
180- til pokattile patiṉañcu māṭaiyāle iraṇṭu tirukkutti
viḷakkuṅ koṇṭu yiṭṭu ittirunaṉtāviḷakku iraṇṭu meṟṟina
palam yeṅkaḷ tāyār meṭacāniyāṟku naṉṟāka ittiru naṉtā
viḷakku iraṇṭum aṉṟāṭu kūṭṭapperumakkaḷum śrīvaiṣaṇavarkaḷum
aṉusandhānam paṇṇi cantira ā-

inscription.head:
No. 582.
(A.R. No. 582 of 1907.)
Nandalūru, Rajampet Taluk, Cuddapah District
Saumyanātha temple—on the 1st tier of the east base of the
same maṇḍapa; right of entrance
Kulōttuṅga III. Year 31: 1208-09 A.D.
inscription.p:
The king is given the appellation ‘who took Madurai and the crowned head
of the Pāṇḍya’. The details of the date are given as Śukla, ba. 1, Tuesday, Svāti.
Chittirai-Vtshu corresponding to A.D. 1209, March 24, the vishu however being
next day. This records a gift of money (due to him as tax from the village on oil
and ghee) by Madhurāntaka-Pottappi-Chōḷa Tirukkāḷattidēva for perpetual lamps
in the temple of Śokkapperumāḷ at Nirandaṉūr-agaram Kulōttuṅgaśōḻa-chaturvēdi-
maṅgalam, for the merit of his father (ayyajiyar) Manumasiddarasa and (uncle)
Nallasiddarasa who, according to No. 578 above was the son of Eramasiddarasa as
also was Manumasiddarasa (No, 580).

l.gr:
[sva]sti siddharājasya caṇḍā dōrdaṇthavikramaḥ viśvaviśvambharā••• dikṣā [sā]
dhurandharāḥ [||*]
l.ta:
yum pāṇ
ṭiyaṉ muṭittalaiyuṅkoṇṭaruḷiya tirupuvaṉaccakkaravattikaḷ śrīkulot
tuṅka coḻatevaṟku

edition.l:
yāṇṭu 31- āvatāṉa śukla samvatsarattu aparapakṣattu prathamaiyum
cevvāykkiḻamaiyuñ c[oti]yum peṟṟa cittiraiviṣuviṉpotu
maturāntakap pottappic coḻan tirukkāḷattitevaṉeṉ niranta
ṉūrakaramāṉa kulottuṅka coḻaccatuṟpetimaṅkala-

l.ta:
viḷakkukaḷukku i[vūri]l eṇṇai
yālum neyyālum vanta [kācu] y[e]ṅka ḷayyajiyar manumacitta
racaṟkkum nalla cittaracaṟkum naṉṟākac ca

kkalveṭṭikkuṭutteṉ itukku a[kutañc]eytāruṇṭākil kaṅkaik
karaiyil āyiram kurālpacuk koṉṟa pāvattile viḻuvār .

inscription.head:
No. 583.
(A.R. No. 583 of 1907.)
Nandalūru, Rajampet Taluk, Cuddapah District
Saumyanātha temple—on the 2nd tier of the same base
[Vikrama-Chōḷa]. Year 3: 1120-21 A.D.
inscription.p:
The inscription is built in at the beginning and is also incomplete. The
introduction is that beginning with the words Pūmādu puṇara. It is dated in
Śaka 104[2], Śārvari, Chittirai-Vishu, when Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Bettarasa was ruling
Pottappi-nāḍu. The purport of the record is not clear except that the chief founded
an agrahāra for the success of the king's arms. Some reference is made to a
parigraha (seizure ?) in which two or three minor chieftains seem to have been
involved.

l.ta:
—ppat taṉ tirupatumalar maṉnavar cūṭa maṉniya urimaiyil maṇimuṭi
cūṭic ceṅkol ticaitoṟuñ cella veṅkalikaṭi

edition.l:
—kuśanayyanasimha śrīmanmahāmaṇḍaleśvara pettarasaṉ pottappi
nāṭāḷāniṟkka śakavarṣam āyirattu nāṟppattu [i]ra[ ṇṭāvatu śā]
rvari samvatsarattu citraiviṣuvi nāḷ nāṉ adhirājendramaṇṭalattu
meṟ-

l.ta:
—nāṭṭil imayatte [i]raṭṭakuṭiyum kāli[ṅka]nāṭāḻvāṉum•••
ṭināṭāḻvāṉum mattappatte [i]raṭṭakuṭiyu muḷḷiṭṭa i

inscription.head:
No. 584.
(A.R. No. 584 of 1907.)
Nandalūru, Rajampet Taluk, Cuddapah District
Saumyanātha temple—on the 3rd and 4th tiers of the same base
inscription.p:
This is also built in at the beginning. This seems to record the confirmation
of a grant of lands made probably by Eṛasiddharasar some time previously to twenty
Brāhmaṇas, ten from the south and ten from the north—settled in the agrahāra, by the
chief's son Madhurāntaka Pottappi Chōḷa Vimalāditya on the day of Chittirai-Vishu.

l.ta:
ma[ṅkala]ttukku melellai kiḷikkūṭṭumalai ekatirttam
tenkarai eḻuruvak kāmaccoḻa nallūr[ki]ṭpaṭak kiḻakkum vaṭakku
periyamalaiyaka••• maṭṭutta-

—meṟkkum teṟkku pākkai ellaikku vaṭakkumāka ivvūril nāl
pālellaikkuḷḷuḷḷa nañcai puñcai nilamum kāṭum meṭum maṭuvum
āṟuka-

inscription.head:
No. 585.
(A.R. No. 585 of 1907.)
Nandalūru, Rajampet Taluk, Cuddapah District
Saumyanātha temple—on the 2nd and 3rd tiers of the
same base; left of entrance
inscription.p:
This is built in at the end. The date portion is lost. It seems to record the
grant of a village by name Toṭṭimeṭṭai alias Śokkadēvaviḷāgam by the chief Mahā-
maṇḍalēśvara Siddharasa ‘ruling Pottappi-nāḍu,’ for the expenses of a festival in the
temple of Śokkapperumāḷ. The same donor probably made another gift in the next
year (for the formation of a road for the god's procession ?)

l.gr:
nāthasya śāsa
nam sarvamaṅgalam [||*] svasti caraṇasarōruhavihitavi—

l.ta:
maṇṭalattu meṟppākkai
ṉāṭṭu toṭṭimeyai āṉa cokkattevaviḷākamā[na]—

[*r]kka kālam cellakkaṭavitāka cokkap
perumāḷ tirumunne nirvārttuk kuṭutten maturā

kuttiṉāṉ vātattil paṭuvān itaṉ piṟṟai āṇṭu koṭaittiru
ṉāḷil paḻaiyapaṟaiyaṟaiccerikku kiḻakku••••ttaṭavi peruvaḻikku
teṟ—

[k]oṭaittiruṉāḷappi cellakkaṭavatāka cokkapperumāḷukku tiru
munne nirvārttukuṭutteṉ maturā

inscription.head:
No. 586.
(A.R. No. 586 of 1907.)
Nandalūru, Rajampet Taluk, Cuddapah District
Saumyanātha temple—on the 4th tier of the same base
Kulōttuṅga-Chōḷa III. Year 3: 1180-81 A.D.
inscription.p:
This is also built in at the end. It seems to record a gift of ten Bhujabalaṉ-
māḍai for burning two perpetual lamps in the temple by a native of Māṅgāḍu in
Kulōttuṅgaśōḻa-vaḷanāḍu a subdivision of Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻamaṇḍalam.

inscription.head:
No. 587.
(A.R. No. 587 of 1907.)
Nandalūru, Rajampet Taluk, Cuddapah District
Saumyanātha temple—on the 5th and 6th tiers of the same base
Kulōttuṅga-Chōḷa III. Year 5: 1182-83 A.D.
inscription.p:
The date is given as Śubhakṛit, Puraṭṭādi, ba•• This seems to be an unfinished
record, also built in at the right end, which mentions Śeyya Śivapūdaṇ Madiśūdanaṉ
Vānavanāyakaṉ of Kōyambēḍu in Māṅgāṭṭu-nāḍu a division of Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻa-
maṇḍalam figuring as a donor in No. 574 above; and also the village Nirandanūr
alias Kulōttuṅgaśōḻa-chaturvēdimaṅgalam in Mēṟpākkai-nāḍu, a division of Adhi-
rājēndra-maṇḍalam.

l.ta:
jayaṅkoṇṭa coḻamaṇṭalattu māṅkāṭṭu nāṭṭuk koyampeṭṭu
ceyyacivapūtaṉ maticūtanaṉ vāṉavanāyakaneṉ icce•••••••••

inscription.head:
No. 588.
(A.R. No. 588 of 1907.)
Nandalūru, Rajampet Taluk, Cuddapah District
Saumyanatha temple—on the south wall of the central shrine
Sundara Pāṇḍya. Year 17: 1292-93 A.D.
inscription.p:
This seems to be a record of Jaṭāvarman Sundara-Pāṇḍya II (acc. A.D. 1276).
The details of the date are given as Nandana, Mīna, śu. 10, Wednesday, Pushya
which would correspond to A.D. 1293; The record states that on this day one Karu-
māṇikkāḻvāṉ [Ja]ndivarmmar the headman of Śiṟumali residing at Toḻūr in Toḻūr-
nāḍu a division of Maṇavil-kōṭṭam in Jayangoṇḍaśōḻamaṇḍalam caused to be given
the sacred bath (tirumañjanam) to Śokkapperumāḷ and his consorts in the temple at
Nirandanūr. The special mention of the bath probably signifies a purificatory
reconsecration of the deities.

This is a Telugu inscription dated Śaka 1[5]41, Siddhārthi, Śrāvaṇa, śu. 12,
Wednesday corresponding to A.D. 1619, August 11. It was Nija-Śrāvaṇa month.
The record states that a certain Chennammagāru a lady of the royal household of
Tiruveṅgaḷanātharāja, with a view to secure śāśvata-parama-pada without any more
birth in the mundane world, offered endless worship to god Saumyanāthasvāmi at
Nelandalūru otherwise known as Nirantarapura, and made a number of gifts to the
deity such as a golden crown, conch and discus, golden plates for the hands and feet,
a golden kalaśa over the vimāna and made provision for feeding the Śrīvaishṇavas
and the Smārtas in their respective feeding houses and also the Paramahamsas and
Jōgi-jaṅgas.

inscription.head:
No. 590.
(A.R. No. 590 of 1907.)
Nandalūru, Rajampet Taluk, Cuddapah District
Saumyanātha temple—On the north wall
of the tiruchchuṟṟumāḷigai
Jaṭ. Sundara-Pāṇḍya. Year [15]
inscription.p:
The date is given as Vibhava (?), Kumbha, śu. 10, Monday, Punarvasu (=A.D.
1290, February 20). The cyclic year was Virōdhi.

This seems to record a gift of money with the interest on which offerings and
worship were to be conducted in the temple on a specific day of the year on the
occasion of a yāga in honour of god Vishvaksēna by the [Darma]talattār with whom
the amount was deposited by the donor one [Ār]yaputra, grandson of Kiḍāmbi
Rāmadāsa.

l.ta: peṟṟa puṉarpūcattu nāḷ nirantaṉūrakarattu kiṭāmpi rāma

ttut tirupperumānār tirunāmattāl nāṉ naṭattip
porukiṟa ciṟappukku amu-

yum itukku taku[nta] upakaraṇaṅkaḷu maṭaikkā
yamutu-

m cāttuppaṭiyum naṭappatāka poliyūṭṭāka ivaruṭaiya tirumuttirai
koṉai••

aḻivu conṉeṉ toḷar taru[ma]talattār kayyil kuṭutta varāhaṉ
paḻampaṇam

inscription.head:
No. 591.
(A.R. No. 591 of 1907.)
Nandalūru, Rajampet Taluk, Cuddapah District
Saumyanātha temple—on the same wall
Jaṭ. Sundara-Pāṇḍya. Year 10
inscription.p:
The cyclic year Vyaya given against the 10th regnal year of the king seems to
point to Jaṭāvarman Sundara-Pāṇḍya II whose accession is known to have taken place
in A.D. 1276. This records a mutual agreement between the Mahāsabhā of Niranda-
nūr and Ulagamuṇḍāṉ-Dāsar one of the Vaishṇava-vāriyam of the temple of Śokkap-
perumāḷ by which an amount of 200 paṇam deposited with the temple by Piḷḷai
Pallavarāyar for the formation of a flower-garden, was invested on 500 kuḻi of land
and made over to the Dāsar on condition that he was to supply flower garlands
regularly to the temple, himself enjoying the yield of fruits of the different kinds of
trees he could plant therein as muṟṟūṭṭu (?)

yum śrīkulottuṅka coḻaviṇṇakarem perumāṉ cokkapperumāṉ
koyil śrīvai-

ṭiya payir ceytu muṟṟūṭ ṭaṉupavippatāka viṭṭaṉa••••

inscription.head:
No. 592.
(A.R. No. 592 of 1907.)
Nandalūru, Rajampet Taluk, Cuddapah District
Saumyanātha temple—on the same wall
Jaṭ. Sundara-Pāndya (II). Year 10:
inscription.p:
This is also an inscription of the same king. The details of date here given
viz. Vyaya, Tulā, śu. 12, Monday, Śatabhishaj, would yield the equivalent A.D. 1286,
September 30, regnal year being 11. This registers a gift of two pieces of areca-nut
garden and house sites after purchase by the same donor mentioned in No. 591
above, whose full name is given as Eḍuttakaiyaḻagiyār Piḷḷai Pallavarāyaṉ of
Tuñjalūr in Tirumiḻalaikkūṟṟam a district in Pāṇḍimaṇḍalam, to provide for offerings,
worship and lamps to the image of Emberumāṉār set up in the temple of Śokkap-
perumāḷ at Nirandanūr by one Ānaimēlaḻagiyār of Vīraśikhāmukhachchēri Kaḷattūr
a suburb of Vīranārayaṇa-chaturvēdimaṅgalam in Śōḻamaṇḍalam and also for the
maintenance of a maṭha presided over by this teacher (?)

l.ta:
yum tiṅkaṭkiḻamai peṟṟa catayattunāḷ pāṇṭimaṇṭalattu
tirumiḻalaikkūṟṟattu tuñcalūruṭaiyār eṭuttakaiyyaḻakiyarāṉa piḷḷai

inscription.head:
No. 593.
(A.R. No. 593 of 1907.)
Nandalūru, Rajampet Taluk, Cuddapah district
Saumyanātha temple—In the same place
Jaṭ. Sundara-Pāndya. Year 10
inscription.p:
This registers a transaction similar and in addition to the one recorded
in No. 591 above. The same donor Piḷḷai Pallavarāyar is said to have given 200
paṇam to Śrīvaishṇavadāsar Ulagamuṇḍāṉ and to Dāsaṉ Kēśavapperumāḷpiḷḷai
Śokkaṉ on behalf of the temple, for the formation of a flower garden called Rāmā-
nujaṉ-tirunandavanam and these latter shared 400 kuḻi of tiruviḍaiyāṭṭam between
them for cultivating the land and enjoying the yield as muṟṟūṭṭu after supplying flower
garlands to the temple out of flower plants grown all round the hedges. Another
condition was that the different fruit trees, plantains and vegetables (kīḷpachchai?)
belonged to the temple.

l.ta: ṭṭaiyil kuḻi nūṟṟaimpatumāka nilam kuḻi nāṉūṟum ikko-

r pātiyum ic cokka nuḷḷiṭṭār pātiyumāka
payiṟ

ce[y*]tu muṟṟūṭṭu manupavikka kaṭavarkaḷākavum tirunaṉtavanam [veli
ilāta]-
paṭi tiruppūmaraṅkaḷ niṟain[ta*]ṉa ākki tirumālaikaḷum eṭuttu tevai-

ṭa nilattu[p puṉcey] kiḻakkaṭaiya viṭṭa kuḻi nūṟum āka kuḻi añ-

inscription.head:
No. 594.
(A.R. No. 594 of 1907.)
Nandalūru, Rajampet Taluk, Cuddapah district
Saumyanātha temple—On the south wall
of the same Tiruchchuṟṟumāḷigai
Jaṭ. Sundara-Pāṇḍya (II). Year 17
inscription.p:
The details of the date are given as Nandana Mīna, śu. 5. Saturday, Rōhiṇī,
which would correspond to A.D. 1293, March 14.

This records the formation of a garden called Māṉaparipālan tiruttōppu at
Nirandaṉūr in the name of minister Toṇḍaimāṉār of Tirunelvēli and an endowment
of land as jīvita for the maintenance of the persons looking after it, for the
benefit of god Śokkapperumāḷ at Nirandanūr, by Karumāṇikka-Āḻvāṉ alias
Nandivarman the headman of Śiṟumali residing at Toḻūr in Toḻūr-nāḍu a division
of Maṇavir-kōṭṭam in Jayangoṇḍaśōḻa-maṇḍalam. These dāsa-nambis of the garden
were to supply flower garlands to the temple daily in addition to 75 palam of
vegetables grown on a plot specially set apart for the purpose. For the 20 paṇam
received by the temple from these persons, two nāḻi of offered rice was to be given to
them every day.

l.gr:
sacivō jīyā dudyānam yasya śāsanāt [|*] śrīmat
sundarēśasya nirmitannandivarmaṇā || undyānamakarōdaccam nandivarmā mahāyaśā
mānapālana mantriśaśśāsanāa sundarēśituḥ [||*]

dēvaṟku yāṇṭu 17- āvatāna nandaṉasamvaasarattu minanāyaṟṟu pūṟuva
pakṣattu pañcamiyum canikkiḻamaiyum peṟṟa rōhiṇi

inscription.head:
No. 595.
(A.R. No. 595 of 1907.)
Nandalūru, Rajampet Taluk, Cuddapah district
Saumyanātha temple—on the inner
eastern gōpura, right side
inscription.p:
This is an incomplete inscription beginning with a Sanskrit verse which states
that this is an edict of Gaṅga-bhūpa. In the Tamil portion that follows mention is
made of this chief under the name Gaṇḍapeṇḍāra Gaṅgayya-Sāhiṇi and of another
person Sāraṅga-Nāyaka. Gaṅgaya Sāhiṇi was a subordinate under Kākatīya king
Gaṇapati and was ‘ruling the whole country from Pānuṅgallu to Mārjavāḍa’ (No. 306
of 1931). The inscription is dated in Sādhāraṇa, Karkaṭaka, śu. 12, Monday,
Jyēshṭhā which should correspond to A.D. 1250.

inscription.head:
No. 596.
(A.R. No. 596 of 1907.)
Nandalūru, Rajampet Taluk, Cuddapah district
Saumyanātha temple—In the same place
Rājarāja III. Year 23 and 24
inscription.p:
This records a gift of 6 māḍai and a lampstand each for two perpetual lamps
in the temple of Śokkapperumāḷ by a merchant named Śemmāseṭṭi of Muraṇōtta-
maṅgalam in Vaḷḷuva-nāḍu a division of Malai-maṇḍalam, and his wife Uṇṇippiḷḷai
Akkaṉ, one made in Hēviḷambi, Kumbha, ba. 6, Saturday, Svāti, and the other a year
later in Vikāri, Tulā ba. 3, Monday, Mṛigaśīrsha corresponding respectively to A.D.
1238, February 6, f.d.t. ·05, and to A.D. 1239, October 17, f.d.n. ·11.

l.ta: ka iṭṭa māṭai āṟu tirukkuttu viḷa-

inscription.head:
No. 597.
(A.R. No. 597 of 1907.)
Nandalūru, Rajampet Taluk, Cuddapah district
Saumyanātha temple—In the same place
inscription.p:
This is dated in Śaka 1193, Prajāpati (1271-72 A.D.) and records a gift of 32
cows for burning a perpetual lamp in the temple by Perumāṉaḍi Dēvarasa younger
brother of Prasādi Tikkarasa a son of Jagadobbagaṇḍa Kāmarasa. It is not clear
whether this Kāmarasa was the same as the chief Jag. Kāmayadēva mentioned in
No. 206 of 1894 from Kāvali in the Nellore district.

inscription.head:
No. 598.
(A.R. No. 598 of 1907.)
Nandalūru, Rajampet Taluk, Cuddapah district
Saumyanātha temple—In the same place, left side
inscription.p:
This is dated in Śaka 1172, Saumya, Rishabha, ba. 15, Friday, Rōhiṇi corres-
ponding to A.D. 1249, May 14, Śaka year being current on which day there is stated
to have been a solar eclipse. It records a gift of all the tolls including the ‘magaṇmai’
dues leviable at Nirandanūr, for the expenses of the several festivals in the temple of
Śokkapperumāḷ, by one Perumāḷ-Piḷḷai the headman of Kaliyūr and a toll officer, to
secure the well-being of Madurāntaka Pottappichchōḻa Gaṇḍagōpālar alias Manma-
siddharasa. The record is incomplete.

edition.l: svastiśrī [||*] cakarai yāṇṭu 1172-

inscription.head:
No. 599.
(A.R. No. 599 of 1907.)
Nandalūru, Rajampet Taluk, Cuddapah District
Saumyanātha temple—In the same place
inscription.p:
This is a badly damaged inscription. It is dated in Śaka 1194, Āṅgirasa, Mārgaḻi
(1272-73 A.D.) and seems to record a grant of 15 kuḻi of land to god Śokkapperumāḷ
for some offerings. Mention is made of an illegal occupation of a land after removing
the boundary stone and of one Tiruvanantāḻvāṉ-Bhaṭṭaṉ who was apparently the
aggrieved person but was able to produce documents proving his ownership rights.
The details are not clear.

l.ta:
tiruvaṉantāḻvān paṭṭan muṉṉāḷ tantoṭṭattile vaiykka••••ñ
[ci*]yile tiruvā-

inscription.head:
No. 600.
(A.R. No. 600 of 1907.)
Nandalūr, Rajampet Taluk, Cuddapah District
Saumyanātha temple—On the base of the
same gōpura, left of entrance
Kulōttuṅga I. Year 27: 1096-97 A.D.
inscription.p:
The inscription has the introduction Pugaḻmādu viḷaṅga etc. It is incomplete.
It seems to record the grant of some land as taṭṭārkāṇi at Nirandalūr to a goldsmith
whose name is not clear.

l.ta:
pukaḻmātu viḷaṅka cayamātu virumpa nilamakaḷ nilava malarmakaḷ
puṇara urimaiyiṟ ciṟanta maṇimuṭi

cūṭi miṉavar nilaikeṭa villavar kulaitara eṉaimaṉṉavar etirmalain
toṭat tikkaṉaittu

lottuṅka coḻatevaṟku [yāṇṭu] 37- ḻāvatu atirājentramaṇṭala
ttu meṟpākkai nāṭṭu [nira]-

l.ta:
ṭṭāṉukku yāṇṭu irupatteṭṭāvatu mutal
pācūr••••• ivaṉukkuk kāṇi

inscription.head:
No. 601.
(A.R. No. 601 of 1907.)
Nandalūru, Rajampet Taluk, Cuddapah District
Saumyanātha temple—On the base of the inner
eastern gōpura, south side
Kulōttuṅga III. Year 24: 1202 A.D.
inscription.p:
The cyclic year Dundubhi corresponding with the 24th regnal year makes this
a record of Kulōttuṅga III. The other details of date are Rishabha, śu. 3, Friday,
Mṛigaśīrsha (A.D. 1202, April 26).

This records a gift of ten Bhujabala-māḍai for two perpetual lamps in the
temple of Śokkapperumāḷ by Nūkkamadēvi the wife of the chief Madhurāntaka
Pottappichchōḻa Nallasiddharasar.

inscription.head:
No. 602.
(A.R. No. 602 of 1907.)
Nandalūru, Rajampet Taluk, Cuddapah District
Saumyanātha temple—On the base of the same
gōpura, right of entrance
Kulōttuṅga III. Year 36: 1213-14 A.D.
inscription.p:
The cyclic year Śrīmukha corresponding to the 36th regnal year makes the
inscription assignable to Kulōttuṅga III. The record is incomplete and registers
some provision made by one Tirumūla-Reḍḍiruvi a nephew of Madhurāntaka Dēśi-
Reḍḍi of Mēṟpākkai in Mēṟpākkai-nāḍu for the daily offering of tiruppōṉagam to god
Vēdanāyaka-Perumāḷ installed by him at Nirandanūr, in a shrine of the temple.

l.ta: kku nittam oru tiruppoṉakam oru tūm pariciyāl

inscription.head:
No. 603.
(A.R. No. 603 of 1907.)
Nandalūru, Rajampet Taluk, Cuddapah District
Saumyanātha temple—on the east wall
of the second prākāra.
inscription.p:
This is dated in Śaka 124[5], Rudhirōdgāri Tai 15 (A.D. 1323-24). It is
damaged. It seems to record the title deed granted to one Perumāḷ-Bhaṭṭar in place
of the one that had been lost reinstating him as the upātti (priest ?) of the Śokkap-
perumāḷ temple after recovering from him some amount as damages caused by his
leaving his post. The mahā-sabhā of the place, the Vaishṇava-vāriyam of the temple
and one Ulagamuṇḍāṉ-Dāsaṉ are said to have set up the enquiry into this transaction
and decided the case.

edition.l: ttu taiy mātam 15- n tiyati nirantanūr akarattu cokka-

l.ta: lum cempilum perumāḷ paṭṭaṟkku nāṅkaḷ vicu••••
koyilil upāttu vaikka ivaṉ oṭi[p*] pokaiyāl•••••

inscription.head:
No. 604.
(A.R. No. 604 of 1907.)
Nandalūru, Rajampet Taluk, Cuddapah District
Saumyanātha temple—on the same wall
inscription.p:
The Śaka year is lost, the other details of date being given as Vibhava, Makara,
ba. 3, Tuesday, Uttiram. It records a gift of gold for waving camphor light before
the god daily during worship and 45 cows for burning perpetual lamps by one
Īñjuḻāṉ Malli Pūvaṟkaṉ Śīyappēraiyaṉ, a merchant of Vallūr.

Saumyanātha temple—on a slab lying in the flower garden
Sadāśivarāya Śaka 1490

inscription.head:
No. 610.
(A.R. No. 610 of 1907.)
Nandalūru, Rajampet Taluk, Cuddapah District
Saumyanātha temple—on the outer gōpura, right of entrance
inscription.p:
This is dated in Śaka 1186, Raktākshi, Makara, ba. 15, Sunday, Śravaṇa,
ardhōdaya (A.D. 1265, January 18).

This records the gift of the magamai (duty on articles of merchandise) from
Pottappināḍu which he had been long levying with the sanction of the Nāṭṭavar,
Śeṭṭis and others, to the temple of Śokkapperumāḷ, by the chief Nāgarasa the pradhāni
of Gaṇḍapeṇḍāra Jannigadēva for tiruppōnagam offering daily with one tūmbu of rice
and with double that quantity on the days of Mārgaḻi and other specified days.
Provision was also made for the daily supply of three flower garlands to the temple
through one Dāsar Tirumaṅgaiyāḻvāṉ for a remuneration of the daily sustenance with
a nāḻi of offered rice and 2 māḍai in cash once every year.

Jannigadēva was a minister under the Kākatīya queen Rudrāmbā and was
administering the country between Pānuṅgallu and Mārjavāḍi in succession to
Gaṅgaya-Sāhiṇi (A.R. 1910, Part II, para 46).

inscription.head:
No. 611.
(A.R. No. 611 of 1907.)
Nandalūru, Rajampet Taluk, Cuddapah District
Saumyanātha temple—on the same gōpura, left of entrance
inscription.p:
This comprises two verses in Sanskrit one recording the construction of this
gōpura by Sōmēśvara son of Vimalāditya, and the other eulogising him as an
ornament of the family of Pottapichōḷa who “had subdued the whole earth by the
strength of his arms.” Vimalāditya who figures in Nos. 579 and 584 above as the
feudatory of Vikramachōḷa was the son of Siddharasa who has been surmised to be
the possible elder brother of Bēta I (A.R. for 1908, Part II, para 78).

citralatikā tāḍaṅga pu•••

vyāptavyōma digantakānti pimalādityāvanīśātmaja śśrīmaa gōpuramēta dūrjita
guṇa-

śrīmatpōttapicōḷa vamśatilaka ssōmiśvara śśāśvatam prōḍām vyūḍadayāgu
ṇaśśataguṇām puṣṇā-

inscription.p:
This is a short inscription in Telugu dated in the cyclic year Viḷambi, Āśvija,
śu. 5, Thursday. It mentions Rāmābhaṭṭa Ayyavāru and one Maddala Basavayya.
The former is evidently the same as the minister of Achyuta who figures in No. 607,
dated in Śaka 1456. The cyclic year Viḷambi would then correspond to Śaka 1460
(A.D. 1538-39).

ṁ komāraḍu padumārānnu ·ā·ubaśa pātraṁ maddala basavayya

inscription.head:
No. 613.
(A.R. No. 613 of 1907.)
Nandalūru, Rajampet Taluk, Cuddapah District
Saumyanātha temple—on a slab set up
in the same place
inscription.p:
This is a damaged inscription of Jaṭāvarman Sundara-Pāṇḍya I with the
Sanskrit introduction Samasta jagadādhāra etc. (acc. A.D. 1251). It seems to
register the gift of a village Maṇaṉūr (?) as sarvamānya (probably by the king) for
worship and repairs to the temple.

edition.p:
On the top row are engraved the figures respectively of Sun, Flywhisk, Lamp,
Flywhisk, and crescent.

The second row has the figures Lamp-stand, Pot on tripod, Flagstaff(?), Fish,
Pot on tripod.

l.gr: hē••• ḷa bhujāma•••••• purā śrāṇavaḥ apatu

inscription.head:
No. 614.
(A.R. No. 614 of 1907.)
Nandalūr, Rajampet Taluk, Cuddapah District
Saumyanātha temple—on another slab set up
in the same place
Jaṭ. Sundara-Pāṇḍya. (II). Year 9.
inscription.p:
The date of this inscription is given as Pārthiva, Puraṭṭādi. This cyclic year
corresponds to A.D. 1284-85 which happens to be the 9th regnal year of Jaṭ. Sundara-
Pāṇḍya II. This seems to register an endowment of land by the king after purchasing
it from the Mahājanas of the place (Nirandanūr) for the requirements of offerings to
god Śokkapperumāḷ on the 6th day of festivals, when the image was taken to the
Rāmānujan-tirunandavanam (flower-garden) the gift of Piḷḷai Pallavarāya for special
worship.

(A.R. No. 615 of 1907.)

Sāḷuva Immaḍi-Narasiṅga Śaka 1423

inscription.head:
No. 617.
(A.R. No. 617 of 1907.)
Guṇḍlūru, Rajampet Taluk, Cuddapah District
Lakshmīnārāyaṇa temple—on a slab set up
in the premises
Dēvarāya. Date lost.
inscription.p: Published in S.I.I., Vol. XVI, No. 34

inscription.head:
No. 618.
(A.R. No. 618 of 1907.)
Guṇḍlūru, Rajampet Taluk, Cuddapah District
Lakshmīnārāyaṇa temple—on another slab set up
in the same place
Ambadēva Śaka 1213 Khara
inscription.p:
The inscription is damaged. It is dated in Śaka 1213, Khara, Kanyā, ba. 8,
Monday, Punarvasu corresponding to A.D. 1291, September 17. It records a gift of
a land behind the temple and an areca-garden as sarvamānya to the temple of
Lakshmīnārāyaṇa-Perumāḷ at Guṇḍalūr in Mēṟpākkai-nāḍu of Adhirājēndramaṇḍalam,
by the chief (Ambadēva) for his own welfare and prosperity. This Ambadēva
belonged to a family of Kāyasthas of whom the first member Gaṅgaya-Sāhiṇi was a
cavalier in the service of Kākatīya Gaṇapati. He was succeeded in his office by his
sister's son Janniga-Janārdanadēva and next to him came Tripurāridēva (No. 229 of
1936) whose younger brother and the most powerful of the family was Ambadēva.
The troublous times that followed after the death of Gaṇapati and during the reign of
Rudrambā account for this chief's not acknowledging any overlord. No. 268 of 1905
from Tripurāntakam in Kurnool gives a long account of his exploits (A.R. for 1906,
Part II, para 44).

l.ta: ṭa nilaṅ kuḻi nānūṟum koyilil piṉ-

inscription.head:
No. 619.
(A.R. No. 619 of 1907.)
Guṇḍlūru, Rajampet Taluk, Cuddapah District
Agastyēśvara temple—on a slab set up in the premises
inscription.p:
Published in S.I.I., Vol. No.
(Miscellaneous Inscriptions-Telugu)

inscription.head:
No. 620.
(A.R. No. 620 of 1907.)
Guṇḍlūru, Rajampet Taluk, Cuddapah District
Agastyēśvara temple—on another slab set up
in the same place
Tribh. Rājarāja (III). Year 21: 1236-7 A.D.
inscription.p:
The cyclic year Durmukhi corresponding to the 21st regnal year of the king
makes the inscription one of Rājarāja III. The record states that a certain Īśāṉadēvar,
an āṇḍār looking after the flower garden of Tiruvagattīśvaram-uḍaiya-Nāyaṉār at
Guṇḍalūr in Pottappi-nāḍu which was a subdivision of Mēṟpākkai-nāḍu, died by
taking the alari plant poison probably under suspicious circumstances. On an order
received from Kuṉṟapiḷḷaiyār Gaṇḍagōpālaṉ, the Nāṭṭavar and the officers with the
Māhēśvaras of the temple made enquiries into the matter and punished the culprits
responsible for the mishap. The maṉṟāḍuvār (assembly) of Guṇḍalūr undertook to
burn a perpetual lamp in the temple for the merit of the deceased and the sons of the
latter were to pay a third of the expense.

edition.l: svasti śrī [||*] tiribhuvaṉaccakkarava-
yāṇṭu 21- vatu dunmuki saṅ-

inscription.head:
No. 621.
(A.R. No. 621 of 1907.)
Guṇḍlūru, Rajampet Taluk, Cuddapah District
Agastyēśvara temple—on the south wall of the central shrine
Ambadēva Śaka 1211
inscription.p:
This consists of a Tamil and a Sanskrit portion. The former gives the name
of the cyclic year only viz. Virōdhi, and records the gift by the chief of some taxes(?)
due to him, for burning three lamps and for rice offering in the temple of Agastīśvaram-
uḍaiya Nāyaṉār at Kuṇḍaiyūr. The latter gives the Śaka year 1211 in addition, and
gives other details of date as Makara-saṅkrama, Smara-tithi (Full-moon ?) and
Monday after eulogising his glory and exploits, of which prominent mention is made
of his victory over a certain Mallidēva-nṛipati.

edition.l: mānyamāka[vum] itdha[ rmattukku]

inscription.head:
No. 622.
(A.R. No. 622 of 1907.)
Guṇḍlūru, Rajampet Taluk, Cuddapah District
Agastyēśvara temple—on the same wall
Sōmidēva Śaka 1206: 1284-85 A.D.
inscription.p:
This is dated in Śaka 1206, Tāraṇa, Tai, Saṅkrama and records the gift of the
magamai duties on articles of merchandise levied at Guṇḍalūr and the śōtram(rent?)
from the dēvadāna, Piḍāri-paṟṟu, Bhairava-sthāna and Gaṇapati-sthāna lands at the
place, for burning perpetual lamps and for offerings and worship to the god in the
temple of Tiruvagastīśvaram-uḍaiya Nāyaṉar, for the merit of Śōmidēva-Mahārāja.
The identity of this chief is not clear.

inscription.head:
No. 623.
(A.R. No. 623 of 1907.)
Guṇḍlūru, Rajampet Taluk, Cuddapah District
Agastyēśvara temple—on the same wall
Ambadēva Śaka 12[19]; 1297-98 A.D.
inscription.p:
The Śaka year is doubtful as also the cyclic year. The latest known date of
this chief is Śaka 1215 (No. 207 of 1936). The inscription records a gift of money
received by way of fines by impoundage of cattle grazing on cornfields, as well as fees
on rush plant collected from channel at Guṇḍalūr, for burning a lamp in the
temple during the morning service of god Tiruvagattīśvaram-uḍaiyār.

edition.l: tam [ 20 tiyati] śrīmat tirume•••
